When ECS needs a new add-on or software, the first place I’m going to look is the Momentive ecosystem to support NetForum, our system of record.
Senior Director of Engagement, The Electrochemical Society

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"2,000";s:16:"data_description";s:96:"Meeting attendees added using NetForum + Momentive Event Management with no additional wait time";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"27k";s:16:"data_description";s:92:"Additional non-dues revenue from YM Careers while automated marketing emails do all the work";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"43%";s:16:"data_description";s:64:"Growth in revenue in exhibits and sponsorships in just two years";}}
Why organizations choose Momentive Software’s connected solutions to amplify their impact

ECS’s lean team turned challenge into opportunity by building a seamlessly integrated software ecosystem: harnessing scalable data management, driving non‑dues revenue, streamlining event check‑in, and enriching learning experiences.

The ECS membership department  is a small team and needed to get creative, so they leveraged NetForum’s knowledge base and the NetForum Users Group to set up efficient processes. They also needed scalability to accommodate its growth, so they moved to NetForum’s cloud option hosted on Microsoft Azure. 

ECS needed additional funding to support programs for members, so they turned to YM Careers job board to drive non-dues revenue. 

Long lines at event registration caused frustration for staff and attendees, so ECS added Momentive Event Management to streamline its registration process. In considering learning management software to enhance the member experience, it only made sense for ECS to choose Crowd Wisdom LMS. 

Crucial to advancing ECS’ mission is its software ecosystem, ECS leverages Momentive solutions, choosing to integrate technology to help meet and exceed its goals. 

Being heavily involved in NetForum’s user group gives ECS support and knowledge. Through integrating NetForum with Momentive Event Management’s Badge[on]Demand, ECS now has automated processes coupled with dedicated support to make the registration process easier and pleasant for attendees. 

Additionally, ECS uses YM Careers job board not only to bring in non-dues revenue, but as a conversation starter, making it easy to tell organizations how partnering with ECS will grant them access to their community.

Now ECS has its software ecosystem figured out. ECS uses NetForum as the source of truth for data, easily integrating with other Momentive Software products. 

The organization successfully accommodated a huge increase in meeting attendees with Momentive Event Management’s Badge[on]Demand integration. They processed over 5000 attendees with no wait times during their biggest event to date, an increase from 3,000 attendees. 

ECS easily boosted non-dues revenue with YM Careers to support member programs, which requires little staff effort, just monitoring. Pleased with its Momentive solutions, ECS onboard Momentive Software’s LMS to improve the staff and constituent experience by tracking learner data and improving educational offerings.

ECS, a nonprofit professional society established in 1902, advances theory and practice at the forefront of electrochemistry and solid state science and technology, and allied subjects. Their robust global membership researches innovative solutions to major challenges. ECS hosts prestigious meetings, publishes research, fosters education, and collaborates with other organizations.
How Electrochemical Society unified systems with Momentive

item.title: American Therapeutic Recreation Association (ATRA)
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Read our case study to discover why the American Therapeutic Recreation Association (ATRA) chose Momentive Software and Path LMS.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Path LMS has proven to be a critical tool to create new revenue, keep members informed and engaged, and streamline the delivery of effective education.
Path LMS has proven to be a critical tool to create new revenue, keep members informed and engaged, and streamline the delivery of effective education.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"$500k";s:16:"data_description";s:44:"in non-dues revenues earned through Path LMS";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"300";s:16:"data_description";s:26:"courses hosted on Path LMS";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"10,000+";s:16:"data_description";s:61:"therapists supported in quinquennial 50-hour CEU requirements";}}
Empowering therapists and growing revenue: ATRA’s Path LMS success

As the only national membership organization for recreational therapists, ATRA connects with more than 10,000 recreational therapists as both association members or recipients of ATRA services and resources.

Before partnering with Path LMS, ATRA struggled with a disjointed and manual process for delivering online education; webinars were stored on YouTube, CEU quizzes were hand-scored by a third party, and transcript retrieval was time-consuming and costly. To streamline operations and expand access to over 10,000 Certified Therapeutic Recreation Specialists (CTRSs), ATRA implemented Path LMS, gaining a flexible, integrated platform to modernize and scale their educational offerings.

Since implementing Path LMS, ATRA has significantly increased member engagement, streamlined CEU management, and made it easier for therapists to meet national recertification requirements. Their subscription model, which is available to both members and non-members, grants access to a growing library of learning modules and has generated over $500K in non-dues revenue in 2024.

Beyond monetization, ATRA leverages Path LMS to power a dynamic learning portal that integrates in-person and online professional development, on-demand content, surveys, certificates, a quarterly book club, and a newsletter, which is all carefully curated and designed to support ongoing learning and connection.

By combining an innovative mindset with the flexibility of Path LMS, ATRA has created a robust educational ecosystem that positions them as a leader in lifelong learning for therapeutic recreation professionals.

The American Therapeutic Recreation Association (ATRA) is a nonprofit organization that represents the needs and interests of recreational therapists. This specialized field of healthcare providers plan, direct, deliver, and evaluate recreation-based interventions for individuals with illnesses or disabling conditions.
American Therapeutic Recreation Assoc: LMS Case Study
Why ATRA chose Momentive Software and Path LMS.
ATRA Enhances Member Education with Path LMS

Education and Programs Coordinator at USCAP

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"3x";s:16:"data_description";s:46:"revenue generated over the span of seven years";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"153%";s:16:"data_description";s:26:"increased users since 2019";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:6:"8,500+";s:16:"data_description";s:14:"members served";}}
USCAP expands CME offerings with agility afforded by Path LMS
USCAP produces a variety of educational resources for its members, including live and on-demand courses as well as two highly regarded scientific journals.

When USCAP set out to expand its online education offerings, it faced limitations with its existing systems. The organization needed a scalable, centralized platform to support a growing library of enduring content from interactive courses to annual meeting sessions, and to simplify the complex process of reporting CME credits to ACCME.

Partnering with Path LMS allowed USCAP to streamline administration, improve the learner experience, and offer a more dynamic, accessible educational hub for pathologists at every stage of their careers.

Since implementing Path LMS, USCAP has seen remarkable results. The organization grew its user base by 153% and tripled revenue from educational offerings over seven years, transforming Path LMS into one of its top revenue generators.

The platform’s intuitive design and flexibility empowered USCAP to expand its eLearning library with engaging, regularly updated content, while the integration with ACCME’s PARS system eliminated time-consuming manual credit reporting.

These improvements have enhanced the learner experience, boosted staff efficiency, and reinforced USCAP’s position as a leader in continuing medical education for its 8,500 members -- and counting!
United States and Canadian Academy of Pathology

The United States and Canadian Academy of Pathology (USCAP) is the largest North American organization for pathologists, with over 8,500 members ranging from pathologists-in-training to practicing pathologists.

USCAP is dedicated to providing these medical professionals with education that reinforces and updates learners’ knowledge of pathology and understanding of pathologic processes, as well as knowledge of current practices in pathology quality and patient safety.

A top continuing medical education (CME) provider, USCAP has been awarded Accreditation with Commendation for 6 years by the Accreditation Council for Continuing Medical Education (ACCME®). Additionally, they offer Lifelong Learning credit in the American Board of Pathology’s (ABPath) Continuing Certification program.

Director of Learning, Knowledge, and Assessment, ACOFP

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"100+";s:16:"data_description";s:45:"hours of new training content captured a year";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"250";s:16:"data_description";s:59:"monthly courses reported for physician certification boards";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"25,000+";s:16:"data_description";s:7:"members";}}
From in-person to on-demand: ACOFP’s digital learning evolution via Path LMS
Using Path LMS, ACOFP was able to master better practices and use more video to provide CME to their members throughout the pandemic.

When the COVID-19 pandemic halted in-person meetings, ACOFP needed a way to continue offering CME. Osteopathy is very hands-on, involving physical stretching and manipulation of the patient. Particularly for the more complex procedures, ACOFP had not created or offered online courses before.

Using Path LMS, ACOFP was able to master better practices and use more video to provide CME to their members throughout the pandemic. ACOFP shifted their focus to center all their learning content on Path LMS, recording all presentations and panels for members. Doing so helped them begin to leverage video in other ways; they started pre-recording lectures that did not require interaction and sent film crews to subject matter experts to record sophisticated video training of the more complex osteopathic techniques.

By fully leveraging Path LMS, ACOFP saw increased engagement and found video-based learning to be the most effective format for complex clinical training. Their streamlined, intuitive platform now supports a growing video library, easy user access with single sign-on, and minimal need for tech support.

With a collection of 150 videos and quizzes (and growing), ACOFP will be giving program directors additional control and visibility to track learners’ progress via the Path LMS Teams feature. ACOFP continues to expand their offerings by creating foundational courses for students and residents, enabling progress tracking, and even collaborating with other organizations on joint programs, like a shared diabetes course with the Endocrine Society
American College of Osteopathic Family Physicians

The American College of Osteopathic Family Physicians (ACOFP) is a professional medical association that represents more than 25,000 practicing osteopathic family physicians, residents, and students throughout the United States.
ACOFP LMS Success Story: Continuing Education Results
Why ACOFP chose Path LMS for scalable member education.

item.title: Veterinary Emergency and Critical Care Society (VECCS): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Momentive Software hosts its International Symposium (IVECCS) every year, and many of the world’s most progressive, forward-thinking clinical veterinarians, technicians, and practice managers attend to learn about the latest advances in equipment, instruments, pharmaceuticals, and information management tools. Momentive Software was looking to create a robust event app that would make vast amounts of data easily accessible to its attendees.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Momentive Software hosts its International Symposium (IVECCS) every year, and many of the world’s most progressive, forward-thinking clinical veterinarians, technicians, and practice managers attend to learn about the latest advances in equipment, instruments, pharmaceuticals, and information management tools. Momentive Software was looking to create a robust event app that would make vast amounts of data easily accessible to its attendees.
How VECCS Uses Momentive Event Management to Streamline Event Information Accessibility

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:186:"Momentive’s Event Management platform and easy integration with Expo Tracker avoided the dreaded double data entry that takes up countless hours for an already busy organization staff.";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:128:"Momentive’s integration with Expo Tracker allowed attendees to have the most up-to-date event information via their event app.";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:139:"Momentive made it easy for attendees to stay informed about all the important information & up-to-date happenings at the IVECCS event. ";}}

VECCS hosts its International Symposium (IVECCS) every year, and many of the world’s most progressive, forward-thinking clinical veterinarians, technicians, and practice managers attend to learn about the latest advances in equipment, instruments, pharmaceuticals, and information management tools. VECCS was looking to create a robust event app that would make vast amounts of data easily accessible to its attendees.

Because this is a large educational event, there is a lot of information presented and available to the attendees, and VECCS wanted a way to make that information as accessible as possible via their mobile event app. VECCS already had a robust event management system in place with Expo Tracker, so the goal was to easily pull the information from the Expo Tracker system into the mobile app so attendees can have all the same information across platforms as well as to make it easy for VECCS only to have to add data in one place. 

Momentive helped VECCS achieve this goal by creating a mobile event app that housed all its important information, from the complete event schedule to exhibit hall information with the complete exhibitors list, as well as their individual session surveys & overall event survey. 

Momentive also worked with Expo Tracker to build web services to pull in the comprehensive event schedule, the complete list of the speakers, including their full biographies, as well as the list of the exhibitors, along with their company & contact information from the Expo Tracker system.

Expo Tracker is a full-featured management system that houses a multitude of VECCS’ information for the IVECCS event, such as all of their participating exhibitors and exhibit hall information, their full program schedule, and their complete speaker directory of all individuals speaking at IVECCS. Momentive’s integration with Expo Tracker enables the program schedule, speaker list, and exhibitor list to come together efficiently, so that when updates are made in the Expo Tracker database, they are automatically carried over into the IVECCS app. The benefit was threefold: 1) We eradicated the need for manual entries in two places. 2) We allowed attendees to have the most up-to-date event-related information via the mobile app. 3) We made it easy for them to know all the important information & happenings at the IVECCS event.
PMK Integration with Expo Tracker to Avoid Double Data Entry; A Seamless Attendee Experience.

About Established in 1969, the membership represents a variety of areas of veterinary medicine, including private general practice, specialty referral practice, emergency practice, and teaching institutions. Membership is worldwide, with representation from nearly 60 countries located on every continent. Their mission is inspiring excellence in emergency and critical care for animals by cultivating an inclusive community and empowering one another to thrive.

item.title: The American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages (ACTFL): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages (ACTFL) sought to develop a robust event app for its annual conference, enabling its members to engage seamlessly while avoiding duplicate manual entry work by ACTFL staff.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: ACTFL Event App Case Study: Conference Tech Results
ACTFL developed a robust event app for their annual conference to streamline engagement and reduce staff manual work.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"54%";s:16:"data_description";s:39:"of the ACTFL members downloaded the app";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:13:">1 million";s:16:"data_description";s:84:"in-app clicks across all features demonstrated extensive engagement across the board";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"avoided";s:16:"data_description";s:59:"double data entry; a significant time-saver for ACTFL staff";}}

The American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages (ACTFL) sought to develop a robust event app for its annual conference, enabling its members to engage seamlessly while avoiding duplicate manual entry work by ACTFL staff.

ACTFL sought a solution to prevent duplicate data entry and streamline the process for its staff to populate their mobile app. Entering the event’s schedule and speakers into their registration system and then needing to re-enter them into a mobile app content management system is time-consuming and not the best use of their resources. 

In addition to entering the bulk of their data only once into the system, ACTFL wanted to ensure that the data was kept up to date in real-time and that there were no discrepancies between their registration system and the mobile event app. This was key to freeing up their staff’s time to focus on other important aspects of their event, as well as ensuring attendees always had the most accurate information. 

Momentive integrated the registration system and event app to pull in the schedule and speakers for ACTFL’s Annual Conference, and scheduled updates to come over every hour. Whenever an edit was made to a record in the registration system, it would automatically be reflected in the mobile app, without any additional work from ACTFL staff. Similarly, if a session were cancelled, it would automatically be removed from the app.

Overall, both ACTFL members and staff were thrilled with the app. The ability to save time by avoiding duplicate data entry was a huge time-saver. And most importantly, the engagement level, based on the usage of the mobile app, was high. Over half of the ACTFL members downloaded the app (54%), and more than one million in-app clicks across all features demonstrated extensive engagement across the board.
American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages (ACTFL)

The American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages (ACTFL) is dedicated to improving and expanding the teaching and learning of all languages at all levels of instruction. ACTFL is an individual membership organization of more than 12,500 language educators, students, and administrators. They host a large Annual Conference bringing together all these various members.
How ACTFL reduced staff workload with a seamless event app
ACTFL Engages Members with a Real-Time Event App

item.title: The United States and Canadian Academy of Pathology (USCAP): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: USCAP partnered with Momentive Event Management to create an app for its annual conference that would provide a high level of security for its data and also restrict app usage to ticket holders of certain events.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: An Easy Solution to a Complex Ticketing System

USCAP partnered with Momentive Event Management to create an app for its annual conference that would provide a high level of security for its data and also restrict app usage to ticket holders of certain events.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:167:"Our integration saved USCAP event staff time by keeping two systems up-to-date at once, as updates made in MemberMax were automatically carried over to the mobile app.";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:92:"The app’s “Schedule” feature was the most popular, holding the most app clicks at 60%.";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:117:"The app’s “My Itinerary” section, featuring the ticketed integration, accounted for close to 20% of app clicks.";}}

USCAP partnered with Momentive Event Management to create an app for its annual conference that would provide a high level of security for its data and also restrict app usage to ticket holders of certain events.

The United States and Canadian Academy of Pathology (USCAP) provides the crème de la crème in continuing medical education and translational research, aimed at improving practices and patient outcomes globally. While global health through education is a strategic initiative, the diverse educational activities provided by USCAP ensure personalized education that will influence personalized medicine. Modern educational delivery methods using digital assets prepare pathologists for next-generation learning and make it possible to reach colleagues working in low-resource countries. 

USCAP hosts an annual meeting with a complex schedule and speaker list, and needed their data to be secured with a login. A high level of security for its event information was essential. Aside from the sheer amount of data USCAP populated into its mobile app, they also had many sessions that required attendees to purchase a ticket in advance. USCAP needed to develop a mobile app that would integrate with MemberMax (their registration system) and create an automatic process to pull in their data and ensure only ticket holders were able to add specific sessions to their personal schedules. 

First, Momentive integrated with MemberMax to pull in the annual conference’s schedule and speaker list, as well as set updates to sync every hour. 

Upon downloading the mobile app, users were prompted to log in to access any information. Momentive authenticated the username and password combination entered by the user against MemberMax to ensure only authorized users could gain access. To handle the ticketed sessions, Momentive worked with MemberMax to implement a system that would only allow an attendee to add a ticketed session to their “My Itinerary” if they had already paid for it. The payment system that allowed an attendee to purchase a ticket resided in MemberMax and needed to go through them.

Momentive’s Event Management platform served up a complex amount of data for USCAP’s annual conference, while keeping it highly secure. Our platform also enabled USCAP to restrict event app usage to specific ticket holders.

Integrating with MemberMax to pull in the schedule and syncing updates every hour eliminated the need for USCAP staff members to format and upload the large amount of event data. It also saved them time keeping two systems up-to-date at once, since updates made in MemberMax were automatically carried over to the mobile app. 

As for their goal of limiting access to specific ticket holders, if a user attempted to add a session on the app that required a ticket, Momentive’s platform allowed USCAP to restrict event app usage to certain ticket holders. They received a pop-up with instructions on where to purchase a ticket at the event. This message was fed to the app via MemberMax. Once a user purchased a ticket, this information was sent to the app, and the session was added to their “My Itinerary” in real time.

Overall, both USCAP members and staff were thrilled with the app. The most popular features, holding the most clicks, were the two areas where Momentive did the most integration and special features. The “Schedule” and “My Itinerary” were the two most used sections of the app.
PMK Integration with Membermax; High Level of Security; Handling Complex Ticketing

The United States and Canadian Academy of Pathology (USCAP) is dedicated to creating a better pathologist. It accomplishes this through its mission to provide the ultimate in continuing medical education and translational research to improve practices and patient outcomes globally. While global health through education is a strategic initiative, the diverse educational activities provided by USCAP ensure personalized education that will influence personalized medicine. Modern educational delivery methods using digital assets prepare pathologists for next generation learning and make possible outreach to colleagues working in low-resource countries. As a member organization, USCAP is dedicated to providing solutions and creating members for life, including Ph.D. colleagues.

item.title: Offshore Technology Conference: Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: OTC ranks among the top 100 trade shows in the United States and is recognized as one of the 50 largest meetings in the country.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: “Exhibitors realize that most people carry around their phones. Their materials and information are easily accessible through the mobile app. This year we had a solid adoption rate that we feel confident will only increase as attendees and exhibitors continue to see its benefits.”

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:120:" Pre-event engagement was key to driving a record number of event app downloads for the Offshore Technology Conference. ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:91:"Even though attendance was comparable to the previous year, app downloads increased by 6%. ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:104:" Their solid adoption rate will only increase as attendees and exhibitors continue to see its benefits. ";}}
Pre-Conference App Promotion Leads to Record Number of Downloads

The Offshore Technology Conference is where energy professionals gather to exchange ideas and opinions, advancing scientific and technical knowledge for the safe, environmentally friendly, and sustainable development of offshore oil and gas resources. They were looking for a more strategic approach to promoting their conference app.

The OTC team promoted their app through various channels, including the website, email, videos, and social media. OTC always started their communication with where to find the app and how to download it, versus ending their talking points with this message. 

OTC included a video tutorial on how to download the app and search for ‘Offshore Technology Conference’ in the app stores. Daily downloads increased significantly during the show because of the promotion. 

The team also responded to past attendee surveys and increased first-time attendee engagement, which was also promoted through the app. They even debuted a first-timer lounge to help answer questions for new attendees, which included training people on how to use the app.

Pre-event engagement was key to driving a record number of event app downloads for the Offshore Technology Conference. Even though attendance was comparable to the previous year, app downloads increased by 6%. OTC had a solid adoption rate and felt confident that it would continue to grow as attendees and exhibitors saw its benefits.

The Offshore Technology Conference (OTC) is the world’s premier event where energy professionals gather to share ideas, drive innovation, and advance the technical and scientific knowledge needed to develop offshore resources responsibly. OTC ranks among the top 100 trade shows in the United States and is recognized as one of the 50 largest meetings in the country. The event is developed by 15 leading industry organizations and societies, with additional support from endorsing and collaborating organizations, ensuring a technical program that reflects the depth and diversity of the offshore energy sector.
Pre-Conference App Promotion Leads to Record Number of Downloads
OTC ranks among top US trade shows for industry engagement.
Record App Downloads Through Pre-Event Promotion

item.title: The Society of Tribologists and Lubrication Engineers (STLE): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: STLE wanted to communicate better with its audience, gain insight about users, and promote important aspects of the organization.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: A Fully Integrated App and a 97% Increase in Member Engagement

“It was important for us to simplify the app as much as possible for our users and our administrative team, and the integration with both iMIS and ScholarOne achieves this for us by eliminating double data entry. It saves valuable admin time.”

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"97%";s:16:"data_description";s:29:"increase in member engagement";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:8:"security";s:16:"data_description";s:97:"achieved by maintaining separate log-ins on the app to keep the “member only” content private";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"1";s:16:"data_description";s:74:"app for member and event information for year-round utilization and access";}}
A Fully Integrated App and a 97% Increase in Member Engagement

The Society of Tribologists and Lubrication Engineers’ (STLE’s) goal was to take the positive experiences they’d enjoyed during the four days of its Annual Conference and expand that to a more all-encompassing, year-round solution. Through that all-encompassing year-round solution, STLE wanted to communicate better with its audience, gain insight about users, and promote important aspects of the organization.

STLE wanted their app to bring value to their members while also incentivizing non-members to join. Having separate log-ins on the app for STLE members vs. individuals who are not members keeps the “member only” content private while allowing non-members to enjoy the majority of the features the app offers, intending to encourage them to join the organization. 

The next goal they had was to keep their members engaged all year. Momentive achieved this by putting all the essential information members would want or need to know throughout the year in the app, at their fingertips, such as: 

  • An Events Calendar that contains the different types of events STLE hosts, such as Section Meetings & Certification Exams, along with registration links to make it easy to sign up.
  • The complete STLE Member Directory is available to make it easy for members to connect with fellow members. 
  • A Hot Topics section that consists of industry-related advocacy topics and industry-related reports
  • Real-Time Alerts that can be sent to app users whenever new information has been added to the app. 

STLE has also taken advantage of Gamification features in its app to further broaden member interaction. The STLE Challenge Game connects the organization to its audience on an ongoing basis. With new games happening every week or two, STLE posts trivia-style questions for app users to test their Tribology knowledge. The winners are then announced via the in-app alerts (push notifications) so players easily know if they’ve won something, like an Amazon gift card. 

While STLE’s app contains all this new year-round information, the app also contains the event pages for its Annual Meeting and Tribology Frontiers Conference. Since members have the app on their device year-round, it’s easy for them to access the event apps when they are onsite at the events or in the weeks/days leading up to the event, building excitement about the upcoming conferences. It also eliminates the need for attendees to download additional apps onto their mobile devices.

STLE has simplified the overall data entry process for its staff by utilizing Momentive’s integration services. Their app integrates with iMIS, the host of STLE’s membership database, and ScholarOne, the host of its event agenda, presentations/papers, and authors. 

STLE continues to expand upon the offerings of their app which brings value to their members on an on-going basis. The positive impact of this is shown through the 97.7% increase in total engagement between the 1st and 2nd quarter of this year.
The Society of Tribologists and Lubrication Engineers (STLE)

The Society of Tribologists and Lubrication Engineers (STLE) is one of the largest professional societies in the field of tribology (the study of interacting moving surfaces), serving 15,000 industry professionals worldwide who comprise the tribology and lubrication business sector. STLE’s membership is highly engaged in the activities of the organization, and members are employed by the world’s leading companies and academic institutions.
How STLE improved communication and insights with Momentive
97% Member Engagement Increase with Momentive Event App

item.title: The Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy (AMCP): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Offers customized solution that streamlined access and provided unique information available to select groups of members.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"3x";s:16:"data_description";s:33:"event downloads over prior years ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:10:"networking";s:16:"data_description";s:116:" made easier with Momentive’s platform, allowing AMCP to target different member groups with targeted information";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:11:"streamlined";s:16:"data_description";s:106:"member access to critical information with Single Sign-On (SSO) offered Momentive’s customized solutions";}}
A Unique and Seamless Experience for AMCP Members

Momentive provided the Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy (AMCP) with a year-round mobile experience that would provide its members with the necessary information, while also offering a customized solution that streamlined access and provided unique information available to select groups of members.

One of AMCP’s goals was to streamline the process of logging into its app and website by allowing members to use the same credentials they use to access their account on the AMCP website. Having this type of single sign-on (SSO) is becoming increasingly popular and crucial to achieving a seamless experience across the multiple technologies an organization uses. 

AMCP wanted the app to include information about its members that went beyond the basic name and contact details typically provided by out-of-the-box solutions. They wanted to display information such as the member’s employer type, primary position, and committee. This industry-specific information would enable AMCP members to understand their fellow members better, allowing AMCP as an organization to target its messages and marketing efforts to specific groups of members. 

Momentive integrated with the database AMCP uses to house their member information for two purposes: 1) To authenticate access to the app, allowing members to use the same credentials as they use on AMCP’s website, and 2) To pull in members’ information, including custom, industry-specific fields to build a complete member directory. This achieved AMCP’s goals of a smooth login process, consistent with their other technologies. It also provided members with more targeted and distinct information about other members, making the member directory more useful and informative. Not only did this integration provide advantages for AMCP members, but it also had significant benefits for the organization and its staff. It gave AMCP the ability to control the information they pushed to certain members in the form of targeted push notifications and targeted “Hot Topics,” allowing them to display and push data based on what specific groups care about.

Momentive Event Management’s platform serves as a crucial networking tool for AMCP members, allowing AMCP to target different member groups with tailored information. 

Both AMCP members and staff were thrilled with the app. It met all of AMCP’s goals, providing streamlined access across platforms and segmented member communication, and AMCP tripled its event downloads over prior years.
The Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy (AMCP)

The Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy (AMCP) is the nation’s leading professional association dedicated to increasing patient access to affordable medicines, improving health outcomes, and ensuring the wise use of healthcare dollars. The Academy’s 8,000 pharmacists, physicians, nurses and other practitioners manage medication therapies for the 270 million Americans served by health plans, pharmacy benefit management firms, emerging care models and government. AMCP serves its members in various ways, including through national conferences, online learning programs, research published in peer-reviewed literature, and advocacy.
How custom solutions improved member access and exclusivity

item.title: National Shooting Sports Foundation (NSSF): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how NSSF and ConvExx increased revenue by 169% using Momentive’s GoExpo platform to streamline digital sponsorships for 1,734 exhibitors.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: NSSF/ConvExx SHOT Show Success | Momentive Event Management
“Using GoExpo increased revenue by 169% for the first year we used it and has steadily scaled since then.”

The Shooting Hunting and Outdoor Trade Show, owned by the National Shooting Sports Foundation and managed by ConvExx, is a well-known, critically acclaimed expo and conference that has been sold out for the past several years. 

This is a great problem to have but unfortunately running at capacity left only a few options for increased revenue generation potential. The SHOT Show is held at the Sands Convention Center, in Las Vegas, where there is no room to expand.

One of the options for revenue is the digital sponsorships through the GoExpo Exhibitor Dashboard. SHOT Show Management wanted to make the three digital mediums; event website, mobile app, and way finders, a one-stop-shop for their exhibitors. The exhibitors appreciated this; having one place to upload and manage everything (through their GoExpo Dashboard). 

To help promote the purchase, SHOT Show management presented the opportunities graphically through screenshots of the website and app. They wanted to give the exhibitors a visual of what to expect when they made the purchase.

This use of the GoExpo Exhibitor Management technology made it easy for the exhibitors to see the value of the enhancements, advertising and sponsorship opportunities as they purchased their booth space. The new sales flow increased revenue by 169% over the prior year. 

Innovative technology and achievement does not have to be anything fancy or earth shaking to be successful and innovative. In this case, designing a system/process to help better display available upgrades and putting everything in one place was simple but achieved great success for the SHOT Show.
National Shooting Sports Foundation/ConvExx SHOT Show

NSSF leads the way in advocating for the industry and its business and jobs, keeping guns out of the wrong hands, encouraging enjoyment of recreational shooting and hunting and helping people better understand the industry’s lawful products.
How NSSF boosted revenue 169% with GoExpo sponsorships

item.title: The Association of Performing Arts Presenters (APAP): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: APAP was seeking a way to seamlessly feature their schedule of events, speakers, and “Showcases” within an easy-to-use mobile app.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Boosting App Downloads by Highlighting Essential Event Information
APAP was seeking a way to seamlessly feature their schedule of events, speakers, and “Showcases” within an easy-to-use mobile app.
The Association of Performing Arts Presenters (APAP)

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"~50%";s:16:"data_description";s:144:"of all app clicks were on the Showcases icon on the home screen, confirming that it was a high-priority request that was successfully addressed.";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"~70%";s:16:"data_description";s:137:"of all app usage occurred in one of three modules (Schedule, Speakers, and Showcases), which integrated with Momentive’s app solution. ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:8:"critical";s:16:"data_description";s:94:"time saved by APAP staff due to not needing to upload the same data twice into two databases. ";}}
Boosting App Downloads by Highlighting Essential Event Information

The Association of Performing Arts Professionals (APAP) brings together performing arts professionals at its annual conference in New York City. APAP was seeking a way to seamlessly feature their schedule of events, speakers, and “Showcases” within an easy-to-use mobile app. Momentive provided an outstanding user experience that highlighted the information necessary to APAP, thereby boosting app downloads of that information, while also easily integrating APAP’s data from A2Z systems.

Attendees of the APAP conference can attend a host of different sessions running on various tracks. APAP’s goal was to find a mobile app tool that was easy to navigate and could handle the numerous sessions available. Momentive’s app solution was ideal due to its intuitive schedule and filter functionalities. Additionally, APAP was able to easily link each speaker to the session they were speaking at by utilizing the speaker mapping functionality, making it easy for attendees to research each speaker before the session. 

Another of APAP’s requests was to house the numerous Artist Showcases taking place during the conference in a separate module within the app. Showcases are various presentations that APAP coordinates in and around NYC, to which attendees have the option to attend or participate. Each Showcase is located at a different venue, and each venue is selected based on its relationship to the Showcase type. Showcases are an essential aspect of the APAP event app solution. By housing these special events in a separate icon on the main home screen of the app, the Showcases were highlighted and not overshadowed by the rest of the sessions located in the normal schedule. In addition, the information provided in the Showcases icon lets an attendee easily look up and map the address of the venue.

Furthermore, by pairing the Showcase location information with the independently curated City Content, an attendee could easily discover the best places to eat and visit around each Showcase venue. Highlighting APAP’s Showcases within its app ensured that these important parts of its event were top of mind to attendees. And they were, accounting for 50% of all app clicks.

Partnering with A2Z to achieve APAP’s goals was a quick and efficient process. Momentive set up an integration link between the A2Z and Momentive Event Management systems to enable the flow of Schedule, Speaker, and Showcases information from A2Z into the APAP mobile app. Because data was integrated from A2Z into the mobile app, the APAP staff did not need to upload the same data twice into two databases. Once attendees logged into the app, they could click on the schedule, speaker or Showcases icons to access the same information shown in the A2Z system. The importance of a seamless and easy integration process is highlighted by the fact that approximately 70% of all app usage was made in one of these three modules (Schedule, Speakers, and Showcases).

The Association of Performing Arts Presenters (APAP) is the nation’s leading professional association dedicated to the performing arts presenting field. APAP serves its members in many ways, including through leadership training and professional development programs, advocacy and an annual conference.
How APAP enhanced events with a mobile event app
Driving Event App Downloads with Essential Information

item.title: California Sharing Science Solutions (CASSS): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: CASSS provides its members a rich array of educational and networking opportunities through over a dozen symposia.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: CASSS and Momentive Team Up to Foster a “Culture of Engagement”
Momentive’s platform allows us to share current industry news, market our events, and serves as a crucial networking tool for our members.

Momentive’s platform allows us to share current industry news, market our events, and serves as a crucial networking tool for our members... Momentive worked seamlessly with YM (Your Membership) to upload attendees to the meeting where they can access the schedule, speaker bios, attendee list and more.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"~50%";s:16:"data_description";s:42:"of the CASSS membership downloaded the app";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"27%";s:16:"data_description";s:59:"of clicks made the Membership list the most popular feature";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:9:"furthered";s:16:"data_description";s:68:"ROI by offering sponsored banner ads and lead retrieval capabilities";}}

California Sharing Science Solutions (CASSS) provides its membership (membership includes scientists from more than 30 nations and nearly every continent) with a rich array of educational and networking opportunities through over a dozen symposia taking place throughout North America, Europe, Japan and South America. CASSS prides itself on encouraging its members to meet others and truly interact with people who understand what they do.

CASSS wanted to create an all-encompassing mobile app that would serve both their year-round communication needs, as well as provide full-fledged event apps for their members and attendees.

One of CASSS’s guiding principles is creating “a culture of engagement.” CASSS wanted a way to engage their members on a year-round basis in addition to giving them access to full-fledged event apps to utilize at their conferences throughout the year. CASSS was looking for a way to give their members easy, on-the-go access to industry information and enable them to more easily network than traditional email and websites allow. Hosting many events throughout the year, it was also important that CASSS provide robust event mobile apps for their members during their conferences. 

Two specific goals CASSS had when selecting an app partner were to: 1) provide attendees with a seamless login process so that they didn’t need to remember multiple sets of credentials, and 2) keep both their Membership list and Attendee lists automatically up-to-date, avoiding manual work by their staff. 

Momentive created a single app for CASSS that housed key member features to be used anytime, anywhere, including a Membership Directory with networking capabilities, Partner and Leadership Directories, an in-app Social Community, Publications, and more. This fulfilled the need for mobile access to crucial pieces of information that were previously only available on their website. Not only did Momentive provide a platform for year-round features, but the app also broke down to the event level, giving members full featured event pages, all within the same, single app.

The integration with Your Membership (YM) enabled the Membership and Attendee Lists, and member-to-member networking features to come together quickly and easily. When updates are made in the YM database, they are automatically carried over into the app, eliminating the need for double manual entries. To address CASSS’ second major concern and create a seamless login, members used the same credentials they used in the YM database for access to the app and each event within the app. This prevented any frustrations with needing to remember multiple sets of login credentials and increased the overall user-friendliness of the app.

Overall, both CASSS members and staff were thrilled with the app. The app enabled members to engage seamlessly and avoided duplicate manual entry work by CASSS staff. CASSS event exhibitors love that the app includes lead retrieval and the opportunity for sponsored banner ads. Nearly half of the CASSS members downloaded the app, and the Membership list was the most popular feature holding the most clicks at 26.8%.

California Sharing Science Solutions (CASSS) is a not-for-profit professional scientific society dedicated to addressing challenges in biopharmaceutical development and regulation. The CASSS membership includes process, analytical, regulatory and other related scientists in the field of biopharmaceutical development and regulation from over 30 nations and nearly every continent. CASSS provides its members a rich array of educational and networking opportunities through over a dozen symposia.
How CASSS supports scientific education and networking
CASSS Builds a Culture of Engagement with Momentive

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: NAPNAP, with 8,000+ members, supports pediatric-focused APRNs. Discover how the association empowers members and advances pediatric nursing today.
National Association of Pediatric Nurse Practitioners (NAPNP)

When we arrived on-site, we told our general services contractor that we would not be needing stanchions. Within 24 hours, he joined us all as true believers of the power of on demand badge printing technology.

They were so experienced in all aspects of the planning process. They kept everything on timeline and knew just how many check-in terminals and how much badge stock we would need. When we arrived on-site, we told our general services contractor that we would not be needing stanchions. Within 24 hours, he joined us all as true believers of the power of on demand badge printing technology.
With more than 8,000 members, the National Association of Pediatric Nurse Practitioners (NAPNP) is the professional association for all pediatric-focused advanced practice registered nurses (APRNs).

Two years after Momentive’s credit tracking and lead capture solutions were developed, Momenitve staff sat nearby while NAPNP managed their on-site registration desk with very labor-intensive processes and very long lines. When we approached them and said that we had a registration technology that could eliminate the long lines and labor-intensive processes, she had doubts that this was possible. Their registration experience was ther “breaking point”, and they knew they had to make a change. 

Having an established partnership with us, NAPNP requested our registration services for their next conference.

“There were never any lines! Participants found the kiosks easy to use. Our staff was freed up at the registration desk to help with questions and greet attendees. On demand badge printing made it easy to create tickets for events that required pre-registration, and it integrated so well with our association management system. We even had leaders from larger organizations in attendance that approached us to ask for your contact information. Thank you, Momentive Event Management Software, for bringing our event’s attendee experience to the next level”.

With 8,000 members, the National Association of Pediatric Nurse Practitioners (NAPNP) is the professional association for pediatric nurse practitioners (PNPs) and all pediatric-focused advanced practice registered nurses (APRNs). Established in 1973, we were the first national professional society for nurse practitioners and remain the only national organization dedicated to both advancing the APRN role and improving the quality of health care for infants, children and adolescents. Our esteemed members include national child health experts, respected authors, distinguished faculty and practicing clinicians who represent many facets of pediatric health care delivery.
How NAPNAP supports 8,000+ members with events and resources

item.title: Association of American Law Schools (AALS): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Each year, between 2,500 and 3,500 individuals attend the Association of American Law Schools (AALS) Annual Meeting and badges must be provided for each attendee.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: AALS Annual Meeting provides 2,500–3,500 badges each year for attendees.

[Momentive Event Management] has revolutionized our process and made the entire registration process better,” Erick says. “The ripple effects of a nice registration process can be felt throughout the meeting. People start off with a positive experience and are happier and more satisfied with the meeting overall.

[Momentive Event Management] has revolutionized our process and made the entire registration process better,” Erick says. “The ripple effects of a nice registration process can be felt throughout the meeting. People start off with a positive experience and are happier and more satisfied with the meeting overall.
Database and Registration Manager at the Association of American Law Schools (AALS)
Each year, between 2,500 and 3,500 individuals attend the Association of American Law Schools (AALS) Annual Meeting and badges must be provided for each attendee.

Prior to using Momentive Event Management’s badge[on]demand™ services, AALS would go through the painful process of pre-printing badges before each event and handing them out to attendees on-site. 

“Our staff was working late hours and every weekend printing badges and stuffing envelopes in the weeks leading up to the event. This caused considerable stress for the staff. We had to hire assistants to print badges and alleviate the staff. There was only one quality check for each badge and so many were printed incorrectly or placed in the wrong envelope,” says Erick Brown, Database and Registration Manager at AALS. 

Printing and organizing badges wasn’t the only problem AALS faced. 

That’s when AALS turned to Expo Logic to improve its processes. AALS employed Expo Logic’s badge[on]demand integration and ExpressPass™ event badge printing services. This new integration with AALS’ database allows badges to be printed on-site, automatically, as attendees check into the event. ExpressPass self-check-in stations are placed at the entrance to the meeting which allows attendees to serve themselves. Using the stations, attendees can either scan their registration confirmation QR code (received in email prior to the event) or select their name on the screen. After this step, their badges print immediately in just a few seconds.

The effects of this change were felt immediately at the last AALS Annual Meeting. Lines were eliminated and attendee satisfaction increased. 

“It has revolutionized our process and made the entire registration process better,” Erick says. “The ripple effects of a nice registration process can be felt throughout the meeting. People start off with a positive experience and are happier and more satisfied with the meeting overall.” 

“Plus, our staff is happy. There is no need to work weekends anymore and we’ve saved weeks of staff time and expense.”

The Association of American Law Schools (AALS) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit association of 174 member and 19 fee-paid law schools. Its member schools enroll most of the nation’s law students and produce the majority of the country’s lawyers and judges, as well as many of its lawmakers. 

Founded in 1900, the mission of AALS is to uphold and advance excellence in legal education. In support of this mission, AALS promotes the core values of excellence in teaching and scholarship, academic freedom, and diversity, including diversity of backgrounds and viewpoints, while seeking to improve the legal profession, to foster justice, and to serve its many communities–local, national, and international.
How AALS scaled badge printing and attendee management

The National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), is dedicated to the academic and professional success of African-American engineering students and professionals. NSBE offers its members leadership training, professional development activities, mentoring opportunities, career placement services and more.

NSBE needed a registration system that would integrate with their association management software IMPak Enterprise. Momentive Event Management’s badge[on]demand™ product was just the item they needed.

Badge[on]demand™ allowed NSBE to keep all registration information in their system while being able to print badges directly from each individual attendees record. Momentive Event Management’s ExpressPass™ check-in stations were used on site to allow for a quick and speedy check-in process.

A beacon of excellence, the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE) stands as one of the largest student-governed organizations based in the U.S. Founded in 1975, NSBE champions the dreams and ambitions of collegiate and pre-collegiate students, as well as technical professionals, in engineering and technology fields. Boasting more than 700 chapters and a membership exceeding 24,000 around the globe, NSBE advances a critical mission: “to increase the number of culturally responsible Black Engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.” 

With a legacy spanning 50 years, NSBE has birthed countless businesses, empowered corporate leaders, moved multitudes of engineers to graduation, and enriched STEM education for hundreds of thousands of K–12 students worldwide. In essence, NSBE members, chapters, and supporters are fervently committed to shaping a brighter future where Black excellence reigns supreme.
How NSBE drives success through events and engagement

item.title: American Association of Suicidology : Momentive Association Management Software Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: See how AAS used YourMembership to centralize operations, streamline training registration, and strengthen member connection.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: AAS Case Study | Centralized AMS Results
See how AAS used YourMembership to centralize operations, streamline training registration, and strengthen member connection.
There has never been anything I’ve wanted to do from a leadership perspective—trainings, newsletters, divisions, advocacy—that YourMembership couldn’t support.

AAS was looking for ways to enhance connection because that's how people learn and have a sense of belonging. Community and belonging are actually the foundations for suicide prevention. As we become more disconnected as a society, it's why we see increasing suicide rates amongst specific populations. This is why using YourMembership by Momentive to enhance connections is such a big part for AAS moving forward.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:6:"1200+ ";s:16:"data_description";s:20:"individual members ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"150+ ";s:16:"data_description";s:21:"member organizations ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"120+ ";s:16:"data_description";s:49:"AAS sponsored training and certification centers ";}}

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bx-laptop";s:13:"feature_title";s:43:"All-in-one association management platform ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:16:"box-bx-mail-send";s:13:"feature_title";s:83:"Integrated email capabilities to centralize communications and the user experience ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bx-group";s:13:"feature_title";s:50:"Building connections through the Online Community ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bx-sort-a-z";s:13:"feature_title";s:68:"Centralized tracking for member registration of training and events ";}}

The American Association of Suicidology (AAS), the nation's oldest suicide prevention entity, plays a pivotal role in advancing awareness, connection, and professional standards across the field. Under the leadership of CEO Jenna Baker, who has led four membership-based organizations, AAS has continued to evolve its approach to member engagement, training, and advocacy, while supporting a growing need for collaboration, community-building, and accessible learning experiences across the field.

When Baker joined AAS, the association was operating in a siloed tech environment—using separate tools for the website, training program registrations, email communications, and member engagement. This piecemeal approach created inefficiencies, increased administrative burden, and made it difficult to maintain clean and accurate data across platforms. Limited adoption of YourMembership’s full feature set left staff uncertain about its capabilities and dependent on additional products. Baker addressed these challenges by optimizing the platform, activating underused tools, and centralizing workflows—driving greater efficiency, clarity, and operational simplicity across the organization.
 

AAS quickly began expanding its use of YourMembership centralizing its operations by using the platform as a single hub for membership management, event and training registration, communications, and community—while leveraging built-in email tools and staff training to replace external systems, improve data accuracy, and maximize efficiency and adoption.

Through this strategic expansion of YourMembership’s capabilities, AAS is moving toward a more connected, centralized, and modernized member experience. Baker’s prior leadership roles demonstrated the tangible outcomes possible with YourMembership—including significant conference growth, improved advocacy responsiveness, and seamless data management. At AAS, YourMembership is now positioned to support enhanced community-building, streamlined operations, and long-term organizational scalability. 
 
Most crucially, YourMembership enables AAS to align technology with its mission, bringing on better for its members and industry: fostering connection, belonging, and professional excellence—key drivers in suicide prevention efforts nationwide.

The American Association of Suicidology (AAS) was founded in 1968 and is the nation’s largest and oldest suicide prevention membership organization. AAS works to support its members in their knowledge and best practices in the field.

item.title: Echo Horizon School: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Night and day difference between what we were able to do with the program, people's satisfaction with the program, the amount of money we raised. In my opinion, GiveSmart is the top of class when it comes to events — everything you need to do from there.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"2x ";s:16:"data_description";s:82:"all-time fundraising record broken at their first GiveSmart-powered Spring Affaire";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:9:"<1 day";s:16:"data_description";s:58:"reconciliation time, down from a month-long manual process";}}

Echo Horizon School is a Pre-K through 6th grade school in Culver City, Calif., serving approximately 200 students. About 15% are deaf or hard of hearing (DHH), and rather than separating them into pull-out programs, Echo Horizon includes them fully in mainstream classrooms, with a dedicated DHH teacher in each class for additional support.  

When Echo Horizon's development lead role was vacant, the Finance and Operations team stepped in to cover fundraising responsibilities, including taking ownership of the school's signature fundraiser, the Spring Affaire gala. That meant finding a platform that could support a volunteer-driven event at scale.

Echo Horizon had used another fundraising platform in prior years, but found the experience lacking. Reconciliation required printing out sheets and manually matching records by hand, dragging the process on for weeks after each event. A team of parent volunteers managed ticket sales, sponsorships, silent and live auctions, and a paddle raise, but the cumbersome back end created administrative friction.  

When Douglas Gardner, Director of Finance and Operations, stepped in to lead the event technology search, he already knew the answer: his wife had used GiveSmart for years, giving him firsthand insight into its capabilities. The school switched platforms, completed four hours of structured training, and entered their first Spring Affaire with GiveSmart ready. 

The difference was immediate. Volunteers who had been frustrated with data entry and item management found the new system far more intuitive. Support was responsive whenever questions came up, even on tight timelines. And the reporting tools that had once been a bottleneck became a non-issue.

At their first GiveSmart-powered Spring Affaire, Echo Horizon nearly doubled their all-time fundraising record. A significant portion of that growth came through the paddle raise, which supports faculty and staff professional development. The result was so strong that the school is now searching for additional ways to spend the restricted funds.  

What once took a month to reconcile through printed sheets and line-by-line manual matching, now takes virtually no time. The ability to download clean reports and run comparisons directly from the platform replaced an entirely manual workflow. For a volunteer-driven operation with no dedicated development staff, that efficiency was transformative.  

Echo Horizon plans to continue using GiveSmart for all Spring Affaire fundraising going forward, and their incoming development manager is being onboarded into a system that's already proven to work.

Echo Horizon School is a Pre-K through 6th grade independent school in Culver City, CA, built on a pioneering co-enrollment model where deaf and hard-of-hearing students learn alongside their hearing peers in fully inclusive classrooms. The school has spent over 40 years proving that inclusion benefits every child, building lifelong communication skills, fostering authentic respect for diversity, and creating a community where every learner feels seen, heard, and valued. Today, Echo Horizon serves approximately 200 students, guided by a mission to cultivate joyful, capable scholars and bold explorers who lead with kindness, integrity, and purpose.

item.title: The Independent Insurance Agents of Houston (IIAH): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Event technology saves time for staff and attendees by easily leveraging AMS data to streamline onsite check-in, floorplan, and exhibitor processes.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The Independent Insurance Agents of Houston (IIAH)
“Our attendees loved it. Our staff loved it. But what I loved most was how it kept our account receivables down.”
Executive Director of The Independent Insurance Agents of Houston (IIAH)

The Independent Insurance Agents of Houston (IIAH) hosts a one-day insurance industry tradeshow, one of the largest in the country.  Expo Logic’s Badge[On]Demand allowed IIAH to save time for staff and attendees and GoExpo by Expo Logic provided a win-win solution for both staff and exhibitors with a more streamlined exhibitor and floorplan management process.

The small association staff at IIAH needed a better way to manage the manual tasks for both attendee badging and exhibitor management during their annual tradeshow, which has been consistently growing and selling out year after year.  

In years prior, IIAH manually handled the printing and stuffing of badges, sorted and printed meal tickets, and  organized them in envelopes with printed labels. As this annual event grew, the small staff needed a more efficient method that would alleviate the burden of manual pre-conference duties. Given the increase in attendance, IIAH also hoped to eliminate long check-in lines for attendees. With over 130 exhibitors, the association staff struggled to manage the floor plan, sponsorship levels, registration, and directory while juggling frequent changes in spreadsheets and the printed floor plan.  

During a Momentive Software conference, the attending IIAH staff were amazed at the onsite check-in process for attendees. No one had to wait in long lines and the entire registration and badge-printing process took seconds to complete. IIAH was ready to explore event technology options and after talking with Expo Logic staff onsite, IIAH discovered the cost would be similar to their current plan for pre-printed badges, badge holders, ribbons, tickets, envelopes, and labels — before factoring in the staff hours necessary for this monumental annual task. The biggest attraction was the way Badge[On]Demand integrated with YourMembership and brought over the different registration types and tickets for attendees.  

Additionally, after describing their existing manual process for managing exhibitors to Momentive Event Management staff, IIAH learned about GoExpo’s ability to eliminate the printed floor plan and transition to an interactive digital floor plan with exhibitor listings managed more efficiently.

Momentive Event Management’s Badge[On]Demand allowed IIAH to save time for staff and attendees. GoExpo by Momentive Event Management provided a win-win solution for both staff and exhibitors with an interactive floor plan, easier way to manage exhibitor directory listings, and a streamlined exhibitor signup process.  

With more attendees expected at the event than ever before, IIAH was relieved by the new automation leading up to the event. When attendees and exhibitors arrived at the event, they did not experience waiting in long lines or idling while staff sifted through registration packets for their tickets. The Momentive Event Management technology eliminated their need for ribbons and tickets, as Badge[On]Demand’s dynamic printing distinguished attendee types and even icons for meal tickets directly on the badge. Leadership was also impressed by the ability to keep receivables down. This was achieved by Badge[On]Demand’s integration and ability to recognize an outstanding balance at the time of the event, which would prohibit the badge printing until paid. Managing the exhibitor floor plan through GoExpo provided easier management of the daily changes that take place, as well as provided a smoother process for exhibitors signing up.

The Independent Insurance Agents of Houston (IIAH) is a nonprofit trade association of local Houston area independent insurance agencies and affiliate companies. Chartered by the State of Texas in 1924, it is the largest local independent insurance agent association in the U.S. IIAH is a volunteer-based organization with a full-time staff to serve its members through education, advocacy, networking and community service. With 10 working committees which comprise of 100 active volunteers, the Independent Insurance Agents of Houston is truly the voice of the Houston independent insurance agents.
How event tech saves time using AMS data and automation

item.title: Comic Con: Momentive Event Management Success Story

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
Since 1970, San Diego Comic Convention (SDCC) has held a comics and multi-genre popular arts event annually which has grown to attract well over 135,000 registered fans from all over the world. 

The organization wanted a total registration solution that could be configured and scaled to meet the demands of their complex event. Momentive Software was able to simplify their processes for the thousands of registrants, exhibitors, volunteers, and more

As with many complex events, SDCC faced the challenge of using multiple disparate systems resulting in having data stored across multiple systems which resulted in more manual work for staff. 

 

Since Momentive Software’s products communicate seamlessly together, SDCC was able to streamline their processes and effectively go from system to system. Momentive Software gave them more control with the power to make edits themselves and accomplish more with less.

With Configio and other Momentive Event Management Software solutions, the organization has saved staff countless hours of manual work allowing them to focus on creating more memorable experiences for attendees.

San Diego Comic Convention (SDCC) produces Comic-Con International and is dedicated to creating the general public’s awareness of and appreciation for comics and related popular art forms, including participation in and support of public presentations, conventions, exhibits, museums, and other public outreach activities which celebrate the historic and ongoing contribution of comics to art and culture.

item.title: The Hematology/Oncology Pharmacy Association: Momentive Event Management Software Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how HOPA utilized Momentive Event Mangement Software to securely streamline their event experience and reduce the need for multiple apps.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: HOPA Event Security Case Study: Safety & Compliance

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"all";s:16:"data_description";s:97:"of HOPA’s goals were met by the app, providing security and streamlined access across platforms";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:6:"secure";s:16:"data_description";s:82:"access to event information provided by Momentive Event Mangement's login solution";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"91%";s:16:"data_description";s:68:"of total attendees downloading and using the HOPA app for each event";}}
A Single Solution for Security and Streamlined Event Access

HOPA hosts several events throughout the year and wanted a solution that offered a single app for all of their events. Momentive event app solution enabled HOPA attendees to log in to a single app and access all of HOPA’s events taking place throughout the year. Instead of downloading separate apps for each event, HOPA attendees could download a single HOPA app and, within that app, access each event's page. Each event operated independently, with its own branding, login, and customizable features, which were integrated seamlessly with their AMS, to achieve HOPA’s goals of security and easy access.

The single most important goal HOPA sought to achieve when selecting its event mobile app was to ensure its security. Momentive achieved this goal by implementing a login for each event, requiring users to enter a username and password to access each event.

HOPA also wanted to take its app one step further and make it easy for attendees to access each event by using the same credentials the user previously used to register for the event. This was achieved by implementing a login for each event and setting up web services to connect the login screen to the HOPA registration system. The outcome was a seamless login process that would keep the information HOPA publishes secure, while allowing users to avoid remembering multiple passwords to access HOPA event-related information.

Momentive staff set up a web service link between the event app and the registration database. However, instead of pulling in and storing each user’s individual username and password from the database, only a validation web service was used. This took security to the next level, as no user’s personal credentials were stored in the app’s system.

Both HOPA attendees and staff were thrilled with the app. It met all of HOPA’s goals, providing security and streamlined access across platforms.
HOPA, the Hematology/Oncology Pharmacy Association, brings together pharmacy practitioners to advance the study, research, and support of hematology, oncology, and pharmacy practices.
How HOPA streamlined events with Momentive software
Streamlined Event Security with Momentive Event App
