item.title: The American Society of Neuroradiology (ASNR): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: See how ASNR leveraged Momentive Event Management Software to save staff time, stay organized, and reduce redundancies in their event planning and management.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: With the number of submissions we receive, I couldn’t manage without the reporting feature, helping to keep track of it all.
With the number of submissions we receive, I couldn’t manage without the reporting feature, helping to keep track of it all.
Director of Education, Society of Neuroradiology

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:10:"eliminated";s:16:"data_description";s:73:"manual online forms across multiple systems, helping staff stay organized";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:10:"duplicated";s:16:"data_description";s:88:"their event each year in the app, with the flexibility to update it as the event evolves";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"reduced";s:16:"data_description";s:33:"redundancies and saved staff time";}}
Simplifying the Educational Conference Planning Process

The American Society of Neuroradiology (ASNR) holds one annual meeting per year, providing attendees with the opportunity to learn the latest best practices in neuroradiology through lectures, research presentations, and exhibits. The conference spans five days and comprises 80 sessions, featuring over 250 oral presentations and approximately 600 e-posters.
The ASNR event staff was stretched thin, as all conference planning processes were handled manually, from collecting abstracts and obtaining financial disclosures to hunting down co-authors. Since the ASNR’s data wasn’t integrated into one powerful system, reporting lacked quality and consistency for planning and improving their annual event.

The events team needed a conference management solution that could be configured and scaled to meet the demands of their abstract submission and review process, as well as to organize and build sessions.

ASNR receives over 1,000 abstract submissions for their annual conference and didn’t have an efficient planning process in place to support it. Since their staff consisted of logistics event planners with little experience in the education department, they relied on committee members to help organize the conference’s content. They had a very manual process with multiple, disconnected online forms to collect and review abstract submissions, build out sessions, and publish a conference agenda.

ASNR needed a solution that would streamline their abstract submission and review process, creating an even spread across speakers, topics, tracks, demographics, and regions when building the session schedule. ASNR chose to partner with Momentive Event Management to gain more control of their annual conference planning and execute more efficient processes. The partnership enabled ASNR to develop a robust content strategy that will ultimately attract more attendees.

Since their partnership, ASNR has established a consistent, clear, and concise platform to organize their annual conference. ASNR has established a consistent process for speakers and authors year after year, providing their staff with in-depth reporting, a reduction in the duplication of speakers and topics, and a way to keep abstracts, speakers, sessions, and the agenda organized all within one platform.

The American Society of Neuroradiology (ASNR) is a professional membership society comprised of almost 6,000 physicians specializing in the field of neuroradiology. They promote the highest standards for clinical practice, education, and research in neuroradiology.
Discover how ASNR used Momentive Event Management to save time, streamline planning, and reduce redundancies. Learn more today.
How ASNR streamlined event planning with Momentive
Simplified Conference Planning with Momentive Events

item.title: Geoconvention: A Momentive Event Management Software Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: GeoConvention was looking to create a robust event app that made vast amounts of technical abstracts easily accessible, while also facilitating easy networking and engagement among attendees.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: GeoConvention Increased App Usage by an Astounding 185%
GeoConvention created an event app to access technical abstracts and enable networking and engagement.
Best I have seen.” “Great, Great, Great.” “I was very impressed.” “Well done.
Best I have seen.” “Great, Great, Great.” “I was very impressed.” “Well done.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-line-chart";s:13:"feature_title";s:27:"App usage increased by 185%";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-notepad";s:13:"feature_title";s:60:"GeoConvention received overwhelmingly positive app feedback.";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:20:"box-bx-book-bookmark";s:13:"feature_title";s:59:"GeoConvention cut printing costs by foregoing its handbook.";}}

GeoConvention was looking to create a robust event app that made vast amounts of technical abstracts easily accessible, while also facilitating easy networking and engagement among attendees. The result was a 185% increase in app adoption.

Because this is a large scientific event, a significant amount of information is presented and available to attendees, both in downloadable form and through sessions. GeoConvention wanted to make sure that technical information was as accessible as possible through their mobile event app. With Momentive Event Management, technical data was easily accessible and downloadable, and information about the numerous concurrent scientific sessions was readily available and could be flagged for personalized scheduling.  

Momentive’s Client Challenge Game was the perfect answer to GeoConvention’s goal, serving as a fun motivator for attendees. This scavenger hunt-style game allows attendees to answer multiple-choice and photo-based questions through the app. GeoConvention posted a significant number of questions and boosted interest in the game by sending push notifications throughout the show, offering prizes for participation. Some of the questions were just for fun, while others showcased GeoConvention, and still others highlighted sponsor brands. The game boosted both attendee mobile app usage, resulting in increased engagement, and boosted GeoConvention’s sponsorship revenue (each question was sponsored!).

The game boosted both attendee mobile app usage, resulting in increased engagement, and boosted GeoConvention’s sponsorship revenue (each question was sponsored!). 

Attendees were thrilled with the app, as evidenced by the fact that app usage increased by 185 percent. GeoConvention received overwhelmingly positive app feedback. The increased app usage led to a significant increase in sponsorship revenue, and GeoConvention reduced printing costs by eliminating its handbook.

GeoConvention is an annual convention produced by the Canadian Society of Petroleum Geologists (CSPG), Canadian Society of Exploration Geophysicists (CSEG) and the Canadian Well Logging Society (CWLS). This convention provides delegates and attendees with the opportunity to network, learn about the latest advancements in geoscience, and explore exhibitor offerings through its robust trade show component.
How GeoConvention boosted engagement with a rich event app

Path LMS is an integral part of delivering a wide variety of education opportunities to our thousands of members across many product lines. Features such as enhanced searching make it so easy to zero in on just the right course that provides answers to common business problems. Our members love it!

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:8:"3-4 days";s:16:"data_description";s:142:"saved each month on manually uploading activity reports, reducing the staff time required to process registrations and post-event activities ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:9:"increased";s:16:"data_description";s:136:"member engagement scores through activity, raising engagement scores through activity tracked in Path LMS and reported back to their AMS";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"reduced";s:16:"data_description";s:188:"registration time and steps for each event, improving menber experience with a seamless single sign-on (SSO) experience and allowing NetVU to report all learning activity back to their AMS";}}

NetVU was seeking a better way to reach members and provide them with a deeper understanding of software operation and business skills development to help them further their career objectives. Their goal was to be the first place members would turn to for industry education, advocacy, and news.

NetVu came to us with three goals in mind. The first was to provide members with a deeper understanding of software operation and help them further their career objectives. Second, they wanted to develop an efficient internal process and workflow for educating members. And finally, they wanted to be the first place members turn to for industry education, advocacy, and news.

Using Path LMS, NetVU created a central, self-paced learning platform that lets members take online courses for deep-dive certifications, as well as attend virtual events. By integrating Path LMS with their association management system (AMS), members get a seamless single sign-on (SSO) experience, and NetVU can report all learning activity back to their AMS.

Path LMS also hosts NetVU’s virtual events, including webinars and annual conferences. The integrated registration engine on Path LMS allows NetVU to run virtual chapter meetings and a webinar series centered around popular topics.

NetVU was able to reduce the staff time required to process registrations and post-event activities. They also saw increased member engagement scores through activity tracked in Path LMS and reported back to their AMS.

• Improved member experience by reducing the registration time and steps for each event
  • Reduced staff time required to process registrations and post-event activities
  • Real-time updates of member activities through integration with iMIS
  • Increased member engagement scores through activity
  • Saved 3-4 days each month on manually uploading activity reports

The Network of Vertafore Users (NetVU) is an independent, national member organization committed to providing industry advocacy, world-class education and networking to the more than 30,000 insurance agencies, carriers, MGAs, and compliance organizations and over 500,000 users of Vertafore’s solutions.
NetVU increased member engagement scores and saved valuable time for staff members with Path LMS.

item.title: American Industrial Hygiene Association (AIHA): A Path LMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: AIHA uses the Path LMS Teams feature to streamline their eLearning delivery for increased content access, membership, and association revenue.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Path LMS Teams Feature Streamlines eLearning Delivery for AIHA
I’ve been very impressed with the resources behind the Teams functionality and their willingness to help with integrating it into our processes.
I’ve been very impressed with the resources behind the Teams functionality and their willingness to help with integrating it into our processes.
Program Director of eLearning at AIHA

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"5,300";s:16:"data_description";s:39:"people gained access to AIHA's content.";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"35%";s:16:"data_description";s:89:"year-over-year increase in revenue from eLearning subscriptions since implementing Teams.";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"55";s:16:"data_description";s:37:"active eLearning subscription groups.";}}
a:1:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:20:"box-bxs-add-to-queue";s:13:"feature_title";s:44:"External content access using Path LMS Teams";}}

The American Industrial Hygiene Association (AIHA) members rely on their services and expertise, including networking and comprehensive educational programming, to ensure the highest level of competency and safety in both scientific and professional fields.

Comprehensive learning programs are a central resource for AIHA members. AIHA aimed to continue offering content tailored to their members' diverse needs while finding a seamless way to expand their reach by selling to external organizational groups. 

Colleen Manning, Program Director of eLearning at AIHA, and her team needed to streamline the purchasing process for organizational groups seeking to enroll multiple learners. Processing individual orders was eating up staff time. AIHA also sought a straightforward way to deliver a variety of eLearning subscription options tailored to the particular sectors and interests of different members. 

After vetting numerous learning management systems, Path LMS™ fit the bill for AIHA’s external content delivery goals. With extended membership needs in mind, we recommended that AIHA use the Path LMS Teams feature to enhance their LMS implementation.   

The Teams feature enables organizations to extend their reach by selling to external organizations, all within one easy-to-use interface. Within the Path LMS platform, external organizations can assign content to their group of learners and access segmented reports on learners’ progress.   

With the ability to grant the desired level of control to external organizations, Path LMS Teams provides organizations with expanded control and versatility. 

A built-in feature of Path LMS, Teams offers flexibility so that existing members of the owning organization don’t lose their group access if they are also added to an external organization’s group of learners. 

The Teams feature enabled AIHA to deliver content to their over 50 eLearning subscription groups and facilitate large seat number purchases by external organizations seeking to enroll a whole cohort of learners in a course at once. 

“[Teams] has allowed for greater brand recognition of the eLearning Subscription and Bulk Purchase program—users know they are getting access to the content due to their participation in a particular program,” said Manning.   

Teams has provided AIHA with seamless flexibility and additional features that enhance the usability and marketability of their eLearning Subscription and Bulk Purchase offerings. Associations typically lack the dedicated resources in marketing and sales to grow revenue and a customer base for the eLearning programs they create and sell. Still, Teams allowed AIHA to do just that.

As a result, AIHA saw a 35% year-over-year increase in revenue from eLearning subscriptions since implementing Teams. The organization now has 55 active eLearning subscription groups, and 5,300 people have gained access to AIHA's content. 

In the first year alone, three organizations made bulk educational content purchases. Bulk purchases have accelerated AIHA processing time and efficiency, reducing the need for individual orders.

The American Industrial Hygiene Association (AIHA) is dedicated to occupational and environmental health and safety in the workplace and community. With a mission to “create knowledge to protect worker health,” AIHA works to prevent work-related occupational and environmental health and safety (OEHS) hazards at a global scale. 

Industrial hygiene professionals engage with a wide array of roles and responsibilities. Over 8,500 members all over the country work in a variety of public and private organizations. More than half of the members are Certified Industrial Hygienists, and many hold additional professional designations.
AIHA uses the Path LMS Teams feature to streamline their eLearning delivery for increased content access, membership, and association revenue.
How AIHA boosted eLearning access and revenue with Path LMS.
AIHA Streamlines eLearning Delivery with Path LMS

item.title: Virginia Land Title Association (VLTA): A Path LMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: VLTA doubled its membership after implementing all-inclusive pricing, revamping its website, and launching a learning portal, all within ten months.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: VLTA doubled its membership in just ten months by implementing a learning management system and a robust online course catalog.
VLTA Doubled Its Membership in Just Ten Months with Path LMS
Thank you for being a part of our journey.
Thank you for being a part of our journey. We could not have made it work without the rapid-build ability. You are a cornerstone of our success and the success of our members.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"2x";s:16:"data_description";s:137:"Membership after implementing the all-inclusive pricing, revamping the website, and launching the learning portal—all within 10 months ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"34%";s:16:"data_description";s:30:"Increase in membership revenue";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"45%";s:16:"data_description";s:53:"Increase in combined membership and education revenue";}}

In 2013, VTLA’s membership was steadily declining, a trend that continued into 2014 at an alarming rate. The organization conducted membership surveys and interviews to determine the cause. They quickly learned that they did not have a strong membership value proposition and that their online presence was outdated. After a thorough analysis, VTLA determined that quick changes were necessary to offer their members what they wanted and needed most.

First, VTLA updated its website’s homepage to provide members with a better user experience and track member engagement. Then, in June of 2015, VLTA signed on to set up a learning management system (LMS). 

VLTA converted all of its educational content into approved, for-credit online courses for its membership, which consisted of insurance agents, real property attorneys, settlement agents, and title examiners. Additionally, they recrafted their title insurance pre-licensing class into an online course, which is required to obtain licensure in Virginia. By late October 2015, they launched the learning portal using Path LMS™. 

The next step was to repackage their entire member benefits structure to allow for an “all-inclusive” purchase level. This pricing allowed member companies and their entire staff access to the online learning portal with a paid membership. They also included other value-added components, including a digital magazine and newsletter, an updated website, a career center, and multi-track events with twice as many educational opportunities.

All these improvements led to impressive improvements across the board. VLTA doubled its membership after implementing the all-inclusive pricing, revamping the website, and launching the learning portal, all within ten months. 

Membership revenue increased by 34%, and combined membership and education revenue increased by 45%. With increased membership at the free events, sponsorship dollars naturally rose, which offset expenses incurred by VLTA.

Founded in 1974, the Virginia Land Title Association stands as a sentinel over Virginia’s title and settlement industry. VLTA’s singular purpose is to facilitate the safe and efficient transfer of real property in Virginia. This is why the VLTA sponsors valuable education, encourages new legislation, promotes high professional standards, and creates valuable networking opportunities for its members.
VLTA doubled membership using LMS and online courses

item.title: Institute of Food Technologists (IFT): A Path LMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: IFT needed a learning management system (LMS) that provided a myriad of eLearning solutions for its members, intending to increase revenue by 50%. They exceeded that goal in only seven months.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: 400% increase in revenue in only seven months with Path LMS
IFT implemented a powerful LMS to offer diverse eLearning solutions, exceeding their 50% revenue growth goal in just seven months. Learn how.
I do not have a technical background, but transferring content and building courses is really easy in Path LMS.

I really appreciated the responsiveness of our project manager and the rest of the staff. They listened to our needs and met all of our scheduled deadlines. I do not have a technical background, but transferring content and building courses is really easy in Path LMS. I also appreciate that they continue to develop and push out helpful features.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"400%";s:16:"data_description";s:132:"Increase in revenue over the previous platform after just seven months, with IFT's financial results significantly beating forecasts";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"reduced";s:16:"data_description";s:77:"Purchase transactions from seven to just two clicks, saving time and patience";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:8:"2 months";s:16:"data_description";s:85:"Was all it took to integrate Path LMS with IFT's AMS and make it available to members";}}

The Institute of Food Technologists (IFT) approached us seeking a more modern and mobile-friendly LMS to enhance the learning experience for their members. They were looking to improve online education revenue by 50% and use a single platform to sell live events and on-demand learning modules.

IFT needed a learning management system (LMS) that provided online courses, virtual meetings, webinars, and interactive eLearning solutions for its members, with a goal to increase revenue by 50%. In addition to wanting greater interactivity, self-assessment, and group discussions, they knew that a cloud-based solution for administering and delivering eLearning courses was key to their success. 

IFT chose Path LMS™, which was quickly integrated with their association management system (AMS), and the system was up and running for their members in only two months.  The initial setup included adding 56 webcasts and eight online courses to the platform. As a result, IFT's in-person learning became available to a worldwide audience with newly accessible online courses. Now, members can easily access courses anywhere and on any device with the Path LMS mobile-responsive interface.

Course creation in Path LMS is straightforward for IFT staff to execute, eliminating the need to hire designers for course development. In addition, reports are easy to run on Path LMS, and they provide data on everything from courses purchased to class averages. With this newly accessible information, IFT can continuously improve course offerings. 

After just seven months, IFT's financial results significantly beat forecasts. The learning content on Path LMS generated an unprecedented 400% increase in revenue over the previous platform.

The Institute of Food Technologists (IFT) exists to advance the science of food. Composed of more than 18,000 members from virtually every discipline related to food science and technology and from more than 100 countries worldwide, IFT aims to ensure a safe and abundant food supply, contributing to healthier people everywhere. IFT hosts several annual meetings, publishes a highly respected peer-reviewed journal, and offers a Certified Food Scientist (CFS) credential.
How IFT increased revenue 50% in seven months with an LMS

item.title: Decorative Plumbing + Hardware Association (DPHA): A Path LMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: DPHA’s members include manufacturers, independent representatives, and dealers of decorative plumbing and hardware products, like toilets, bathtubs, bathroom furniture, cabinet hardware, door hardware, and more. The association has over 2,700 individual members across more than 250 member companies.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Boosting Non-Dues Revenue with Path LMS
DPHA used Path LMS to create a certificate program that boosted non-dues revenue and provided manager visibility into employee progress.
Our members love that they can manage their employees’ progress with our courses

Before we broke it down into the two programs, it was just DPHA University, and there was a lot of confusion about paid vs. free content. Path LMS has worked with us to strategize how to customize the platform to work best for our organization and provide an easy-to-navigate experience for our learners.
Director of Marketing and Sales, DPHA

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"63%";s:16:"data_description";s:47:"Increase in revenue generation in just one year";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:9:"Increased";s:16:"data_description";s:115:"Enrollment numbers for microlearning, assessments, and tracking by switching from regular marketing emails to demos";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:9:"Automated";s:16:"data_description";s:117:"ertificate generation and badging through Path LMS, allowing companies to manage their members’ course progression ";}}

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bxs-graduation";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"On-demand courses ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bxs-group";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:"Path LMS Teams ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bx-git-compare";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:"AMS integration ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bx-award";s:13:"feature_title";s:26:"Credits and certificates ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:11:"box-bx-edit";s:13:"feature_title";s:25:" Quizzes and assessments ";}}

DPHA offers a gathering place and a platform for dealers, manufacturers, and representatives within the decorative plumbing and hardware industry. But industry networking isn’t the only valuable member benefit; the education programs offered by DPHA have always been a cornerstone of the association.

Initially, DPHA simply housed educational manuals on their website, lacking any way to track usage and engagement. Their recognition program relied on Google Forms, which was a very manual process for both participants and staff. Additionally, DPHA’s member company managers lacked a way to monitor employees’ progress and performance with the education program. 

DPHA knew it was time to implement a learning management system to create a more professional, automated, and robust online education program. Led by Director of Marketing and Sales Kymberly Glazer, DPHA partnered with us to make this vision a reality. 

DPHA chose Path LMS as the home of their new learning program, DPHA University. One of the key benefits of Path LMS that Kymberly identified was that the platform integrates with DPHA’s association management system, Novi AMS. 

The Novi AMS and Path LMS integration for DPHA provides a seamless learner experience between the two platforms. Member company managers and their employees can log in to DPHA University with the same credentials they use for DPHA via a single sign-on (SSO) integration. Path LMS also sends back eCommerce and activity data to Novi AMS, making it easy for Kymberly and her team to track and manage transactions and program completions. 

DPHA enlisted our Learning & Development team to help them determine how to offer and package their educational offerings under the DPHA University umbrella. They strategically broke their offerings into two programs: 

  • The Core is a content library in Path LMS that is accessible to all members, including DPHA education manuals and past webinars. 
  • The Vault is DPHA’s online testing and certificate program in Path LMS with courses that correspond to their education manuals. Most of the courses are broken into micro-learning modules to help with knowledge retention. 

Participants in the certificate program are required to pass tests within Path LMS with questions from an assessment pool. When each course is completed, learners receive an automatically generated certificate of completion. Once all of the Vault courses are completed, learners receive a digital badge for their own professional purposes, and the badge is added to their profile in Novi AMS by DPHA staff. 

One of DPHA’s biggest hurdles before implementing Path LMS was giving member company managers visibility into how their employees were progressing through the certificate program. This also hindered member companies from recognizing the real value of DPHA’s programs. 

DPHA leveraged the Path LMS Teams feature to give member company managers full visibility into employees’ progress. With Path LMS Teams, they can use a single sign-on login to manage their users (or team) and view and export a myriad of reports, including content metrics, assessment completions and scores, credit completion, and more.

While their education program had been a non-dues revenue stream since its inception over 10 years ago, they were able to bolster revenue generation with the new program format by 63% in just one year. “The Vault has made our professional program much more robust and has increased enrollment numbers. We have also been able to increase the cost of the program, increasing our revenue," says Kymberly. 

Comparing DPHA’s website and their Path LMS site, it’s easy to see the care and thoughtfulness behind the graphic design and user experience. Kymberly has seamlessly translated DPHA’s brand between the two sites and took advantage of the highly customizable Path LMS interface. The site has become an important marketing tool for DPHA: “As soon as I show prospective participants the microlearning modules, assessments, and the fact that they can track their team’s progress, then enrolling their teams becomes a no-brainer for them. Seeing, hearing, and understanding how it works through a demo has a different impact from a regular marketing email,” says Kymberly. 

Additionally, “Our members love that they can manage their employees’ progress with our courses,” says Kymberly.  “They love how professional everything looks and operates—it is WORLDS above and beyond what we used to have.”

The Decorative Plumbing + Hardware Association is a community of industry professionals whose goal is to connect, educate, and elevate the decorative plumbing and hardware industry. DPHA believes in the positive impact of bringing people together and elevating kitchen and bath spaces.
How DPHA grew non-dues revenue with Path LMS certificates

item.title: AMC Institute (AMCI): Momentive Learning Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: AMCI expanded member access to professional development, established revenue-generating programs, and achieved a 450% increase in platform engagement with Path LMS.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: AMCI achieves a 450% increase in platform engagement with Path LMS
AMCI expanded access to professional development, increased revenue programs, and achieved 450% engagement growth with Path LMS.
We especially like using Path LMS for our online learning content because of the ease of use.

We especially like using Path LMS for our online learning content because of the ease of use. This is great for the learners and for the staff. Uploading content to Path LMS is easy for staff, and the online learning is easy to navigate for individual learners.
CMP, CAE, Chief Operating Officer at AMCI

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"450% ";s:16:"data_description";s:32:"Increase in platform engagement ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"150";s:16:"data_description";s:72:"On-demand courses added to their library, focused on AMC-specific topics";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"New";s:16:"data_description";s:75:"Source of non-dues revenue created through specialized certificate programs";}}

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bxs-videos";s:13:"feature_title";s:23:"Live webinar hosting ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bxs-graduation";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"On-demand courses ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bx-book-content";s:13:"feature_title";s:32:"Multi-format content delivery ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bx-award";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"Professional certification ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bxs-badge-check";s:13:"feature_title";s:39:"Custom-branded certificates and badges ";}}

AMCI is the only organization focused on networking and education for association management companies (AMCs), providing these businesses with a destination to collaborate and discuss the challenges that AMCs face. By hosting in-person events like their annual conference, AMCI members can network with other AMC owners to share ideas and learn from each other how to better run their businesses.

AMCI recognized the valuable opportunity for expanded reach that online access to their conference sessions would provide their members. So, they sought out a partner and platform that would enable them to repurpose their in-person sessions as on-demand online content. 

They chose Path LMS™ to house AMCI’s Professional Advancement Online, which provides learning experiences designed to develop AMC competencies and hosts a wide range of AMC-focused educational content for all levels of professionals. 

Path LMS hosts AMCI's live webinars on current industry topics, programs and services, and membership updates. The content of their popular EduSeries webinars is member-driven based on session submissions for AMCI’s in-person meetings and other topics that come up throughout the year. Building on this success, AMCI's Path LMS site has grown to host nearly 150 on-demand courses accessible to members at their convenience. 

AMCI also uses Path LMS to host its certificate and specialist programs, which generate additional revenue while providing valuable professional development opportunities that help members advance in their careers. These Certified Association Executive (CAE)-eligible programs include: 

  • Professional Advancement Specialist Program: Designed to elevate proficiency in management areas essential for successful AMC operations, this robust program targets rising executives looking to master necessary skills. There are six specialist areas, each with an earnable digital badge, covering topics like finance, membership, leadership, and more. 
  • Certificate Program: From ethics to communication skills, AMCI's certificate program provides specialized foundational-level education in key functional areas to help rising AMC professionals advance their careers. 

The content for these programs is delivered in Path LMS through multiple formats including instructional video presentations, digital workbooks, resources, surveys, and final quizzes. Upon completion, learners automatically receive custom-branded certificates or badges for their achievements. 

Momentive’s virtual events team provides comprehensive management and facilitation for AMCI's professional EduSeries. This partnership extends beyond basic hosting to include production expertise, technical support, and audience engagement strategies that elevate the overall presentation quality. 

The managed webinar services include a custom live stream experience branded with AMCI's visual identity and AI-generated captions that ensure accessibility during all live broadcasts. Behind the scenes, Momentive’s team handles technical troubleshooting, presenter coaching, and audience Q&A moderation, allowing AMCI staff to focus on content delivery.

This partnership has dramatically improved member engagement with AMCI's digital offerings. Since implementing these enhanced live event services, AMCI has documented a remarkable 450% increase in active users on their platform—a clear indicator of the value members place on high-quality, accessible professional development resources. 

In addition, they created a new source of non-dues revenue through specialized certificate programs. The easy-to-navigate platform for both content creators and learners established a seamless experience for staff, speakers, and attendees. They also improved the professional appearance and production quality of live events with Path LMS.

The AMC Institute (AMCI) is the premier non-profit trade organization for association management companies (AMCs)—for-profit businesses that manage multiple nonprofit organizations. AMCI advances industry standards through accreditation programs, drives new business to its members, and creates educational and networking opportunities for professional development. With 180 AMC members managing more than 1,800 associations representing 2.8 million individual members, AMCI plays a crucial role in strengthening the association management profession and ensuring nonprofits receive quality management services.
How AMCI grew engagement 450% using Path LMS
AMCI Achieves 450% Engagement Growth with Path LMS

item.title: National Association of Enrolled Agents (NAEA): A Path LMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: NAEA now has over 300 products available on Path LMS. The increased breadth of available content for purchase resulted in a 400% increase in revenue since 2019.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: NAEA used Path LMS to Increase Non-Dues Revenue by 400%
NAEA now offers 300+ products on Path LMS. Expanded content drove a 400% revenue increase since 2019, discover how they achieved this growth.
Path LMS is very user-friendly, and new features are added all the time.

Path LMS is very user-friendly, and new features are added all the time. Our members have a great learning experience, and we can better keep track of our members’ CE requirements. Path LMS is easy for staff to navigate and create the products, and it’s easy for users to find their learning content.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"400%";s:16:"data_description";s:59:"Increase in revenue from their eLearning content since 2019";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"300+";s:16:"data_description";s:30:"Products available on Path LMS";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"1,000";s:16:"data_description";s:63:"Learners attended NAEA’s virtual certificate programs in 2022";}}

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bxs-videos";s:13:"feature_title";s:21:"Live webinar hosting ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bxs-graduation";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:"On-demand courses ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bx-award";s:13:"feature_title";s:25:"Credits and certificates ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bxs-palette";s:13:"feature_title";s:24:"Path LMS design services";}}

The National Association of Enrolled Agents (NAEA) is the nation’s leading community for tax practitioners of all levels. They provide extensive education offerings designed to help Enrolled Agents complete their continuing education requirements.

Enrolled Agents are federally-authorized tax advisors empowered by the U.S. Department of Treasury and represent taxpayers before the Internal Revenue Service for tax issues that include audits, collections, and appeals.

Becoming an Enrolled Agent entails more than just passing the initial three-part examination—it requires a significant amount of continuing education (CE). Enrolled Agents must complete a minimum of 16 CE credits and two ethics credits every year and complete 72 hours within three years to obtain license renewal.

As the leading education provider for Enrolled Agents, NAEA is a vital resource for these tax practitioners seeking to further their careers.

Without an easy-to-use, accessible cloud-based platform, NAEA could only offer a small selection of CE content in addition to in-person events and occasional webinars. Additionally, not utilizing a learning management system meant that NAEA wasn’t generating significant non-dues revenue from that CE content. 

NAEA chose Path LMS™ as the catalyst to help them expand their library of learning content and the reach of their certificate programs. Now, NAEA offers over 300 different products to its members and guests on Path LMS. 

Since its launch, NAEA has utilized Path LMS for in-person and virtual events, as well as live and on-demand webinars. They are hosting around ten webinars every month on Path LMS, including a free monthly webinar series that their members love. They’ve even created a popular “boot camp-style” certificate program where members can complete 12 hours of continuing education in one week.

“We launched the certificate programs last year, doing five virtually. We’re planning on doing six in 2023. Over 1,000 people took our certificate programs in 2022, and expect that number to grow in 2023,” said Erin Mosher, Director of Education & Events.

Our team also helped NAEA refresh the look and feel of its Path LMS site. “The site looks so sleek now,” mentioned Erin. “It’s much more user-friendly with explicit instructions for signing up and accessing content.”

Most importantly, Path LMS has made it much easier for NAEA members to report their continuing education credits. Members appreciate the convenience of receiving an automatically generated certificate after completing a live assessment in Path LMS.

“It’s just so easy for them to make sure everything’s right…in turn, it’s made our lives a lot easier. I honestly don’t know how we could do it without an LMS,” said Erin.

The organization now has over 300 products available on Path LMS. That increased breadth of available content for purchase meant revenue increased 400% since 2019! 

 “We launched the certificate programs last year, doing five virtually. We’re planning on doing six in 2023. Over 1,000 people took our certificate programs in 2022, and expect that number to grow in 2023,” said Erin Mosher, Director of Education & Events.

Our team also helped NAEA refresh the look and feel of its Path LMS site. “The site looks so sleek now,” mentioned Erin. “It’s much more user-friendly with explicit instructions for signing up and accessing content.”

Most importantly, Path LMS has made it much easier for NAEA members to report their continuing education credits. Members appreciate the convenience of receiving an automatically generated certificate after completing a live assessment in Path LMS.

“It’s just so easy for them to make sure everything’s right…in turn, it’s made our lives a lot easier. I honestly don’t know how we could do it without an LMS,” said Erin.

The National Association of Enrolled Agents (NAEA) is the nation’s leading community for tax practitioners, serving upwards of 8,000 members at various stages in their careers—from aspiring enrolled agents to experts with decades of experience. They provide a critical service to their members through their extensive education offerings designed to help Enrolled Agents complete their continuing education requirements.
NAEA grew revenue 400% with Path LMS

item.title: NAFSA: Association of International Educators: Momentive Learning Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: NAFSA needed an easily accessible (LMS) to manage and host webinars and offer on-demand recordings to their worldwide membership base.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: NAFSA reduces costs and expands their content library with Path LMS
NAFSA chose Path LMS to manage webinars & offer on-demand recordings to their worldwide membership base.

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bxs-videos";s:13:"feature_title";s:23:"Live webinar hosting ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bxs-graduation";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"On-demand courses ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:21:"box-bx-calendar-event";s:13:"feature_title";s:32:"Managed virtual event services ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bx-shopping-bag";s:13:"feature_title";s:33:"SSO and eCommerce integrations ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:21:"box-bxs-dollar-circle";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"Product bundles ";}}

Each year, NAFSA: Association of International Educators delivers an average of 16 live webinars to provide its members with timely and relevant information on successfully managing study abroad and student exchange programs.

NAFSA needed an easily accessible learning management system (LMS) to manage and host webinars and offer on-demand recordings to their worldwide membership base. The LMS needed to seamlessly handle registration, accommodate a membership database, and integrate with both an association management system (AMS) and eCommerce platform. Additionally, they wanted to drive member engagement, increase revenue, and better predict and control eLearning costs. 

NAFSA enlisted Path LMS™ to help manage live webinars and on-demand recordings. Integrating Path LMS with NAFSA's AMS database enabled single sign-on (SSO) for a seamless user experience, with all payment and registration information on one platform.

Path LMS allows NAFSA to integrate chats, polling, and native discussions to enhance social learning during virtual events. In addition, the virtual event team works with NAFSA to produce pre-recorded or "mock live" webinars, ensuring a smoother experience for volunteer presenters and more Q&A sessions.

Path LMS reduced costs and allowed for predictable budgeting of expenses, and reducing costs enabled NAFSA to offer a wider variety of content. Comprehensive technical support  ensured a superior presenter and attendee experience. 

Now, NAFSA can easily control costs while offering more choices to members and delivering content based on audience type. They gained the ability to bundle several on-demand events with similar themes to provide even more value and increase revenue.

NAFSA: Association of International Educators is the world’s largest nonprofit association dedicated to international education and exchange. NAFSA’s 10,000 members from more than 3,500 institutions in over 150 countries are a global community of administrators, advisors, instructors, students, advocates, and volunteers committed to the growth of international education.
How NAFSA expanded global learning with Path LMS.
NAFSA Reduces Costs and Expands Content with Path LMS

item.title: American College of Sports Medicine® (ACSM): Path LMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: ACSM's partnership with Path LMS modernized their educational offerings, launched new revenue-generating courses, and created a strategic roadmap for their 50,000 learners.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: American College of Sports Medicine | LMS Case
ACSM needed a strategic roadmap for their 50,000 learners, and Path LMS delivered.
You hire experts because they're experts.

Path LMS became an arm of ACSM, not just a vendor. They took the extra time, care, and attention to learn about us, and they cared about what we were doing, and that made the difference—when you can tell that the people that you're working with care just as much as you do.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:8:"Enhanced";s:16:"data_description";s:193:"The online educational experience for ACSM members by gaining a comprehensive understanding of educational gaps and needs and creating a clear roadmap for online learning department development";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:11:"Streamlined";s:16:"data_description";s:168:"The management of educational offerings by implementing Path LMS to serve as their online learning catalog, completing stalled course development project in four months";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:8:"Expanded";s:16:"data_description";s:204:"Availability and accessibility of educational content by maintaining revenue-generating content production during staffing transitions, establishing a foundation for future growth with scalable processes ";}}

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:23:"box-bxs-bar-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:59:"Strategic Workshop to analyze content and audience needs ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:11:"box-bx-bulb";s:13:"feature_title";s:68:"Ongoing instructional design support for revenue-generating content ";}}

ACSM's journey with educational content began with recording conference sessions on VHS tapes and cassettes that they would then sell to members. As technology evolved, the organization started exploring online education. They started utilizing a learning management system to host recorded content and reach professionals who could no longer attend in-person events due to the pandemic.

To keep pace with their members’ evolving needs and adapt to trends in adult learning, ACSM recognized the importance of transitioning from traditional recorded presentations to more interactive, technology-enabled experiences. This meant implementing SCORM-compliant solutions that could deliver engaging content to learners wherever they were. 

ACSM was also looking to expand their educational offerings through new course development initiatives by partnering with external vendors to enhance their content portfolio. 

ACSM recognized the need for expert partnership in learning technology and content development. "You hire experts because they're experts," notes Katherine Schowengerdt, Vice President of Operations at ACSM. After conducting an RFP process, ACSM chose Momentive and Path LMS for both their learning management system and Learning & Development services. 

Our technology-forward vision and deep understanding of associations are what set us apart. "Associations are different and have unique needs. We serve diverse customer groups who each require different types of content and varying levels of access," explains Schowengerdt. “This understanding, combined with their complete learning ecosystem that brings together innovative technology and expert Learning & Development services, made them a uniquely valuable partner.” 

Our partnership began with the Strategic Workshop that helped ACSM address one of their core challenges: creating a comprehensive educational path forward that would serve their diverse audience segments, from undergraduate students to PhD researchers. The Strategic Workshop provided a valuable outside perspective and helped create visual frameworks that made their complex organizational needs more digestible for both staff and volunteer leaders. 

On the content development front, our Learning & Development team tackled stalled course projects, implementing strong review processes and completing the remaining modules. This quick win demonstrated the value of having experienced instructional design support.

ACSM gained a better understanding of their gaps and needs, as well as how to effectively use both internal and external resources for learning content development. The Strategic Workshop enabled them to build a new online learning department with a clearly defined purpose. At the same time, the instructional design support has allowed them to continue producing revenue-generating content during staffing transitions.

The American College of Sports Medicine® (ACSM) is the largest sports medicine and exercise science organization in the world. Serving nearly 50,000 members and certified fitness professionals, ACSM supports students, academics, physicians, exercise physiologists, and personal trainers.
How ACSM managed 50,000 learners with Path LMS.
ACSM Modernizes Education and Revenue with Path LMS

item.title: Federal Laboratory Consortium for Technology Transfer (FLC): Path LMS Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Education is at the heart of FLC’s mission to enhance federal technological transfer. The increased turn toward eLearning in response to the impact of the Coronavirus pandemic necessitated a cohesive, reliable learning management system. With a network of 300 federal laboratories, agencies, and research centers, their eLearning community comes from various roles and contexts.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Interactive, Easy-to-Navigate Learning Content with Path LMS
FLC adopted Path LMS to create a learning center for online professional development curriculum.
Federal Laboratory Consortium for Technology Transfer (FLC)
The support you get with Path LMS has been unmatched by anything else we’ve ever seen.
Path LMS is not just a learning management system. The service you get with the contract is above anything I’ve ever seen…The staff will not stop until you are satisfied!
Director of Professional Development at FLC

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bx-book-reader";s:13:"feature_title";s:38:"Learning & Development services ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bxs-videos";s:13:"feature_title";s:23:"Live webinar hosting ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bxs-graduation";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"On-demand courses ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-line-chart";s:13:"feature_title";s:45:"Detailed learner analytics and data tracking ";}}

Over the years, FLC has compiled learning content on a website that lists everything available, but without any organization by level, career path, or content type. Responding to the shifting educational landscape, they brought on Amy Thomsen to guide the process of organizing decades of learning content into cohesive curriculum pathways for FLC careers. 

Experienced with several learning management systems (LMSs) as both a learner and an instructor, Thomsen was well aware of how academic LMS formats can lock users into dry and rigid content delivery and assessment. She was seeking an LMS that engaged FLC learners interactively and creatively.

FLC came to us with three primary goals for their LMS. They wanted to move content from a disorganized list to an interactive platform that guides learners through different career paths. They sought to replace traditional talking head videos and lengthy text content with an interactive curriculum that engages learners. And finally, they wanted to tailor educational offerings to suit the needs of a variety of audiences. 

FLC also had to consider how the new LMS would integrate with their existing technologies. Such integrations are notorious for challenges and headaches, which can delay a launch, reduce available features, and increase costs. The FLC team needed to be able to build and move independently and sought a consultative partner that would enable internal people to learn how to manage courses on their own. 

With concerns about upfront costs and time-consuming onboarding, FLC talked to five different vendors before deciding on Path LMS™ for their online learning center. Other LMS systems fell by the wayside as FLC found personalized answers to their questions and a team willing to work within their budget. Even more, Path LMS has a single sign-on integration with its existing association management system (AMS), Impexium. 

Thomsen, FLC’s Director of Professional Development, said, “We eliminated other LMS platforms mainly because your staff was incredible at getting all the answers I needed in a timely manner, as well as working with me on my budget. I was brand new to the job, but you did not take advantage of that. The team went above and beyond in explaining how contracts work and what questions I should be asking. They stood out among other vendors who just wanted a quick sale.”  

FLC and the Path LMS Learning & Development Team dove into developing interactive, easy-to-navigate learning content with Storyline 360 by Articulate, an interactive course development software that integrates with Path LMS. A big proponent for engaging adult learners, Thomsen emphasized the value that Storyline 360 brings to FLC’s content. Instead of 200 pages of content or an hour and a half video of a talking head, Thomsen loved being able to interweave interactive questions, voiceovers, videos, and visuals into content delivery to create “continuous movement for the learner.” 

Unlike other LMS experiences Thomsen has had, with Path LMS, “you’re not stuck with an off-the-shelf kind of LMS. All the samples are completely different, and I can bring a lot of creativity to it. Alternatively, you can give them an idea, and they are quick to come up with mockups for options. The support you get with Path LMS has been unmatched by anything else we’ve ever seen.” 

Using Path LMS, FLC has been able to gather detailed data on how learners navigate material in a way Thomsen never experienced with academic LMS offerings. With learner data, FLC could make strategic marketing and course development decisions moving forward. Gathering this data revealed a need for more intermediate and advanced content. FLC is now working with Blue Sky eLearn on developing those learning pathways! 

Integration and deployment of new courses took some troubleshooting, and Thomsen found our team’s approach refreshing, even under pressure. With personalized and flexible coaching, Thomsen was able to maximize and customize the benefits of what Path LMS has to offer, bringing FLC’s Learning Center to life.

The Learning Center on Path LMS is now accessible via the FLC website, providing access across their nationwide network. The platform includes courses, hosted virtual live events, and an on-demand video library covering a breadth of topics. FLC is now working to develop new intermediate and advanced content based on data they’ve gathered from Path LMS. Having shared their process with other departments at FLC, their marketing and outreach staff are now exploring ways to utilize Path LMS.

The Federal Laboratory Consortium for Technology Transfer (FLC) is a formally chartered, nationwide network. It promotes, facilitates, and educates labs and industries about the process of moving federal technologies out of laboratories and into the marketplace. By increasing the impact of new technologies, FLC aims to grow new industries, businesses, and jobs that benefit the U.S. economy, society, and national security.
How FLC built a professional learning center with Path LMS

item.title: American Network of Community Options & Resources (ANCOR): A Momentive Software Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover why the American Network of Community Options & Resources (ANCOR) chose Momentive Software to meet its technology needs.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: ANCOR Case Study | Momentive Software Resources
Discover why the American Network of Community Options & Resources (ANCOR) chose Momentive Software to meet its technology needs.
American Network of Community Options & Resources (ANCOR)
The theme that runs between Express Pay and on demand badge printing is delivering a better user experience with less staff time.
Membership Director, American Network of Community Options & Resources

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"6x ";s:16:"data_description";s:91:"attendee increase from previous event, and no lines at check in with onsite badge printing ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"80% ";s:16:"data_description";s:59:"approximate decrease in aged invoices by adding Express Pay";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"100%";s:16:"data_description";s:51:"immediate ROI after adding Express Pay in NetForum ";}}

ANCOR has over 2,500 members all over the United States. With a small staff. ANCOR creates a lot of opportunities to find efficiency in everyday tasks. Staff often found themselves burdened by manual processes including paper membership applications, mailing invoices, not receiving payments, and data input errors.

ANCOR’s annual conference was growing exponentially, and its current processes were not sustainable. To keep up, ANCOR started to explore event technology, adding onsite badge printing and offering lead capture to exhibitors.  

The integration between NetForum AMS and the Momentive badge printing solution allows NetForum to be the source of truth for all data. Attendee information pushes to the badging system, you can efficiently print badges onsite, accommodating last minute changes by tracking data in real time, so no waste occurs.

ANCOR’s largest event was a huge success due in part to Momentive’s solutions. The event had 1200 members and attendees, no long lines, and a great first event impression for new attendees. On demand badge printing provides attendees with a seamless, modern, quick and easy user experience with a quick scan of a QR code. 

With the power of NetForum and Express Pay, ANCOR staff are replacing paper and mailing membership processes with online renewals. Additionally, ANCOR experienced an immediate return on investment through Express Pay because it allows anyone to pay an invoice or multiple invoices through a secure online site.

For more than a half-century, ANCOR has worked to shape policy and share solutions to strengthen the ability of community-based providers, supporting people with intellectual and developmental disabilities. With 2,500 members operating in nearly every corner of the country, ANCOR is making inclusion a reality for people of all abilities.

item.title: The North Carolina Bar Association (NCBA): A Path LMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The North Carolina Bar Association chose Path LMS to manage virtual events services, streamline CLE management, expand its content library, and improve member satisfaction.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: How NCBA boosted member satisfaction and efficiency with Path LMS
From onboarding to ongoing support, the whole Path LMS team is really great to work with, and I always have direct access to any support I need.

Path LMS has really streamlined the user experience—from shopping for educational programs to watching a live webcast. From onboarding to ongoing support, the whole Path LMS team is really great to work with, and I always have direct access to any support I need.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"100+";s:16:"data_description";s:49:"Hours of new continuing legal education annually ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:6:"20,000";s:16:"data_description";s:72:"Lawyers, paralegals, law students, and other legal professionals served ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"125+";s:16:"data_description";s:70:"Years of serving the public and the legal profession in North Carolina";}}
The North Carolina Bar Association chose Path LMS to manage virtual events services, streamline CLE management, expand its content library, and improve member satisfaction.

The NCBA faced the challenge of enhancing its continuing legal education (CLE) delivery during the COVID-19 pandemic. While the NCBA had previously conducted online broadcasts, the transition to Path LMS was driven by the need for a more seamless integration with their new AMS and a more user-friendly catalog layout.

The integration with Path LMS allowed for a smoother transition and better management of virtual offerings, providing a more efficient and reliable platform for their members. The new system's improved catalog sync feature also streamlined operations, reducing administrative tasks and enhancing the overall user experience.

The integration between Path LMS and its AMS has streamlined administrative processes, reduced redundant data entry, and freed up staff time for other priorities. This efficiency has enabled the NCBA to focus on improving and expanding its CLE offerings.

The transition to online learning has been met with overwhelmingly positive feedback from NCBA members. Members greatly appreciate the flexibility to participate in live events remotely, coupled with the option to access rebroadcasts and on-demand content.

By leveraging Path LMS, the NCBA has successfully expanded its library of CLE programs, offering over 100 hours of new training content annually. This expansion has not only maintained but also enriched the professional development opportunities available to members and non-members.

The North Carolina Bar Association (NCBA) is a prominent legal association serving 16,000+ members. A voluntary membership organization of lawyers, judges, paralegals and law students, the NCBA is dedicated to serving the public and the legal profession.
The North Carolina Bar Association uses Path LMS to manage virtual events, streamline CLE, expand content, and improve satisfaction.
NC Bar improved CLE and events with Path LMS.
NCBA Improves Member Satisfaction with Path LMS

Previously ARMA International used an LMS that was very dated, offering limited search capabilities, and organizational features. The platform didn't support modern workflows, allowing staff to track or analyze content usage.

With its old LMS platform, ARMA felt like it couldn’t scale its organization effectively. ARMA selected Path LMS for its platform capabilities, responsive support, and instructional design services.

Since moving to Path LMS, member feedback has been overwhelmingly positive compared to ARMA’s previous platform. Additionally, increased sales from improved content organization and new bundle offerings demonstrate measurable business impact.

Transitioning to Path LMS delivered immediate operational improvements. ARMA staff have reported Path LMS has saved hours of time while making volunteers much happier. Additionally, Path LMS enabled instant content gap analysis, allowing staff to immediately identify areas needing more educational resources.

ARMA is the World's Leading Information Management Membership Organization. ARMA partners with information management & governance professionals, in their essential industry leadership roles—through education, thought leadership, research, peer community, technology, and other resources.

item.title: Plimoth Patuxet Museums: MIP Accounting Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: See how Plimoth Patuxet Museums improved financial reporting, streamlined accounting, and supported long-term growth with nonprofit fund accounting software. Read the full case study today.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: I would recommend MIP Accounting to anyone looking for effective software designed with the specific financial considerations of nonprofits in mind.
I would recommend MIP Accounting to anyone looking for effective software designed with the specific financial considerations of nonprofits in mind.

a:10:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-notepad";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"General Ledger ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-calculator";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"Accounts Payable";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bx-award";s:13:"feature_title";s:48:"Professional certification resources and bundles";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:22:"box-bx-pie-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:8:"Budget ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-bank";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:"Bank Reconciliation";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-lock";s:13:"feature_title";s:15:"Encumbrances ";}s:6:"item-6";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:16:"box-bx-file-find";s:13:"feature_title";s:33:"Purchase Orders with Encumbrances";}s:6:"item-7";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-sort-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Data Import Export ";}s:6:"item-8";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-user";s:13:"feature_title";s:30:"Additional Executive View User";}s:6:"item-9";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bx-dollar";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Grant Administration";}}

Plimoth Patuxet Museums has been an MIP customer for over 27 years. Through powerful personal experiences with history, Plimoth Patuxet tells the stories of the Indigenous Wampanoag community and the English colonists who created a new society – in collaboration and in conflict – in the 1600s. 

In a typical year, Plimoth Patuxet welcomes more than 300,000 visitors annually. In 2022, the living history museum – whose exhibits include the historic Mayflower II reproduction – will celebrate its 75th anniversary

With growth comes challenges, and Plimoth Patuxet Museums needed a robust module-based solution in order to seamlessly organize its finances. Struggling with disconnected systems proved to be inefficient and ineffective. They had half entries, incomplete posts and a cash account that was set up as a liability. For their small staff, reducing time spent on reporting, auditing and reconciling budgets was a pressing need. 

The museum’s finance team was also responsible for various time-sensitive reports for board meetings, monthly internal meetings and banks and census reports. 

Juggling diverse sources of funds encompassing the museum itself, gift shops, grants, donors and various programs and special events, the museum needed an easier path to integrating and reporting on all the funds.

Plimoth Patuxet can now depend on their financial accuracy, budgeting, and capabilities to run custom reports and easily manage multiple funding and revenue streams. 

Now over 25 years with MIP, they've traveled the path from MIP DOS to the Windows-based MIP Accounting software. Additionally, Plimoth Patuxet successfully implemented MIP module add-ons including: budget, executive view, encumbrances, purchase order, and data Import/Export to help manage the entire organization’s finances. 

“In my role as finance manager for Plimoth Patuxet, I have been using MIP for nearly three decades,” said Laura Reilly, Finance Manager. “While I have tried accounting software from a well-known global brand, it didn’t meet our needs well and was missing the fund accounting controls I feel are needed. I would recommend MIP Accounting to anyone looking for effective software designed with the specific financial considerations of nonprofits in mind.”

About Momentive Software   

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.   

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

Through powerful personal experiences of history, Plimoth Patuxet Museums tell the stories of the Wampanoag people and the English colonists who created a new society – in collaboration and in conflict – in the 1600s. Major exhibits include Mayflower II, the historic Patuxet Homesite, the 17th-Century English Village, and the Plimoth Grist Mill. 

A private, 501(c)(3) not-for-profit educational organization, the Museum is a Smithsonian Institution Affiliate and receives support from the Massachusetts Cultural Council, private foundations, corporations, and local businesses.
Plimoth Patuxet Museums improved reporting, streamlined accounting, and supported growth with fund accounting software.
How Plimoth Patuxet improved fund accounting
