For me, MIP has been about the ease of accounting, and I’d buy it all over again.
Director of Finance, Central Kansas Mental Health Center (CKMHC)

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:30:"7+ years using MIP Accounting ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:37:"Unlimited number of accounts tracked ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:16:"12 modules used ";}}

a:12:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-notepad";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"General Ledger ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-calculator";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"Accounts Payable";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:22:"box-bx-pie-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:8:"Budget ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-bank";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:"Bank Reconciliation";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:23:"box-bxs-bar-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"Accounts Receivable Reporting";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:11:"box-bx-file";s:13:"feature_title";s:27:"Accounts Receivable Billing";}s:6:"item-6";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-sitemap";s:13:"feature_title";s:21:"Allocation Management";}s:6:"item-7";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-sort-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Data Import Export ";}s:6:"item-8";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bxs-palette";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"Forms Designer";}s:6:"item-9";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bx-transfer-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:6:"EFT AP";}s:7:"item-10";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bx-dollar";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Grant Administration";}s:7:"item-11";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-receipt";s:13:"feature_title";s:11:"Order Entry";}}

Central Kansas Mental Health Center was running into issues with their old accounting software.  

“We were growing, and it was difficult to add account numbers to the system without totally reformatting everything,” Sheila Srna, Director of Finance at Central Kansas Mental Health Center, said. “We weren’t able to produce departmental income statements, and we had some areas that the system consolidated, that we didn’t want consolidated. We began searching for a new system to solve the issues of what we couldn’t do now.”

Central Kansas Mental Health Center was using an accounting system that was cumbersome in its formatting and was no longer meeting the needs of the center’s finance department. 

In 2018, CKMHC made the switch to MIP Accounting. MIP helped CKMHC track an unlimited number of accounts, define infinite segments, and tailor functions to meet their organization’s needs, all while staying compliant with accounting and audit standards and county and state reporting guidelines

CKMHC could tell their organization’s financial story while staying compliant with accounting and audit standards as well as county and state reporting guidelines. 

“There are a lot of things about MIP that make accounting easy for us,” Srna said. “We really built MIP to grow with it. It gave us flexibility. When we went into MIP, we knew it was a system that we could grow with.”  

During and after CKHMC’s implementation, Srna said the support she received was top-of-the-line.  

“We had a very good implementation,” commented Srna. “If there was something we didn’t know how to do, [our support member] was always a phone call away.”  

Now that they use MIP, Srna couldn’t imagine using any other system.

About Momentive Software   

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.   

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

Central Kansas Mental Health Center’s mission is to make life better in their communities by providing excellent mental health services, education and integrated care which exceeds patients’ expectations. The center is one of 26 community mental health centers in the state of Kansas and serves around 4,500 patients annually, with growing patient volumes.
Central Kansas Mental Health: Accounting Success
See how Central Kansas Mental Health Center improved financial visibility, streamlined reporting, and boosted efficiency with nonprofit accounting software.
How CKMHC improved financial visibility with accounting
Kansas Mental Health Center Accounting Win

item.title: Humanities North Dakota: MIP Accounting Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how Humanities North Dakota improved financial management, streamlined reporting, and enhanced efficiency with nonprofit fund accounting software. Read the full case study today.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: MIP was the only system that worked really well... And that’s why we’ve stuck with it for so long.
MIP was the only system that worked really well... And that’s why we’ve stuck with it for so long.

a:13:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-notepad";s:13:"feature_title";s:15:" General Ledger";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-calculator";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"Accounts Payable";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:22:"box-bx-pie-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:6:"Budget";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:21:"box-bx-money-withdraw";s:13:"feature_title";s:9:"Payroll ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-bank";s:13:"feature_title";s:21:"Bank Reconciliation ";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:23:"box-bxs-bar-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"Accounts Receivable Reporting";}s:6:"item-6";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bxs-bookmarks";s:13:"feature_title";s:15:" Fixed Assets ";}s:6:"item-7";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-sort-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:"Data Import Export";}s:6:"item-8";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-user";s:13:"feature_title";s:21:"Executive View User ";}s:6:"item-9";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bxs-palette";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"Forms Designer";}s:7:"item-10";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bx-download";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"Direct Deposit";}s:7:"item-11";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bx-transfer-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:8:"EFT AP ";}s:7:"item-12";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bx-dollar";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Grant Administration";}}

Since 1973, Humanities North Dakota has offered opportunities for lifelong learning through affordable classes and events to North Dakota residents. Ken Glass, Associate Director of Humanities ND, joined the organization in 1986 and has been in the middle of it all, with MIP Accounting® at his side.  

Humanities ND switched to MIP in 1985 after an auditor recommended the organization replace its paper books that consisted of long ledgers and multiple columns. It wasn’t until Glass arrived that Humanities ND, dove into the program and began to optimize its finances for the digital age using the MS-DOS version of MIP, one of the first versions of the software. 

“Back then, your computer had two disc drives. One for the operating system, and one for whatever program you were running,” Glass said. “We had to use these big floppy discs to run the program.”  

“Even from the beginning, MIP worked for our needs. It worked really well because we received large federal grants from the National Endowment for the Humanities, and those grants are open for as long as five years. We needed a system that could manage those grants.”

Budgeting, especially for grants, is a large portion of Glass’ job. With MIP, he could easily account for grants over a five-year period and appreciated how MIP lets him compare Humanities ND’s budget to actual spending.  

MIP also allows Glass and Humanities ND to account for various types of funding like state funding, foundation grants, gifts and donations, restricted gifts, and so many others. When it comes to budgeting, Glass likes how MIP can track each funding source with its own budget. 

“[Grantors and organizations] want to know how their money was spent,” said Glass. “[With MIP], we’re able to produce a report for a single foundation, but we can also do reports for the whole project budget and for programs across various funding 

“MIP was the only system that worked really well,” Glass said. “....And that’s why we’ve stuck with it for so long.” 

Many Humanities ND grants have a cost-matching element where the organization must match each grant dollar-for-dollar as the money for the grant is allocated and spent. The organization also receives donations to offset the spending. Glass created a special fund, which he refers to as the “Cost-Share Fund” to track the cost-share from each of these grants and ensure the organization meets the cost-sharing requirements. 

“For-profit bookkeeping systems have all these extra spreadsheets trying to keep track of prior years, budgets, and all this business,” Glass said. “[MIP] makes it much easier to see [your organization’s fiscal picture] all at once. You print out just the reports you need for that year or for the last three years, or if you need to look at just one source of funds … you can do that as well as looking at 

“It's the all-in-one accounting solution for us,” Glass said.

The last 38 years have been great for Glass, but he’s preparing to retire, and his son Nick Glass, Fiscal Operations Assistant, is preparing to take over. “He decided that it’s time to move past DOS,” Ken Glass joked. With Nick Glass' encouragement, Humanities ND decided to move into MIP Cloud—the time-tested MIP platform in an always-connected, modern interface. 

Humanities ND is excited at the prospect of allowing multiple people to look at their finances simultaneously and can’t wait for the opportunity to access MIP remotely. 

The organization is also taking its payroll paperless with MIP’s Payroll Module, moving away from a manual operation after 38 years. 

“We currently process payroll on a spreadsheet,” Ken Glass said. “It’s time to move on from that too.” 

As Ken Glass retires and Humanities ND moves to MIP Cloud, they’re ready to keep spreading the love of lifelong learning to North Dakota residents for years to come, and MIP is ready to keep accounting for their future.

About Momentive Software   

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.   

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

Humanities North Dakota is an independent nonprofit organization that has developed affordable and engaging classes and events since 1973. It is a nonprofit dedicated to helping North Dakota residents experience the benefits of lifelong learning.
Humanities North Dakota enhanced financial management, reporting, and efficiency with fund accounting software.
How Humanities ND improved efficiency with fund accounting

a:12:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"General Ledger";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:20:"box-bxs-user-account";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"Accounts Payable";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bx-money";s:13:"feature_title";s:11:"Budget ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bxs-notepad";s:13:"feature_title";s:10:"Payroll ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-bank";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:"Bank Reconciliation";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bxs-report";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"Accounts Receivable Reporting";}s:6:"item-6";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bxs-bookmarks";s:13:"feature_title";s:12:"Encumbrances";}s:6:"item-7";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:16:"box-bxs-cart-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:33:"Purchase Orders with Encumbrances";}s:6:"item-8";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-data";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Data Import Export ";}s:6:"item-9";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-user";s:13:"feature_title";s:32:"Additional Executive View User ";}s:7:"item-10";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:20:"box-bxs-calendar-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:"Forms Designer ";}s:7:"item-11";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:25:"box-bx-subdirectory-right";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"Direct Deposit ";}}

Lowcountry Community Action Agency was looking to upgrade to software that featured internal controls and could cut audit preparation time. The agency sought out software that would produce grant reports much faster, reduce the burden of audits, and save time on budgeting. They found MIP Accounting and gained a supportive team from implementation to everyday execution.
The agency needed to save time accounting for and managing budgets, grant reports for over 20 local, state, and federal grants totaling over $16 million annually, and payroll for 70-plus employees.

With Tamika Shiggs, fiscal officer, at the helm and MIP by her side, the agency cut its audit preparation time by 50%, from six months to three months. They share view-only access, through an Executive View License, with their auditor, eliminating the time and costs to print all documents or upload them to a secure file transfer service. By including the auditor at the very start of the process, they streamlined communication. When Lowcountry’s auditor has a question, they reach out, and the agency quickly and easily uploads the necessary documentation.   

Before Shiggs joined Lowcountry in October 2021, she worked with them in another capacity and knew their internal controls could be improved. MIP’s robust internal controls now segment the agency’s information by title or role, allowing Lowcountry to control who and which roles can access financial data.  

“MIP is all about internal controls,” Shiggs said. “I can lock a grant, flag a grant when the line item or budget is at a certain percentage or before it goes over.”   

When it came to budgeting, Lowcountry cut the amount of time spent developing budgets by nearly 50%. With the old software, they had to start with a blank budget each year. With MIP, they use a past year’s budget as a starting template for new budgets, which saves considerable time and prevents data entry mistakes. 

They saw immense benefits with grants too. With 20-plus grants, the agency needed a way to easily and efficiently track and report on them. With MIP, the agency now tracks grants across multiple budget years and creates reports however needed, whether starting blank, from previous reports, or from a template to save hours of time.   

“I love the way that you can report quality monthly, yearly, however you desire, whatever the request may be,” Shiggs said. “... You can specify the grants, identify your object code and also it allows you to forecast. It is very user-friendly when it comes down to the fundamental part of the grants and the funding.” 

Above all else, Shiggs and Lowcountry wanted a trusted partner that would make the implementation process easy and provide support for questions or issues that might come up in the process of transitioning accounting software. With nearly 200 years of combined experience, MIP’s support team partnered with the agency and was readily available to provide complete support.   

“Whenever we reach out, … you all find a result, and if you can’t find a result immediately, you don’t just leave us hanging out there,” Shiggs said.

About Momentive Software  

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.  

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

Lowcountry Community Action Agency is a private non-profit organization established in 1974, because of the war on poverty. The agency provides services to low-income constituents and families of Colleton and Hampton counties in South Carolina. Services include financial assistance, outreach and referral, advocacy, counseling, case management, training and employment, youth development, emergency homeless shelter, health, and nutrition, Early Head Start, and Head Start.

How LCAA improved audits with MIP Accounting

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"30+ ";s:16:"data_description";s:28:"Years of sustained success ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"43 ";s:16:"data_description";s:39:"Cities in southern Los Angeles serviced";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"12 ";s:16:"data_description";s:13:"Modules used ";}}

a:12:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-notepad";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"General Ledger";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-calculator";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:" Accounts Payable";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:22:"box-bx-pie-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:8:"Budget ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:21:"box-bx-money-withdraw";s:13:"feature_title";s:9:"Payroll ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-bank";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:"Bank Reconciliation";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:23:"box-bxs-bar-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"Accounts Receivable Reporting";}s:6:"item-6";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:11:"box-bx-file";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"Accounts Receivable Billing ";}s:6:"item-7";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-lock";s:13:"feature_title";s:15:" Encumbrances ";}s:6:"item-8";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:16:"box-bx-file-find";s:13:"feature_title";s:33:"Purchase Orders with Encumbrances";}s:6:"item-9";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bxs-bookmarks";s:13:"feature_title";s:12:"Fixed Assets";}s:7:"item-10";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-sort-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Data Import Export ";}s:7:"item-11";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bxs-user-detail";s:13:"feature_title";s:25:" Executive View Licenses ";}}

Right before pen went to paper, a pivotal moment in a meeting 20 years ago set Lakewood, California-based Water Replenishment District up for sustained, consistent success and a future intertwined with MIP Accounting®.

Senior Accountant Binhyen Bui had just joined the organization in May 2002, and although Water Replenishment District was an early adopter of MIP and first contracted with the company in 1991, they were at a crossroads after Bui finished onboarding.

The Problem Bui’s manager had begun the process of recommending the organization move to a national payroll processing company instead of using MIP’s payroll capabilities with the goal of having the organization’s payroll process seamlessly continue after her departure.

Water Replenishment District contacted the payroll processing company and began working on a contract to take over payroll processing. As a government organization, Water Replenishment District has specific payroll requirements around labor allocation, cost, benefits, taxes, and other similar elements to remain compliant.

“We explained how we wanted to split the different programs because, as a government organization, we have to make sure we distribute and allocate our labor and related costs accurately to the different programs we’re working on,” Bui said.

Just as Water Replenishment District leadership was ready to sign the contract at the final meeting, the payroll processing company pivoted and recommended the district continue with MIP.

“They said, ‘You guys have the best software right now for you,’” Bui said. “You need to keep what you have right now.”

MIP’s payroll module would allow Water Replenishment District to have more control of its financial data. By keeping payroll in-house, they could react to changes quicker, and have complete control of their data.

For example, Water Replenishment District could easily account for payroll cost elements like employee benefits and labor allocation in lieu of having to communicate with an outside party. By keeping payroll in-house, it allowed the Water Replenishment District to minimize the potential for errors and ensured payroll cost elements such as labor allocations and related costs were allocated correctly.

Using one multi-purposed accounting system would reduce redundancies and allow the team to be more efficient, instead of relying on multiple systems to perform individual, siloed tasks.

Water Replenishment District took their advice and decided to stick with MIP. Bui got up to speed on payroll processing and was able to take over payroll processing for the organization seamlessly. It was a massive success.

Twenty years since that meeting, Bui and Water Replenishment District are still using MIP with fantastic results.

“MIP is a great fit for our government sector accounting because we are able to track and drill down on specific costs and develop customized reports,” Bui said.

As the senior accountant, Bui oversees the Water Replenishment District’s finances and has even got involved with training.

“It’s really user-friendly,” Bui said. “When we set it up and we train someone, it doesn't take long for that person to learn, even if they’re a first-time user.”

As Water Replenishment District has grown and evolved, the finance department has evolved too, and as more people join the finance team and get involved with MIP, Bui and MIP are at the center of it all.

“When they learn [MIP], they no longer compare it to anything else,” Bui said. “…They learn it quickly and pick it up right away. … For example, when I’m training someone, if they have a question or don’t know something, it’s easy to go into [MIP] and play around with the system. … It doesn’t take long for me to train them, and it doesn’t take long for them to understand and get through the process.”

Through several leadership changes, Bui has always defended MIP and said it was the best solution for their organization. Even when someone wonders, “can MIP do that or do we need a different solution?” Bui reassures them that, yes, MIP can do that.

When it comes to accounting solutions for government entities, Bui said MIP is unmatched.

“For government solutions with their accounting and resource needs, MIP should be something to look at, because for us, over the years we’ve grown, we have liked it more and more,” Bui said.

About Momentive Software

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges, engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

The Water Replenishment District is the largest groundwater agency by population in the state of California, managing and protecting local groundwater resources for four million residents. Their service area covers a 420-square-mile region of southern Los Angeles County, the most populated county in the United States. The 43 cities in the service area, including a portion of the City of Los Angeles, and other unincorporated parts of LA County use about 220,000 acre-feet (72 billion gallons) of groundwater annually which accounts for nearly half of the region's water supply needs.

The Water Replenishment District owns three water treatment facilities: two advanced water treatment facilities and a groundwater desalter. Furthermore, WRD leads the robust Regional Groundwater Quality Monitoring Program, a Safe Drinking Water Program, and a Water Education Program.
How a public agency improved compliance with accounting

a:15:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-notepad";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"General Ledger";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-calculator";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:" Accounts Payable";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:22:"box-bx-pie-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:8:"Budget ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:21:"box-bx-money-withdraw";s:13:"feature_title";s:9:"Payroll ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-bank";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:"Bank Reconciliation";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:23:"box-bxs-bar-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"Accounts Receivable Reporting";}s:6:"item-6";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bxs-bookmarks";s:13:"feature_title";s:12:"Fixed Assets";}s:6:"item-7";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-sort-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Data Import Export ";}s:6:"item-8";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-user";s:13:"feature_title";s:30:"Additional Executive View User";}s:6:"item-9";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bxs-palette";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"Forms Designer";}s:7:"item-10";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bx-download";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"Direct Deposit";}s:7:"item-11";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:20:"box-bxs-check-shield";s:13:"feature_title";s:17:"Advanced Security";}s:7:"item-12";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bx-transfer-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:6:"EFT AP";}s:7:"item-13";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bx-dollar";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Grant Administration";}s:7:"item-14";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bx-credit-card";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"SVC Multitenancy";}}

Clinton (S.C.) Newberry Natural Gas Authority was using a custom-built accounting program running IBM’s AS/400 with a SQL database and required programmers to maintain it.

Finance Director Phillip Allison knew it was time to find a modern system that could provide the flexible and reliable reporting they required with modern technology and features to increase efficiency

They began looking for accounting and utility billing software because the solution they were using did not provide enough flexibility in reporting and was not easy to use and manage. It also required additional, often complicated, manual data manipulation to run reports and did not have the ability to accept credit card payments outside of the office.

MIP Accounting and CUSI Utility Billing have over 80 years of combined experience supporting private and local utilities, local governments, and Native nations. MIP streamlines financial and billing operations with user-friendly applications that, when combined, create a truly comprehensive municipal accounting and utility billing solution.

With accounting and utility billing technologies, Clinton Newberry Natural Gas Authority can now:

  • Invoice and receive payments digitally – customers can fully manage their utility accounts and pay invoices online
  • Easily import transactions from billing application to accounting with a click
  • Save time and quickly generate accurate reports in-house
  • Produce specialized and uniquely configured financial reports
  • Manage accounting and reporting across multiple funding sources, programs, and locations
  • Boost reporting and billing efficiency, and scale effectively, through modular accounting and utility billing software
  • Track the nature of restrictions on net assets
  • Document and report on future budget commitments accurately and automatically allocate costs between multiple cost centers, either directly or after-the-fact
  • Monitor transactions between funds
  • Maintain and report on binding budgets, satisfying any government regulation and enforcement

Combining accounting with utility billing in an integrated approach to save time on financial reporting and increase efficiency with billing, Clinton Newberry Natural Gas Authority created a complete solution to help work towards its mission to be the provider of choice in its service territory.

“Reporting is much stronger in both systems by far than what we were getting,” Allison said. “Anytime there was a special report that needed to be run, you had to call the programmers and get them to run the report, create the report. Whereas between [CUSI’s Utility Billing Software] and MIP, there’s not much that you can’t report out.”

Allison and his team also found MIP and CUSI’s support teams to be “outstanding.” “Between the knowledge base in MIP and their support staff, and [CUSI’s SQL-based Utility Software] it has just been fantastic to work with on the tech support side. And the whole thing, really. It’s eased a lot of the pain points.”

Allison would recommend any government organization in a similar situation to switch to MIP and CUSI.

About Momentive Software

Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges, engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

Clinton Newberry Natural Gas Authority has handled the natural gas needs of 13,000 customers in parts of three South Carolina counties since 1952. It’s the organization’s mission to deliver its customers safe, affordable, and reliable natural gas and complimentary services while promoting the communities it serves by ensuring good corporate citizenship through sound business practices, community involvement, stakeholder education, and pipeline safety awareness.

item.title: Ponca Tribe of Nebraska: MIP Accounting Success Story

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: See how the Ponca Tribe of Nebraska improved financial management, streamlined reporting, and increased efficiency with nonprofit accounting software.
MIP’s ability to track expenses, budgets, and taxes has allowed growth of services to skyrocket over the last 4 years.
MIP’s ability to track expenses, budgets, and taxes has allowed growth of services to skyrocket over the last 4 years.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"50%";s:16:"data_description";s:46:"reduction in Accounts Payable processing time ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"15";s:16:"data_description";s:15:"counties served";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:9:"Over 4600";s:16:"data_description";s:32:"individuals receiving services ";}}

The Ponca Tribe of Nebraska has over 202 employees with seven offices across fifteen counties and serves over 4600 individuals annually. 

The Ponca Restoration Act established our fifteen-county Service Delivery Area across Nebraska, Iowa, and South Dakota. We offer a broad range of health, social, educational, and cultural services. The Ponca Tribe of Nebraska is structurally unlike any other Tribe in Nebraska. 

This uniqueness made the Tribe embark upon a vigorous program of educating and lobbying state and federal legislator officials to ensure that its members receive all the benefits and programs that the status as a federally recognized Tribe of Indians implies. The Ponca Tribe of Nebraska is one of the most successful Tribes in Indian Country when it comes to effective lobbying and passage of favorable legislation and administrative policies. 

The Ponca Tribe of Nebraska provides dedicated social and cultural services across Nebraska, Iowa, and South Dakota. As their services to members grew, so did their need for finding a stronger solution for managing the budget, expenses, human resources, fixed assets and payroll and direct deposit. 

The tribe’s accounting team desired a system that would securely and efficiently enable them to track Tribal expenses across all their administrative offices and the ability to change or edit reports directly within a centralized system.

Working with disparate solutions over 20 years, The Ponca Tribe of Nebraska wanted to make sure as well that they could find an accounting solution built for managing different funds and the diverse system of services they were offering, including: domestic violence support, health services, educational services, environmental services, Ponca youth Enrichment fund and social services. 

Managing Tribal Council expenses without accounting software became very difficult requiring back-end work on spreadsheets and integrating separate software solutions. 

Through conversations, MIP was recommended as it was a strong solution being used by other Tribal Nations. The Ponca Tribe of Nebraska had a seasoned longtime staff, some of whom had worked with the tribe for over two decades, so changing existing accounting tools was a challenge. 

In addition, The Ponca Tribe of Nebraska wanted to ensure the solution they picked would have strong tech support and training to ensure the shift to the new solution would be easier on the existing finance team. The Tribe also needed a system that would streamline their Accounts Payable bi-weekly processing time.

With the implementation of MIP’s accounting software, the Ponca Tribe of Nebraska saw a reduction of 50% in the time spent processing bi-weekly Accounts Payable processing time. 

They also were able to reduce the staff managing that task from 2 people to 1 person. 

“MIP’s ability to track expenses, budgets, and taxes has allowed growth of services to skyrocket over the last 4 years,” said Jacob Olsufka, CFO of Ponca Tribe of Nebraska.

About Momentive Software   

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges, engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.   

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

The Ponca Restoration Act established the Ponca Tribe of Nebraska over a fifteen-county Service Delivery Area across Nebraska, Iowa, and South Dakota. The Tribe offer a broad range of health, social, educational, and cultural services to its members.

a:11:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-notepad";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"General Ledger ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-calculator";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:"Accounts Payable ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:22:"box-bx-pie-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:8:"Budget ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-bank";s:13:"feature_title";s:21:"Bank Reconciliation ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bxs-report";s:13:"feature_title";s:31:"Accounts Receivable Reporting ";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-sort-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Data Import Export ";}s:6:"item-6";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-user";s:13:"feature_title";s:32:"Additional Executive View User ";}s:6:"item-7";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bxs-palette";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"Forms Designer ";}s:6:"item-8";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bx-transfer-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:8:"EFT AP ";}s:6:"item-9";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bx-dollar";s:13:"feature_title";s:22:"Grant Administration ";}s:7:"item-10";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:11:"box-bx-euro";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:"SVC Multitenancy ";}}

The Ela Public Library was growing and needed an accounting solution that would grow with them. They also desired a system that would give clearer reporting for department budgets and easier data import and export capabilities. 

Lauren Rosenthal, Executive Director for Ela Area Public Library did an extensive analysis of fund software options, analyzing functionality, ease of use, popularity, availability of call center staff, ease of data migration, hardware requirements, and price including both setup and recurring costs.

Library managers each had to use individual Excel files to keep track of budget spending. Additionally, managers had little to no tracking of spending. The library needed a more robust and long-term system to manage and plan their budgets with efficient and quick importing and exporting capability from Excel sheets.  

Rosenthal had experience with QuickBooks and knew the library needed to invest in an accounting software solution built for nonprofits. With so many budgets, the library finance team also wanted a solution that could provide strong fraud protection and transparency.

The library turned to MIP Accounting to manage its finances.  The budget module implementation for the library has provided more freedom for each department manager and higher transparency overall for audits. 

"The budget module allows each department manager to create a budget worksheet complete with chart of account codes. It allows managers to include detailed data so we’re performing zero-based budgeting," Rosenthal said.   

Using the module worksheets, the library can quickly export from Excel import back into MIP serving the finance a superb long-term solution where worksheets can be easily modified for budgeting year after year. 

Using the module worksheets the library can quickly export from Excel import back into MIP serving the finance a superb long-term solution where worksheets can be easily modified for budgeting year after year. 

"The biggest benefit in MIP versus QuickBooks, which isn’t a true [accounting] system – it's too easy to delete transactions in QuickBooks, we're looking for more security than that," Rosenthal said. 

MIP’s audit trail and session reversing provides complete traceability. MIP’s reporting is robust and versatile, offering summary and detailed reporting for the board and staff. 

“After using the product for seven years I am fully aware of its superiority for processing general ledger and creating reports, as well as integrating the financials with the entire budgeting process so department heads can better understand their individual budgets,” Rosenthal said.

About Momentive Software   

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.   

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.
Ela Area Public Library's mission statement is, "share the joy of discovery." The library circulates over 1,000,000 items yearly and has two state-of-the-art digital media labs on its second floor.
Ela Area Public Library: Accounting Success
How Ela Library streamlined accounting and reporting

item.title: Community Action Partnership of Central Illinois: MIP Accounting Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how Community Action Partnership of Central Illinois improved financial oversight, streamlined reporting, and boosted efficiency with nonprofit fund accounting software.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: See how Community Action Partnership of Central Illinois improved oversight, reporting, and efficiency with fund accounting software.
Community Action Partnership of Central Illinois (CAPCIL)
If something happened to this building or if we had some sort of catastrophic event here, we’d still have access to everything by way of the cloud.
If something happened to this building or if we had some sort of catastrophic event here, we’d still have access to everything by way of the cloud.
The Community Action Partnership of Central Illinois (CAPCIL) serves low-income families and senior citizens of Central Illinois.

CAPCIL’s leaders knew that having their fund accounting software on the organization’s server made them vulnerable to loss in the event of catastrophe. Additionally, access to the system was limited, making it difficult and inconvenient for everyone across the partnership to enter and retrieve information.

CAPCIL moved its MIP Accounting software off its server and into the cloud. Having MIP Cloud means information can be accessed from anywhere at any time, and operations can continue in the event of an emergency or natural disaster.

“Prior to moving to the cloud, we could remotely log into our desktop computers, but it was inconvenient and clunky,” said Alison Rumler-Gomez, Executive Director of CAPCIL. “Often when our CFO was away, things would basically come to a halt. Now, if I have a question and the CFO is at a training event in Washington, I can phone her and say, ‘Hey, can you get into the system? I need to get an employee report on Amanda.’ Just having that access has been fantastic. We are big fans.”

According to CAPCIL Executive Director Alison Rumler-Gomez, the nonprofit’s subscription to the MIP Cloud is fundamental to CAPCIL’s success. CAPCIL benefits from ironclad security, ensuring the partnership’s data is safe if and when disaster hits. Additionally, it provides everyone in the organization anytime/anywhere access.

“We addressed business continuity in our risk management plan, and realized that just having software loaded onto our server locally made us vulnerable,” Rumler-Gomez said. “Moving to the cloud subscription was really integral to us. If something happened to this building or if we had some sort of catastrophic event here, we’d still have access to everything by way of the cloud.”

About Momentive Software

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges, engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

Community Action Partnership of Central Illinois has served low-income families and senior citizens of Central Illinois since 1966. The nonprofit organization administers federal, state, and local programs addressing both the immediate and long-term needs of its clients, working tirelessly to serve and support vulnerable populations. CAPCIL receives nearly $8.5 million toward this mission, providing services in six different counties through 11 Head Start Program sites and five outreach offices, allowing the nonprofit to serve nearly 24,000 individuals annually, and employ more than 140 Central Illinois citizens.
How CAP Central IL improved oversight with fund accounting

Sheila Long, CFO of Neosho, Missouri based ACCESS Family Care helms a 20-million-dollar budget for clinics covering five counties in Missouri. These clinics serve over 24,000 patients including uninsured and under insured with over 59,000 visits for medical, dental and behavioral health services as well as pharmacy services. ACCESS Family Care has grown its employee base to 230 employees. 

“We are a Community Health Center, and I was probably the typical patient we serve as a child as I grew up poor. I have a passion for what I do because I’ve been there. In the last [several] years, my work here is to ensure financial stability for our organization and to expand our services to those in need,” states Long. 

Under Sheila’s tenure with the support of MIP Accounting solution, Long has seen a $4 million budget grow to over $20 million.

When Sheila started at ACCESS Family Care they were using Sage Mas 90 for their solution. Additionally, the prior CFO was managing the accounting records on a cash basis and they used an outside accountant to do straight line depreciation for the fixed assets for the accounting records. [At the beginning of] her tenure, Long realized as oversight of funding and grant funding was increasing, they had outgrown the capabilities of their software. 

“We had to account and track everything we were doing in multiple spreadsheets. We were outgrowing our software. Mas 90 was not user friendly. We were not able to track reports. With MIP we set up the Chart of Accounts to track the grants and MIP has helped us have expedient tracking” said Long. 

ACCESS Family Care also needed flexibility and range in creating original codes for CHC funding grants, quality awards, and many other grants.

Successfully using MIP since May 2012, the healthcare system has added four new dental clinics, two new medical clinics and a mobile health unit. The organization has seen a marketable reduction in the time needed for producing reports and are able to manage nine different segments in their chart of accounts. ACCESS Family Care has been able to track every grant and follow every dollar for robust reporting. 

“MIP enables us the flexibility with all our codes. CHC funding grants, quality awards and many other grants can be accounted for in MIP. ... These resources in MIP have allowed us to do more with the same number of staff.” 

With the power of flexible coding, speed of reporting, and continued focus on expansion, ACCESS Family Care has a comprehensive fund accounting system that will continue to grow with them. The community health center also relies on fraud mitigation tools to reduce fraud or privacy issues.

About Momentive Software 

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.
ACCESS Family Care Medical & Dental Clinics provide southwest Missouri residents with high quality primary medical and general dentistry care.

The American Academy of Neurology (AAN) had outgrown its legacy LMS. Years of over-customization made the platform difficult to maintain, with only a few staff members able to navigate its complexity. Software updates were slow or incomplete, leading to inefficiencies in content creation and delivery, ultimately impacting both staff productivity and learner experience.

With Path LMS, AAN transformed its content operations by seamlessly managing over 1,400 annual conference presentations, organizing them into 400+ courses using bulk import tools, and delivering 350+ CME-eligible programs. The team also gained efficiency through advanced reporting and saved time with intelligent course duplication and reusable templates.

“The accessibility of support at Path LMS has been a positive. You’re not just out on your own island where you’re figuring things out and your vendor just goes into a void where you rarely hear back.” — Elizabeth Bradshaw, AAN Associate Director, Digital Content

The AAN is the world’s largest association of neurologists and neuroscience professionals, serving over 40,000 members globally. It provides continuing education, research grants, advocacy, and essential tools to advance brain health
American Academy of Neurology: LMS Case Study

a:12:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-notepad";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"General Ledger";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-calculator";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:" Accounts Payable";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:22:"box-bx-pie-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:8:"Budget ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-bank";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:"Bank Reconciliation";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:23:"box-bxs-bar-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"Accounts Receivable Reporting";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:21:"box-bx-calendar-event";s:13:"feature_title";s:11:"Scheduler ";}s:6:"item-6";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-sort-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Data Import Export ";}s:6:"item-7";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-user";s:13:"feature_title";s:21:"Executive View User ";}s:6:"item-8";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bxs-palette";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"Forms Designer";}s:6:"item-9";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:20:"box-bxs-check-shield";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:"Advanced Security ";}s:7:"item-10";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bx-transfer-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:6:"EFT AP";}s:7:"item-11";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bx-dollar";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Grant Administration";}}

The year is 2011. The University System of Georgia (USG) Foundation was hard at work creating a more educated Georgia through providing need-based financial aid and overseeing the facilitation of financing for capital improvement projects for Georgia’s 26 public higher education institutions.

Yet, the USG Foundation had a challenge. It was using QuickBooks to manage its finances and had reached the point where it needed a more robust system. It needed a system that embraced flexibility and one that would grow with the Foundation as it progressed.

USG Foundation Director of Finance and Operations Christina Paloski, CPA, was a consultant for MIP Accounting® back in 2011. She was brought in to showcase how MIP could be that system.

“They didn’t have all the flexibility they needed with QuickBooks,” Paloski said. “They needed a way more robust system.”

Paloski approached the USG Foundation with a mindset to share how MIP would let them look ahead and grow.

“It’s easy to use,” Paloski said. “This system is straightforward…I felt like there were way more opportunities to manage finances the way they needed in MIP. I was able to show that to them and show them the flexibility of MIP.”

The USG Foundation was so impressed they not only partnered with MIP, but they brought Paloski in to serve as a consultant with their team. After a year, they hired her full-time and Paloski now has been with the USG Foundation for over nine years.

When it comes to ease use and the ability to generate quick reports, “I know how to make MIP work, and I knew how to do everything,” Paloski said. “I’m able to slice and dice the data any way that I need it in order to answer reports, answer questions, and provide information for management to make decisions.”

It’s challenging to run a foundation that oversees public scholarships and capital improvement at such an array of universities. From traditional cash donations to creative ways people support specific scholarship programs, Paloski knew MIP could account for the multiple donation sources, and she was confident it could support the foundation 10 years down the road as those assets had aged and grown.

MIP could handle the complexity of USG Foundation’s accounting needs because MIP accounts for every fund individually, and its reporting and budgeting features allow nonprofits to project how those funds will grow and look years down the road. The USG Foundation oversees several types of needs-based scholarships, and accounting for each of those individual funds was where Paloski knew MIP would shine when she introduced it to the foundation, and where it’s made a lasting mark almost a decade later.

“We have new challenges every day with the ways people are giving, and the things people are giving,” She said. “We’ve had to come up with ways to pull the information out, and as we continue, I’m very happy with it. MIP continues to meet our needs, and I know we can grow with it.”

The USG Foundation has grown substantially and seen the first-hand effects of how its scholarships have made a difference. Over the last three years, they awarded more than $1 million in scholarships alone to help advance the educations of more than 330,000 students in Georgia.

And when it comes to accounting, Paloski knows that impact wouldn’t have been possible without MIP.

“I really enjoy MIP,” she said. “It makes my life easier. I'm not having to manipulate data in order to get information to present. I truly can pull financials out of the system and then make pretty charts out of them for my board. It's always meeting my needs.”

About Momentive Software

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges, engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

The USG Foundation is a philanthropic foundation that raises and manages donations to support their mission and goals. As the cooperative partner of the USG and a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, it is managed by a separate governing board of trustees. The Foundation is dedicated to raising funds for needs-based scholarships, overseeing financing for capital improvement projects at USG institutions, and providing financial management services for UG programs and private grants held by the foundation.
Georgia Foundation | MIP Accounting Case Study
How USG Foundation improved reporting with accounting

item.title: Area Agency on Aging of Southwest Arkansas: MIP Accounting Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: From typewriter to on-premise, and now to the cloud, as the Area Agency on Aging of Southwest Arkansas works tirelessly for the state’s senior citizens, MIP Accounting is accounting for their future.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover how the Area Agency on Aging of Southwest Arkansas moved from paper-based accounting to MIP for secure grant tracking and reporting.
Area Agency on Aging of Southwest Arkansas (AAASWA)

Being a nonprofit that is federally and state funded, we must have a secure program that can track each grant separately. MIP was recommended by our auditors and several other area agencies use it as well.

Being a nonprofit that is federally and state funded, we must have a secure program that can track each grant separately. MIP was recommended by our auditors and several other area agencies use it as well.

a:11:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-notepad";s:13:"feature_title";s:16:"General Ledger ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-calculator";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:"Accounts Payable ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:21:"box-bx-money-withdraw";s:13:"feature_title";s:10:" Payroll ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bxs-bank";s:13:"feature_title";s:21:"Bank Reconciliation ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:23:"box-bxs-bar-chart-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:31:"Accounts Receivable Reporting ";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-sort-alt-2";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:"Data Import Export";}s:6:"item-6";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bx-download";s:13:"feature_title";s:17:" Direct Deposit ";}s:6:"item-7";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bx-dollar";s:13:"feature_title";s:22:"Grant Administration ";}s:6:"item-8";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bx-credit-card";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:" SVC Multitenancy ";}s:6:"item-9";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:11:"box-bxs-cog";s:13:"feature_title";s:8:"RestAPI ";}s:7:"item-10";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bxs-group";s:13:"feature_title";s:15:"HR Management ";}}

“The auditors came in, and they said, ‘Oh no.’”

It was not an auspicious start when CFO Cindy Lewis joined the Area Agency on Aging of Southwest Arkansas (AAASWA) as a bookkeeper in June 2009. A new auditor came to examine their finances, and the organization’s fund accounting system was underequipped. Lewis and her team were even writing out some checks on a typewriter.

“I don’t remember the name of [our old fund accounting program] because it causes nightmares,” Lewis said. “It was a program that was not user-friendly. You’d input the information into the computer and then we typed checks on a typewriter.”

AAASWA had just changed auditors at the time, and when the new auditors arrived, they said, “Oh no.” Lewis knew AAASWA needed a change. Four of the other area agencies were already using MIP Accounting®, and they suggested AAASWA do the same.

“Being a nonprofit that is federally and state funded, we must have a secure program that can track each grant separately,” Lewis said. “MIP was recommended by our auditors and several other area agencies use it as well.”

The board approved it, and their MIP implementation was underway.

AAASWA began using MIP in March 2010 using MIP as its accounting solution. They serve 12 counties in Southwestern Arkansas, overseeing 24 senior centers and serving about 5,000 individuals monthly.

From providing in-home meals to seniors or to providing them in a community setting, to hosting social events, to in home personal care, case management, legal services and providing transportation to and from senior centers, the AAASWA exists to help senior citizens retain their independence and to assist them with the problems that arise.

When it comes to AAASWA’s finances, MIP helps to manage the variety of funds that are managed and administered, ensuring essential operations like payroll are done correctly and on time. The end result? Accurate reports that demonstrate their agency’s impact to their board and external stakeholders.

“With EWS, employees now log their hours in the system and supervisors approve them online. There’s no manual entry required, and the data is automatically shared with MIP,” says Terry. “EWS saves the Payroll team a day and a half worth of time every single pay period. EWS allows mileage reimbursements to be entered as well, to be paid through payroll.”

The HR modules have also helped CFHC streamline the management of work scheduling, giving supervisors visibility into clinic-by-clinic coverage, and benefit elections and tracking. In addition, the team has integrated Aatrix, which automatically pulls data from MIP for e-filing tax forms, including W2s and 1099s.

Lewis couldn’t imagine what life without MIP, or its support services could be.

“We have Gold Support, so we have a dedicated support person,” Lewis said. “Whenever something comes up, we’ll say, ‘Well, we have to call [oursupport person].’”

AAASWA manages several funds and sends regular reports to the Arkansas Department of Human Services in Little Rock, with MIP managing their funds and creating those reports is effortless.

“I love being able to go in and pull a report,” Lewis said. “If we need to see how much we’ve spent with these different funds, I’ve set them all up as programs, and because each fund is different, we have to report them [differently.] I like being able to customize the reports. If I just need to see how much we’ve spent in Title III C1 for this amount of time, I like being able to pull that up without needing several different reports.”

In the midst of the pandemic, Lewis decided to migrate to MIP Cloud.

“We started looking at the cloud, and saying, ‘You know, we may really need to look at this as an option,” Lewis said, and AAASWA did. In early March 2022, they officially signed on to migrate to MIP Cloud. Having the ability to access their data anywhere securely was one of the biggest reasons AAASWA moved to a cloud environment.

From typewriter to on-premise, and now to the cloud, as AAASWA works tirelessly for the state’s senior citizens, MIP is accounting for their future.

About Momentive Software

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

The Area Agency on Aging of Southwest Arkansas serves as the visible regional focal point for older adults and persons with disabilities for advocacy for public policy, which effectively addresses their needs, the dissemination of pertinent information, planning, development, funding, implementation, operation, technical assistance and evaluation of a service system which is responsive to individual needs and assists service recipients to live at their maximum levels of independence in the least restrictive environment.
Aging Agency of Southwest Arkansas: Accounting Success
How an Area Agency modernized accounting with MIP

CFHC has used MIP Accounting software since 2010 to help with the complexities of managing multiple grants and complying with the reporting idiosyncrasies of diverse grantors.

In addition, three years into using MIP, CFHC needed help to manage its finances and subsequently implemented several MIP modules – Human Resource Management, Payroll, and Employee Web Services (EWS) modules.

  • With MIP Cloud, CFHC can customize segments across multiple programs and fiscal years.
  • With the EWS module, the Payroll team now saves a day and a half each pay period.
  • HR modules have helped CFHC streamline their processes and automate seamlessly.

The finance team at Carolina Family Health Centers, Inc. manages revenue from programs and services, as well as a dozen different public and private grantors – each with its own stipulations regarding how the money is spent and reported. Its HR team manages nearly 200 staff members at four centers.

With MIP, we can create segments to allocate and track grant money across multiple programs and fiscal years,” explains Kimberly Terry, CFHC Director of Finance. “You have to understand the full capabilities of what MIP can do to get the most benefit out of it. Because, when you set up the system to do exactly what you need it to do – for example, categorize programs and funding sources using segments – it makes tracking and reporting much easier in the long run. It’s all about the input.”

Terry says MIP also helps CFHC with its segregation of duties. “We give role-based access to MIP to different individuals in the finance department and across the organization,” she explains.

“So, for example, one person in finance has access to enter accounts receivables, while another checks the data entry to ensure it was done accurately. Then, the data is posted by the Director of Finance after review. Nobody’s perfect – so any time you have humans inputting data, there’s opportunity for error. But this way, 99 percent of the time, we’ll catch any errors immediately.”

And with countless stakeholders – from internal program directors to the board, to state and federal grantors – financial reporting can be time-consuming and complex.

“We generate and send reports monthly, quarterly, annually, depending on the stakeholder,” says Terry. MIP enables the team to customize reports to reflect what each stakeholder needs, when they need it. “For example, using DrillPoint Reports,” explains Terry, “I can generate profit and loss statements by department, by location, and against budget. I can get data out of MIP in multiple ways for multiple reports to satisfy multiple audiences.”

Additionally, CFHC has transformed how it handles HR and payroll. HR Modules streamline work and eliminate costly duplicate entry.

“Before we implemented the HR modules, we were having to enter payroll information into MIP and then enter it again into our payroll system,” explains Terry. “EWS was a game changer for us. Because before, all employees had to swipe their personal card at a time clock to log their hours. Then, we had to print out the time clock logs, manually calculate hours worked, then get each employee and his or her manager to sign off on the calculations. We were doing this for 140 employees.”

“With EWS, employees now log their hours in the system and supervisors approve them online. There’s no manual entry required, and the data is automatically shared with MIP,” says Terry. “EWS saves the Payroll team a day and a half worth of time every single pay period. EWS allows mileage reimbursements to be entered as well, to be paid through payroll.”

The HR modules have also helped CFHC streamline the management of work scheduling, giving supervisors visibility into clinic-by-clinic coverage, and benefit elections and tracking.

In addition, the team has integrated Aatrix, which automatically pulls data from MIP for e-filing tax forms, including W2s and 1099s.

About Momentive Software

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

Carolina Family Health Centers, Inc. (CFHC) provides affordable, high-quality health care services in Edgecombe, Nash, and Wilson counties in Eastern North Carolina. Through four centers, it offers a comprehensive scope of medical, dental, pharmaceutical, and behavioral health services to thousands of uninsured, the working poor, and those who are unemployed or underemployed.

The Town of Rising Sun's goal was to scale and sustain their financial future for business and residents. In only 18 months from researching an accounting software program to implementing MIP's accounting solution they achieved just this.

Judy Melton, Office Manager and Town Clerk for Town of Rising Sun, Maryland, shared her insights and challenges with the town's journey from manual workarounds, decentralized systems, and staffing constraints to seamless integration of all critical financials and databases.

After a decade of outsourcing all city accounting processes, the Town of Rising Sun's Judy Melton was determined to find a system that internal staff could implement. The city needed a versatile, easy to learn and implement, well-supported accounting system.

Town of Rising Sun was no longer challenged with managing core and critical areas of accounting and administration, gaining proper functionality and flexibility with the right accounting system.

With MIP, the town was able to empower reporting efficiency, reduce reliance on outside accounting professionals and have a cloud-based system that will serve them in the future. Since implementing, they gained greater reporting flexibility, superb data integration and key budget reconciliations. They also enjoy consistent and solid support and ongoing MIP customer support for module education.

“We had limited resources in our city,” said Judy Melton, town clerk. “Towns have limited budgets, so we had to start with the intention of finding a solution that would grow with us for the long-term. Working in MIP is so easy, every module performs the same way, master one and you'll be able to solve more problems!”

In 1720, Henry Reynolds established a stone tavern on Nottingham Lot No. 17 to serve as a stage stop. Over the entrance was a swinging sign depicting the rays of the sun at dawn and the lettering THE RISING SUN. It was around this busy tavern that the village of Summer Hill began to grow.

The Town of Rising Sun was incorporated in 1860 and currently is home to over 3000 close-knit residents.

item.title: Community Action Agency of Butte County, Inc.: MIP Accounting Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: See how the Community Action Agency of Butte County Inc streamlined financial processes, improved reporting, and gained efficiency with nonprofit accounting software.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Community Action Agency of Butte County streamlined finances, improved reporting, and gained efficiency with accounting software.
Community Action Agency of Butte County, Inc. (CAABCI)

A main advantage of MIP is we’re able to adapt to each funding stream’s unique conditions. This is vital because funding sources typically have diverse requirements, and even interpret regulations differently, sometimes within the same federal department.

A main advantage of MIP is we’re able to adapt to each funding stream’s unique conditions. This is vital because funding sources typically have diverse requirements, and even interpret regulations differently, sometimes within the same federal department.
CFO, Community Action Agency of Butte County, Inc.

Community Action Agency of Butte County, Inc. operates various projects and programs funded by multiple and widely varying sources, making the accounting piece of the business extremely complex.

It’s an environment in which small business accounting software, such as QuickBooks, simply wasn't enough.

“In our environment a QuickBooks is just too flexible,” Dana Campbell, CFO, said. “We could easily do things with our funds and grants that are flat-out wrong and never know it, which could get us into a lot of trouble.”

CAABCI operates four major programs. Its $7.5 million budget is funded by nearly 30 different sources, including grants from federal, state, and local government entities, as well as foundations, corporations, contracts, fees for services, and donations. It was looking for a system that could simplify its finances and track its varied unique funds.

The agency turned to MIP Accounting to manage its finances.

Campbell said, MIP contains actual accounting controls, so you can’t make mistakes. Yet, it’s flexible enough to adapt to what’s needed in any given environment.

“A main advantage of MIP is we’re able to adapt to each funding stream’s unique conditions,” Campbell said.” This is vital because funding sources typically have diverse requirements, and even interpret regulations differently, sometimes within the same federal department. For example, one grantor may define ‘occupancy cost’ as rent, while another defines it as rent, plus telephone and Internet services, plus taxes and insurance. MIP allows us to code each one differently in response.”

“And, we can adapt the coding to track very specifically when necessary-all the way down to the pencil we’re using,” Campbell said. “For example, the utility company that helps fund our weatherization program recently requested that we track what percentage of insurance costs are allocated to different aspects of the program, including administration, customer enrollment, and field activity. We can code this into the system and easily capture the various allocations going forward.”

Campbell pointed to MIP's flexibility as the biggest benefit the agency gleaned from MIP. CAABCI can adapt to exactly what’s needed in any given environment, whether it’s adding a new program or writing new code in response to ever-changing funding source demands.

“A main advantage of MIP is we’re able to adapt to each funding stream’s unique conditions. This is vital because funding sources typically have diverse requirements, and even interpret regulations differently, sometimes within the same federal department.”

About Momentive Software

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

Community Action Agency of Butte County, Inc., seeks to improve conditions of low-income constituents through a variety of approaches, including community economic development, human service delivery, advocacy, micro enterprise, and affordable housing.

After the Pandemic and staff turnover, LeadingAge PA worked with the Momentive team to further utilize the platform, empowering the organization to achieve its goals and maximize its investment.
