item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: For more than a decade, the South Carolina Nurses Association (SCNA) has used YourMembership as their association management software (AMS). With only a two-person staff, the organization serves more than 2,000 members in a critically important industry for the economy – nursing.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: South Carolina Nurses Association Case Study
See how the South Carolina Nurses Association improved member engagement and operations with AMS. Read the case study for real results and insights.

YourMembership AMS makes it possible for our two-person staff to do everything we need to do to manage our professional organization. We wouldn’t be nearly as efficient working with multiple products from different companies.” Rosie Robinson, Executive Assistant SCNA

YourMembership makes it possible for our small staff to do more. We use it for almost everything, and I’ve never had a customer service complaint. Also, the product education that Momentive provides their clients is outstanding, which makes it possible for me to understand and use the product more effectively.

a:6:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bxs-book-reader";s:13:"feature_title";s:22:"Membership management ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:22:"box-bxs-calendar-event";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:"Event registration ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:12:"box-bx-money";s:13:"feature_title";s:25:"Invoicing & payments ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:16:"box-bx-mail-send";s:13:"feature_title";s:21:"Email communications ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:16:"box-bxs-edit-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"Continuing education tracking";}s:6:"item-5";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:14:"box-bx-command";s:13:"feature_title";s:18:"Online communities";}}

For more than a decade, the South Carolina Nurses Association (SCNA) has used YourMembership as their Association Management Software (AMS). With only a two-person staff, the organization serves more than 2,000 members in a critically important industry for the economy – nursing. SCNA’s executive assistant, Rosie Robinson, has worked at the organization since 2006 and serves in multiple roles, including membership and marketing director, meeting planner, and webmaster. She credits YourMembership AMS for enabling the small staff to work more efficiently and effectively, ultimately providing a professional, well-run organization for the nursing industry.

With only two staffers, SCNA runs a professional organization for the nursing industry with 2,000+ members who must maintain continuing education requirements. The staff needs technology to help them work efficiently while delivering a great member experience.  

The organization uses YourMembership AMS for everything from online event registration and payment processing to email marketing, providing an online member community, and tracking/issuing continuing education certifications for members.

The small team quickly and efficiently handles processes, including tracking/ issuing members’ continuing education credits, and members have a convenient way to look up their individual continuing education history online, anytime.
The mission of the South Carolina Nurses Association is to represent all South Carolina registered nurses in a membership organization to promote and advocate for the role of nurses.
How a small-staff association scaled with YourMembership

Working with YourMembership allows us to focus and expand our efforts to help drive deeper member engagement and increase member retention and growth.
Vice President of Communications & Technology, Ewald Consulting
Ewald Consulting migrated the entire client list to a single membership management software, YourMembership, enabling cross-training of staff on a single solution.

After switching to YourMembership, Ewald more than doubled its business, expanding from 20 associations to 47, without increasing its internal staff. item.title: Catholic Health Association of the United States (CHA): Aptify Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: CHA implemented Aptify as its single enterprise system and has been a customer ever since.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: CHA uses Aptify to free up internal IT staff time for mission-critical work
Aptify AMS meets CHA requirements while offering IT teams flexibility to configure the platform for their specific needs.
Catholic Health Association of the United States (CHA)

As leaders of the nation’s largest group of nonprofit healthcare providers, with more than 45,000 records in its membership database, Catholic Health Association of the United States (CHA) needed a reliable association management system (AMS) that was flexible enough to keep up with members’ ever-changing needs, wants, and priorities. 

The primary goal was to track information more accurately and in real time so it could be used to make informed business decisions.

Operationally, CHA needed a system that could manage the following data and functions: 

  • Association records  
  • Member and association finances  
  • Committees  
  • Subscriptions  
  • E-commerce, including shipping and mailing  
  • Contact management  
  • Email integration   
  • Meeting and event management  
  • Member sign-up, profiles, and education 

CHA needed an AMS that met its functional requirements, was flexible enough to allow CHA’s in-house IT team to easily configure the solution to the organization’s specific needs, and also offered a hosted solution so CHA’s IT team did not have to support hardware, databases, and applications.  

CHA chose Aptify’s AMS with Aptify Cloud Hosting for a reliable and flexible system that also includes access to a team of seasoned database administrators and infrastructure engineers trained in the Aptify solution.

CHA implemented Aptify as its single enterprise system and has been a customer ever since. Not only does Aptify AMS meet CHA’s requirements, but its flexible platform also enables CHA’s IT team to easily configure the AMS to the organization’s specific needs. CHA is also an Aptify Cloud client, giving CHA all the benefits of Aptify without the burden of managing the platform and system availability. 

CHA chose Aptify for the significant flexibility of its AMS, which is made possible by Aptify’s highly configurable platform. CHA has a configurable, flexible membership management platform that frees its IT team from managing the back end of the system, allowing the team to focus on the end-user experience and develop unique configurations to better serve CHA’s members.

Founded in 1915, CHA supports the Catholic Health Ministry’s commitment to improve the health of communities and create quality and compassionate health care that works for everyone. The Catholic Health Ministry is the nation’s largest group of not-for-profit healthcare providers, employing more than 750,000 women and men who provide healthcare services that combine advanced technology with the Catholic caring tradition.
How CHA used Aptify for flexible association management
CHA Frees IT Resources Using Aptify AMS

item.title: The Air Line Pilots Association (ALPA): Aptify Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com

ALPA uses Aptify AMS for community management, member participation tracking, targeted marketing, process automation, and reporting.
I would highly recommend Aptify to other unions. The flexibility of the platform to effectively perform the features and functions that a union needs has been just wonderful.

We really needed a more flexible system in-house. Something that our end users could use more readily and didn’t have to rely on IT to do most of the support with, and so we were really looking for a product for our team to be able to utilize.

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:27:"community management tools ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:30:"member participation tracking ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:"targeted marketing ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:19:"process automation ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"easy-to-use reports ";}}

The Air Line Pilots Association (ALPA), the world’s largest airline pilot union, recognized the need to meet modern demands by abandoning the organization’s traditional enterprise resource planning (ERP) system and moving to Aptify, a membership management software solution. In partnership with Momentive, ALPA has taken a significant step forward by implementing Aptify, providing members with easier access to membership benefits, facilitating participation in union-sponsored programs, and simplifying staff access to essential data.

ALPA was using a traditional ERP system with limited membership management functionality. The organization sought a more flexible system with enhanced membership capabilities that staff members could efficiently utilize without relying on ALPA’s IT team.

ALPA has enhanced member engagement and now offers members convenient self-service tools. The organization has also achieved significant efficiencies through the automation of billing and payment processes, streamlined processes for sponsored insurance products, and improved access to data.

ALPA represents and advocates for more than 66,000 pilots at 40 U.S. and Canadian airlines, making it the world’s largest airline pilot union. ALPA provides three critical services to its members: airline safety, security, and pilot assistance; representation; and advocacy. Through unbiased, fact-based evaluation of airline safety and security issues, ALPA works to ensure that the airline industry remains safe. ALPA represents the views of pilots to decision-makers, including Congress and federal agencies, and ALPA pilot groups have negotiated hundreds of contracts with airlines. ALPA is a member of the AFL-CIO and the Canadian Labour Congress.
How ALPA streamlined operations with Aptify AMS
ALPA Builds Modern Union Solutions with Momentive

item.title: Society of American Military Engineers (SAME): Nimble AMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: SAME needed an innovative AMS to enhance its reporting functionality, provide extensive chapter management features, and offer advanced technology solutions that could grow with the organization.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: SAME Nimble AMS Case Study: Association Results
The Society of American Military Engineers connects government officials, private sector individuals, and organizations for networking.

One of the things I love [about Nimble AMS] is nobody ever says ‘you can’t do that.’ With the help of Momentive staff, Salesforce, the support team, and Nimble AMS resources such as NimbleLand, and documentation, if you want to make a change on your own, you can. Nimble AMS gives you the tools, and you can create anything.

It’s a breath of fresh air to know the Momentive team cares about our association and is willing to spend hours supporting our issues. I submitted a case yesterday, but I didn’t expect a response until the next day. Then I got an email half an hour later resolving our issue. It’s just refreshing to know that there is someone responsive on the other end. It’s a real benefit.
Associate Director of Operations, Society of American Military Engineers (SAME)

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:84:"Intuitive and user-friendly reports and lists empower staff to become data-driven. ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:108:"Extensive chapter and committee management simplifies reporting processes and increases member engagement. ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:122:"Improved flow and ease of payment processes with Nimble AMS allow for user-friendly and efficient membership management. ";}}

Since 1920, the Society of American Military Engineers (SAME) has functioned as the networking bridge between government officials, private sector individuals, and organizations. SAME serves 27,000 individual members who work for organizations like the Army Corps of Engineers, the Navy Civil Engineer Corps, and the Air Force Civil Engineer Center, as well as 1,500 company members in the national defense and military construction industry. The organization offers annual conferences, events, and programs, as well as educational webinars, professional development opportunities, and STEM engineering and construction camps. Chapters are also a vital aspect of SAME, with the organization hosting 99 chapters both nationally and internationally.

In 2018, SAME decided it had outgrown its legacy system. Using its old software, SAME staff had limited access to reporting, which was complicated and restrictive. Only the administrator could modify and pull most reports and had to know SQL to code and build reports. Chapter management, a crucial aspect of the organization, was another pain point for SAME. The legacy database was unable to report on chapter leaders holding multiple positions, nor could the chapter leaders update their chapter rosters or communicate efficiently with their chapter members, which reduced chapter leader self-sufficiency and created barriers to their success.  

Because SAME staff faced multiple product limitations with its legacy software, the association hired a consultant to guide them through the current digital landscape. Starting with six AMS vendors, SAME narrowed the list to three: one Salesforce-based AMS (Nimble AMS), one Microsoft-based AMS, and one proprietary AMS. In the end, Nimble AMS prevailed due to its innovative solutions for associations, particularly in reporting, chapter, and committee management.  

SAME looks to build bridges with its members and key industry stakeholders and chose Nimble AMS because it’s geared towards collaboration. For example, with Nimble AMS, SAME employees can simplify their chapter management by leveraging reports, assigning chapter roles, creating portals, and empowering members as chapter leaders.

Since SAME has implemented Nimble AMS, they’ve observed a major technological leap. With SAME’s former legacy software, only the administrator could access and update reports, so some were impossible to complete, while others would take an entire day to resolve. Now with Nimble AMS, reports are quick, intuitive, and user-friendly, empowering all employees to use the feature.

Founded in 1920, The Society of American Military Engineers (SAME) builds leaders and leads collaboration among government and industry to develop multidisciplined solutions to national security infrastructure challenges. SAME unites public and private sector individuals and organizations across architecture, engineering, construction, environmental, and facility management, as well as cybersecurity, project planning, contracting, and acquisition, and related disciplines, supporting national security.
SAME connects professionals for networking and growth.

item.title: International Health, Racquet & Sportsclub Association (IHRSA): Nimble AMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: IHRSA chose Nimble AMS in part because they appreciated the innovative and customer-focused culture of the Momentive team, as well as the modern association software built on Salesforce. The organization can now easily make improvements for staff and members quickly and efficiently.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: From outdated software to digital transformation with Nimble AMS
IHRSA created an online member forum using Nimble AMS in response to COVID-19, reaching 1,100 users in weeks.

Momentive’s Nimble AMS has set us up for innovation and success. We’re able to do so much now without spending a lot of extra time or money. When we want to do something new or make an improvement, we can just do it, leveraging the investment in technology we’ve already made.
We would not be functional at IHRSA today during the COVID-19 pandemic had we not replaced our legacy AMS with Nimble AMS. We are able to stay connected to our team and our members like never before.

For years, IHRSA had used the same version of their old association management software (AMS). They hesitated to upgrade the system because the minor improvements available did not outweigh the difficulty of the upgrade process. They wanted more than just incremental updates. They wanted an AMS that would help them grow their association and move them forward.

IHRSA knew their AMS was outdated, but they did not want to go through the hassle of upgrading their old AMS for the small improvements the new version offered. If they were going to make the move to a new AMS, they wanted a system that would help them do more. 

IHRSA moved from an outdated legacy AMS system to Nimble AMS. The organization now has a solution that enables them to innovate and grow in numerous ways without incurring additional costs and hassle.

Nimble AMS is a modern, easy-to-use, Salesforce-based platform that allows the IHRSA team to put new online processes and member offerings in place quickly and efficiently. The team works just as well from home as it does in the office and they always have the latest technology, without extra costs or hassles. 

According to Thomas Durkin, Vice President of Technology and Digital Publishing at IHRSA, the organization can now make improvements for staff and members quickly and efficiently. For example, in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, IHRSA wanted to provide members with an online forum to share ideas. Using the Nimble AMS online help site, one person on Durkin’s team was able to set up a member community within days. A few weeks later, the forum already had 1,100 users. 

 

Another example: The organization had been regularly generating a critical report that was only available on one computer. One person spent about five hours every couple of days to run the report. Today, the entire IHRSA team can see key insights from the report any time through a dashboard in Nimble AMS.

The mission of IHRSA is to grow, protect, and promote the health and fitness industry, and to provide its members with the benefits that will help them achieve greater success. IHRSA and its members are committed to promoting global health through regular physical activity.
How IHRSA built a thriving online community with Nimble
Digital Transformation Success with Nimble AMS

item.title: North American Association of Central Cancer Registries (NAACCR): Nimble AMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: NAACCR’s legacy association management system didn’t receive routine updates, making it a challenge for both staff and members to use, and requiring developers to be consulted to complete simple tasks. The organization needed an AMS that could easily scale with the organization.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Momentive provides NAACCR with a one-stop portal that reduces support cases and empowers members to manage their journey.
North American Association of Central Cancer Registries (NAACCR)
Nimble AMS just fit with what NAACCR needed in terms of scalability. There was room to grow, but it was also right for an organization of our size. Nimble AMS can also help us grow in the future.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"100+";s:16:"data_description";s:141:"sponsoring, sustaining, and full members representing population-based central cancer registries and other cancer surveillance organizations ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"14";s:16:"data_description";s:13:"staff members";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:0:"";}}

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:102:"Intuitive and user-friendly reports and lists empower staff to become data-driven and self-sufficient.";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:111:"Personalized, one-stop member portal that reduces support cases and empowers members to own their own journey. ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:103:"Continuous, seamless upgrades for constant innovation, driving a superior staff and member experience. ";}}

Since 1987, the North American Association of Central Cancer Registries (NAACCR) has been working to improve the quality and utilization of cancer registry data in the United States and Canada. The professional organization maintains data standards for cancer registration while promoting the use of cancer surveillance data for epidemiologic research. NAACCR also offers members extensive education and training opportunities during its annual conference, online webinars, NAACCR Talks, and lectures.  

NAACCR’s legacy association management system didn’t receive routine updates, making it a challenge for both staff and members to use and requiring developers to be consulted to complete simple tasks. The organization needed an AMS that could easily scale with the organization—NAACCR turned to Nimble AMS.

NAACR required a system with robust integration capabilities for complex third-party systems, including eight cancer surveillance tools. However, the organization’s legacy system offered limited innovative solutions, restricted research and development investment, and an inconsistent release schedule.  

Ultimately, the legacy system's lack of continuous upgrades resulted in a negative staff and member experience. Members were unable to navigate their own journey in the member portal, and completing simple tasks, such as password updates or resets, required a considerable amount of staff time. Additionally, generating reports was complex, requiring staff to request that developers run both basic and custom reports. 

When it was time to switch software, NAACCR knew it needed a solution that could scale and was easy to use for both staff and members. The organization wanted a system that would routinely update with new and emerging technologies. NAACCR attended the AMS fest and engaged with a consultant to help it find the perfect AMS—a system that could simplify everyday tasks and support its growth. The organization chose Nimble AMS, as it exceeded all necessary software requirements. During NAACCR’s implementation of Nimble AMS, no question was ever off the table. The AMS implementation was seamless with no issues at all.

After switching to Nimble AMS, NAACCR has dramatically improved the staff and member experience. The organization can now leverage a member portal that is intuitive and user-friendly, driving more members to resolve their own issues without staff guidance. Due to Nimble AMS’s ease of use, member support requests have decreased significantly, allowing staff to spend more time on activities that enhance member value. Staff can now leverage automated reports and notifications, thereby increasing their time to respond to members, especially regarding volunteer requests.

The North American Association of Central Cancer Registries (NAACCR) was established in 1987 as a collaborative umbrella organization for North American cancer registries, governmental agencies, professional associations, and private groups interested in enhancing the quality and use of cancer registry data. NAACCR is a professional organization that develops and promotes uniform data standards for cancer registration, provides education and training, certifies population-based registries, and encourages the use of cancer surveillance data and systems for cancer control, epidemiologic research, public health programs, and patient care to reduce the burden of cancer in North America.
How NAACCR reduced support cases with a member portal
Scaling Operations After Leaving a Legacy AMS

item.title: National Investment Company Service Association (NICSA): Nimble AMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: When NICSA discovered they would have to pay the same price as a new customer just to upgrade to the latest version of their old AMS, they recognized an opportunity to implement a new solution that better met their needs.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: NICSA upgraded Salesforce-based AMS to integrate all technology platforms and improve member management.
The Momentive team has been so transparent, patient, and human from the get-go. We have never felt like we’re a number or a sales goal.

Our old AMS system was outdated. It was difficult to configure and use, and we didn’t feel like a priority to our vendor. With Nimble AMS, we now have a fresh, modern, updated system that’s much easier to use. And, the Momentive team has been so transparent, patient, and human from the get-go. We have never felt like we’re a number or a sales goal.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:40:"Reliable and worry-free updates 3x/year ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:41:"Real-time reports and dynamic dashboards ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:43:"Member Portal to enhance member experience ";}}

In 2019, the National Investment Company Service Association (NICSA), a trade association for the global asset management industry, had been using its Salesforce-based association management software (AMS) for six years. They decided to upgrade it as part of an initiative to integrate all of their technology platforms. When they discovered they would have to pay the same price as a new customer just to upgrade to the latest version of the product, they recognized an opportunity to implement a solution that better met their needs.  

They chose Nimble AMS for a modern solution that includes:  

✓ Easy configuration to address their specific processes  

✓ A modern member-facing portal to deliver a great member experience  

✓ Regular upgrades so they always have the latest technology at no additional cost  

✓ A positive vendor relationship for help when they need it

NICSA was stuck on an outdated AMS system. They could not configure the system without going to their AMS vendor for help, and they often ended up being charged an additional fee. The organization needed a system that would help them work more efficiently while always staying on the latest version of the product. 

NICSA worked with the Momentive team to migrate to the modern, easy-to-use Nimble AMS system, which is backed by excellent customer service.

NICSA now has a modern system with regular upgrades included, ensuring it will always use the latest technology. NICSA saw immediate improvements, including the ability to easily configure the AMS to meet its needs and save time through easier-to-use navigation. They also enjoy a friendly and approachable customer service experience with Momentive.

NICSA is a not-for-profit trade association that strives to connect all facets of the global asset management industry to develop, share, implement, and advance leading practices. For over fifty years, Nicsa has aligned industry participants through formal education programs, interactive forums, networking opportunities, and initiatives such as the Diversity Project North America and the Blackwell Scholarship.
How NICSA modernized AMS to improve operations
NICSA Transforms Operations with Momentive AMS

item.title: Communications Workers of America (CWA): Aptify Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Momentive’s Aptify offers rapid membership software implementation, enhanced data quality, and reduced system issues for CWA.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Momentive Helps CWA Say Goodbye to System Issues
A large and complex union stays current with dues processing and says goodbye to system issues
We found only a few software options that could handle the complexity and scale we needed in a membership management system. There are many times that I’ve thought how glad I am that we chose Aptify.

We needed a membership software company that could move fast, was smart enough to handle our complex needs, and was nimble enough to support our needs as they evolve. I would absolutely recommend Aptify to other unions, especially if they need to do a lot of different kinds of things with their membership and dues system.

The Communications Workers of America (CWA) is a union that represents workers in various fields across the U.S. and Canada. Halfway into building a new membership system to replace their outdated one (which often had technical issues), CWA realized that the latest software would not be able to handle their complex membership structures and dues processes. So, they stopped the project. 

After working with a consulting firm through a software selection process, the CWA team chose Aptify membership management software instead. With Aptify, the CWA staff no longer loses time due to system issues. They now have a modern system that supports higher-quality data and effortlessly handles the union’s complicated invoicing and dues processes.

After striking out with a new membership system to replace their outdated software, CWA turned to Momentive for modern membership software that could handle their complex membership and dues structures while supporting their evolving needs. 

CWA was up and running quickly with Aptify. The union’s member data is now consolidated in a single system, enabling CWA and local unions to process dues, enter and manage member information, and view detailed reports from a single, easy-to-use platform.

With all data in one modern and reliable system, CWA works much more efficiently. After regularly being months behind in dues processing, the CWA team is now consistently current. The CWA team is also better able to support local CWA unions.

Members of the Communications Workers of America (CWA) are part of one of America’s largest and most diverse unions. CWA works not only in the communications and information industries, but also in the news media, airlines, broadcast and cable television, public service, higher education, healthcare, manufacturing, high-tech, and more.
How a large union improved dues processing with AMS

item.title: Massachusetts Collectors and Treasurers Association (MCTA): YourMembership Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: By switching to YourMembership, MCTA streamlined its database and website processes.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: MCTA Saves Time and Costs with Nimble AMS
MCTA was thrilled to upgrade to a consolidated platform, improve organizational efficiencies, and reinvigorate the member experience.
When we had to send out an email, it was time-consuming to configure. And now with YourMembership, everything is much smoother since it’s all integrated into one platform.
We had a really good team that helped us with the integration. They really listened and were very responsive if we had questions.

The purpose of the Massachusetts Collectors and Treasurers Association (MCTA) is to provide a continuing program of education and training for the management of public funds, sponsor and support legislation, and encourage and foster the development of effective municipal procedures. One of the biggest challenges the MCTA faces is keeping up with turnover in municipalities, driven by an aging population and Treasurers and Collectors leaving their positions for new opportunities in other communities. By switching to YourMembership, they streamlined their database and website processes in a clean, easy format.

For years, MCTA used multiple products across several brands for each of its services. Having their website, database, and other functionality spread across different systems meant the data was not integrated, and the cost of each product was inefficient for their budget. Because MCTA lacked a modern AMS, staff handled invoices and other tasks manually, inefficiently, and tediously. With new employees onboarding, it seemed like the ideal time to migrate to a system that could help MCTA continue to advance and meet the organization’s latest needs. 

MCTA was on the hunt for the right software to help them through their organization’s next phase. Momentive offered MCTA a team of hands-on experts to execute a smooth integration while ensuring the software could be customized to their specific needs. Throughout the integration, the Momentive team worked diligently to maintain excellent communication despite setbacks, providing a seamless transition. During the migration process, the implementation staff dedicated extra time to listening to MCTA’s needs and gaining a clear understanding of what their association/membership flow looked like. MCTA had a clear vision of what they wanted and needed, and Momentive brought that vision to life.

MCTA unveiled its YourMembership integration at its annual meeting, explaining the platform and demonstrating its advanced features. Association officers addressed and alleviated their members’ fears by showcasing the platform’s powerful, easy-to-use capabilities.  

Members were not only on board with the integration but also excited to explore all YourMembership’s robust features. Members of MCTA joined groups and updated their profiles, while staff could run queries to email 800-900 invoices for membership dues with a single click—something previously done via U.S. mail.  

MCTA was thrilled to upgrade to a consolidated platform and improve organizational efficiency with YourMembership’s all-in-one solution—the staff at MCTA dove into training courses to learn more about the platform and its features.  

Since integrating with YourMembership, MCTA has greater independence and efficiency in its current workflows, and, overall, the organization can now offer a more enjoyable experience for staff and members.

Organized in 1912, the Massachusetts Tax Collectors Association and Massachusetts Treasurers Association led to a merger of these two groups into the “Massachusetts Collectors and Treasurers Association” in 1928. The association has served as a medium for discussion and dissemination of information concerning the work of Collectors and Treasurers, which is continually changing due to improved techniques and evolving legislation. Its membership today includes all the Collectors and Treasurers of the State’s 351 communities, as well as many treasurers and collectors from districts and other entities.
How MCTA improved efficiency with a consolidated AMS
MCTA Saves Time and Money with Momentive AMS

item.title: ASCENT (Administrator Support Community for ENT): YourMembership Success Story

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: ASCENT Community | Membership Growth Case Study
ASCENT increased membership engagement by moving to Momentive’s YourMembership with a “Try Before You Buy” campaign.
We have had greater exposure doing this than we’ve made through any of our other marketing campaigns, which is why we decided to continue.
We have had greater exposure doing this than we’ve made through any of our other marketing campaigns, which is why we decided to continue.

Desiring membership growth, ASCENT moved its website to Momentive’s YourMembership and drove member engagement through a “Try Before You Buy” membership campaign.

There are more than 3,500 otolaryngology administrators in the country, but ASCENT membership base remained stagnant at nearly 1,000 members despite membership campaign efforts.   

Aiming to grow membership, ASCENT moved its website to Momentive’s YourMembership membership management software in mid-2014, then boldly announced it would offer free memberships to new members.

YourMembership gave ASCENT the membership flexibility, reporting, and communication options to drive member engagement through a “Try Before You Buy” membership campaign. With their new membership model, membership increased by 30 percent.   

Other results: 

  • 26% of trial members renewed with a paid membership the following year.  
  • 22% of trial members attended ASCENT annual conference that year.  
  • 13% of trial members paid to participate in an online webinar.  
  • 27% of trial members purchased at least one product from the online store.  
  • 35% of trial members registered from new medical practices that ASCENT had never before enrolled a member.

ASCENT has been providing resources, continuing education and networking for ENT practice management professionals for nearly four decades. We provide invaluable support, networking, professional and financial development to leaders in ENT medical practice management.
How ASCENT boosted engagement with YourMembership
ASCENT Adds 300 Members in Six Months

item.title: Alpha Omega International Dental Society (AOIDS): YourMembership Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: AOIDS chose YourMembership because it allowed their organization’s small staff to be independent, streamlined, and effective in their work.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Alpha Omega Dental Society | AMS Case Study
Enhanced AMS functionality offers a small staff more time to support their mission
Letting go of some of the monthly subscription products will potentially save the organization financially.

YourMembership works for complicated chapter-driven organizations like mine, and maybe a simple state organization with, for example, one trade membership level and one dues level. I think it offers a wide range of experiences for organizations.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:49:"User-friendly reporting boosts staff efficiency ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:51:"Easy integrations create cost-saving opportunities ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:0:"";s:13:"feature_title";s:51:"All-in-one solution drives staff and member value ";}}

Founded in 1907, the Alpha Omega International Dental Society (AOIDS) is a leading international organization with more than 100 dental alums and student chapters in ten countries. Their mission is to offer dentists, oral health professionals, and students a welcoming fraternal community that focuses on professional excellence and promotes access to oral health care for all. The organization is deeply engaged in philanthropic work, raising awareness and advocating for better access to oral health care internationally. AOIDS chose YourMembership because it allowed their organization’s small staff to be independent, streamlined, and effective in their work.

AOIDS’s former software solution was not keeping up with organizational needs. Because of limited bandwidth and challenging software requirements, there was no time to configure the necessary queries or reports in their system. Quickly, data was amassed, records duplicated, and employees didn’t have the resources for data hygiene efforts. Using a legacy system without R&D investment led to staff and member frustrations due to manual processes, outdated technology, and data hygiene issues.  

Since they did not have an all-in-one system, they had multiple technology solutions, ultimately increasing their technology costs. When their technology partner stopped serving their system, AOIDS had a choice: Continue investing in outdated software or take a leap and try something new. 

AOIDS knew they needed a new system and a quick implementation to improve their staff and member experience. Compared to the organization’s previous system, YourMembership also offers more organized, easily accessible queries and reporting resources.  

The user experience has empowered staff to learn the system’s robust capabilities, in hopes of reducing the need for support. Still, to reap the benefits of their new system sooner, Weber enlisted outsourced support, which she said has been instrumental throughout implementation and beyond.  

On her ongoing support from the Momentive team, Heidi Weber, Executive Director of AOIDS, said, “There is communication -- it’s a partnership -- and that’s a wonderful process.” Another benefit, she said, is that resources and training on the software are readily available. During the YourMembership migration, the implementation team worked with AOIDS on their data hygiene efforts, helping them refine their records as they switched systems.

With YourMembership, the AOIDS staff found peace of mind. Their small staff has limited resources, and using YourMembership levels up the organization's technology. Weber said, “I can easily hop into this product and pull reports. It’s made me very independent, and it’s given me a lot of confidence in this technology solution.” YourMembership offers various integrations, such as QuickBooks, that present multiple cost-saving opportunities for AOIDS and make staff more efficient. “The payments landscape has opened up,” said Weber. “It’s a huge time saver for me and the [external] vendors who work with me. I think there will be a cost saving, too.”  

The small staff now finds all reporting resources user-friendly, which is crucial to staff efficiency. The ease of YourMembership queries and reports has also given Weber and her staff the ability to quickly access the reports they need for their board members and chapters. Even better, said Weber, the all-in-one solution makes it easier for staff and members to access the data and information they need. The investment value is apparent: “I can feel that Momentive is putting time, money, and effort into this product, making sure it’s successful. And that makes me, as an Executive Director, successful in my work,” said Weber.  

Since implementing YourMembership, AOIDS has significantly improved access to data analytics, increasing bandwidth for its mission.

As an international dental organization with a 115-year history, AOIDS has built an inclusive community that welcomes dental professionals who align with its vision of ensuring that all communities have access to oral health care. AOIDS partners with like-minded organizations within the dental industry and realizes this important vision through high-quality continuing education opportunities, a vibrant alumni and mentorship program, and members donating time and talent to care for Holocaust Survivors and support dental education in Israel. AOIDS works to improve dental health care in local and global communities through compassion and inclusion initiatives.
AMS enhancements give small teams more mission time
Alpha Omega Gains Time to Focus on Mission

item.title: American Orthopaedic Association (AOA): NetForum AMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: With over 2,000 members/affiliates collectively, AOA needed comprehensive association management software (AMS) with strong integration capabilities. NetForum proved to be the best fit from contract to launch.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: NetForum Enhances AOA Staff & Member Experience
A growing association like AOA felt secure in knowing a longstanding AMS like NetForum is highly scalable.

It’s really helpful to have an AMS like NetForum that’s so well established and gives us so many options. With NetForum, our members are now able to easily follow a user-friendly process, cutting steps and saving time, which ultimately drives member retention and loyalty.” Kerry Lennon, Business Analyst and Technology Manager, AOA

The entire team is really happy to be with Momentive’s Netforum, from our Executive Director to accounting to committees, membership management, fellowship, fundraising and donations. This is changing our world.
Business Analyst and Technology Manager, AOA

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"15";s:16:"data_description";s:86:"Annual renewal process now takes 15 minutes instead of countless hours across 2 months";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"100s";s:16:"data_description";s:62:"Hundreds of baseline reports available in NetForum to leverage";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"99%";s:16:"data_description";s:108:"Optimized implementation process led to early and often system access, so staff were 99% trained upon launch";}}

The American Orthopaedic Association (AOA) is dedicated to its members and the specialty through the sharing, collaboration, and creation of intellectual resources, educational events, conferences, webinars, journals, podcasts, and other educational materials.  

Seeking to transcend subspecialties and unify orthopaedic leaders, AOA works to impact its field and support its members. With over 2,000 members/affiliates collectively, AOA needed comprehensive association management software (AMS) with strong integration capabilities. NetForum proved to be the best fit from contract to launch.

AOA experienced an incomplete implementation and data conversion with its previous AMS, resulting in a system with broken processes and numerous workarounds. Many of the everyday tasks performed by AOA staff were manual and time-consuming. AOA was constantly hit with extra costs and no promise to fix what was broken. A lack of technical support added to staff frustration and prolonged the time it took to resolve the issue.   

An association consisting of orthopaedic physicians in leadership, AOA needed to support its members with a robust AMS while also gaining efficiencies for its smaller staff and IT department of one.  

AOA selected NetForum AMS for its integration capabilities, transparent pricing, and organized implementation process. A growing association like AOA felt secure in knowing that a longstanding AMS like NetForum is highly scalable.

With NetForum, AOA now has a fully implemented AMS, including optimal features and bonus functionality to leverage. AOA has achieved significant efficiencies with NetForum, enabling staff to work smarter, not harder. With NetForum, AOA now owns and can access its code, utilizing configurations that have been added to the system.  

With a user-friendly membership portal and the convenience of quickly paying invoices using a secure link via Express Pay, NetForum has enhanced the member experience. AOA is excited to grow with NetForum for many years to come.

The American Orthopaedic Association (AOA) equips orthopaedic surgeons with the knowledge and skills necessary to lead effectively in the ever-changing landscape of the health care environment. Through its diverse constituency of orthopaedic leaders, the AOA focuses on leadership, academic, and critical issues associated with the specialty.
How AOA improved scalability with a trusted AMS
Enhancing AOA Member Experience with NetForum

Washington Association of School Business Officials (WASBO)
With Momentive, they have forums where people can ask questions, and it builds this library of information.

Having the systems all integrate saves time and money because if the two systems can talk to each other, you’re not going in and doing any intervention or making sure it’s working. It’s been great having all the pieces that we need in one place.

As an association that serves and supports financial and operational leaders, the Washington Association of School Business Officials (WASBO) needed strong association management software (AMS), learning management software (LMS), and a career center solution to deliver a connected experience.  

WASBO chose YourMembership to provide an all-in-one AMS that could handle events and membership management. Compared to other AMS options, YourMembership AMS proved to be the best software solution for the organization, and WASBO knew that they had the support and advocacy of the Momentive team to succeed.  

To offer more member benefits like professional development and career advancement, WASBO chose to integrate YourMembership with the Community Brand solutions, Path LMS and YM Careers. Choosing to integrate Path LMS and YM Careers provided a connected software experience for the association. WASBO appreciates having multiple systems integrated under the Momentive umbrella, boosting organizational efficiency and cost savings.

WASBO wanted to offer more member benefits while providing a connected software experience for staff. To best meet member and staff needs, WASBO required additional systems, including a career center and an LMS to integrate with their AMS. To avoid standalone systems and siloed data, WASBO turned to the Momentive family of software solutions.  

As an existing Momentive customer, WASBO found several robust solutions that integrated well with their AMS. WASBO chose YM Careers and Path LMS to offer career advancement and professional development opportunities for members.

WASBO has gained efficiency, increased productivity, and garnered cost savings by leveraging connected systems. Because their AMS, career center, and LMS integrate seamlessly, WASBO members reap all organizational benefits in one location. WASBO now offers all its live stream and in-person events using its integrated platforms. Members can meet all their professional development and career advancement needs within the Momentive family of solutions.
The Washington Association of School Business Officials (WASBO) exists to engage, develop, and serve successful leaders in school finance and operations.
How WASBO improved productivity with Momentive solutions

item.excerpt:encoded: The Canadian Association of Emergency Physicians (CAEP) is a mid-sized organization with no IT staff and over 2,000 members. It provides continuing education and fosters research in emergency medicine. To support its members and amplify its mission, CAEP needed full association management software (AMS) capabilities. CAEP chose NetForum's optimized implementation to streamline staff operations, enhance the member experience, and advance its mission.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: CAEP chooses NetForum AMS for its ease of use, superior support, and transparent pricing.
The Canadian Association of Emergency Physicians (CAEP)
We no longer pay for patches or updates or receive surprise invoices. Now, we get regular updates and keep our AMS current, all at one transparent cost.

As soon as we kicked off the implementation, the Momentive team was amazing. Our Project Manager kept us on track with weekly check-ins. Everyone was amazing and really responsive. We did the implementation through holding our annual conference with 1000 delegates. The team did an excellent job of working around those blackout dates. It was not stressful at all, and we finished with time to spare.
Director, Member Engagement and Advocacy, CAEP

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"1";s:16:"data_description";s:89:" hour of daily staff time saved due to streamlined processes and increased efficiencies ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:6:"<60";s:16:"data_description";s:63:" days post-implementation for CAEP to see a rise in donations ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"0";s:16:"data_description";s:57:"time now spent on manually updating prorated memberships ";}}

With over 2,000 members, CAEP provides continuing education and fosters research in emergency medicine. To support its members and amplify its mission, CAEP needed full association management software (AMS) capabilities. CAEP selected Momentive’s NetForum AMS, featuring an optimized implementation, to streamline staff operations, enhance the member experience, and advance its mission. NetForum AMS emerged as the winner due to its ease of use, superior support, and transparent pricing.

To best support emergency physicians and advance emergency medicine guidelines, CAEP searched for a comprehensive AMS. With no IT staff, CAEP needed an AMS that didn't include all the bells and whistles that accompany complex tech solutions. CAEP wanted an AMS that offered best-in-class software support, without any hidden fees.  

The organization chose NetForum for its intuitive and user-friendly experience and is thrilled with the software’s fixed costs and stable infrastructure. CAEP’s previous AMS initially seemed robust; however, it was missing key functionality that led to costly fees for patches and platform updates. CAEP’s previous system experienced significant lag time and had slow, unresponsive support. These challenges caused staff and member frustrations and led to a loss in revenue. Facing an impending software renewal, CAEP needed a quick solution to avoid re-signing an unwanted contract.   

CAEP chose NetForum AMS for the optimized implementation, resulting in fixed costs, stable infrastructure, and robust features.

Since choosing NetForum, CAEP has experienced great success. The implementation of NetForum was completed on time, resulting in a smooth software adoption. With Momentive, CAEP staff have responsive tech support and reduced lag time, saving the organization money and time. Best of all, CAEP can now leverage an easy-to-use system with key functionality that was missing in their previous system.  

While the implementation took place during CAEP’s major annual conference, everything proceeded smoothly, and the software launch was completed with ample time to spare. With NetForum, CAEP staff now have an easy-to-use system, key functionality that was previously missing, and responsive technical support.

As the national voice of emergency medicine (EM), the Canadian Association of Emergency Physicians (CAEP) provides continuing medical education, advocates on behalf of emergency physicians and their patients, supports research, and strengthens the EM community. In collaboration with other specialties and committees, CAEP also plays a crucial role in developing national standards and clinical guidelines, promoting excellence in EM across Canada.
Why CAEP chose NetForum for usability and pricing

item.title: Georgia Public Library Accounting Alliance; Coastal Plain Regional Library: MIP Accounting Success Story
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: See how the Georgia Public Library Accounting Alliance and Coastal Plain Regional Library System streamlined accounting, improved reporting, and achieved financial clarity with nonprofit accounting software.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Georgia Public Library Accounting Alliance; Coastal Plain Regional Library
[MIP Accounting is] much more intuitive and I feel like I spend less time fighting the software to get the information I want out.
[MIP Accounting is] much more intuitive and I feel like I spend less time fighting the software to get the information I want out.

Libraries aren’t known for being renegades, but for the 40-member Georgia Public Library Accounting Alliance; Coastal Plain Regional Library System there’s no stopping progress when it comes to best meeting the needs of their communities and staff. For over a decade they relied on a previous provider for their accounting software solution. With an ongoing ecosystem of complex funding streams, they knew it was time to consider other options.

In researching other accounting software, they also discovered that their current accounting solution provider was not going to offer a payroll module in their next product. That was a deal breaker.

“I considered the previous software to be not at all user-friendly. I find MIP much more intuitive and I feel like I spend less time fighting the software to get the information I want out,” said Kim Spencer, CFO of Coastal Plain Regional Library System.

The alliance desired an accounting software product that was more intuitive and could support a wide range of accounting skill sets. The software also had to be affordable because of the library’s commitment to donors, grants, and the public system. The alliance had to secure a system that was easy to use as some of the alliance members were hesitant to change the system in place. Additionally, the library system also needed an integrated payroll, that could easily handle different pay periods and dates.

As Spencer said, “We’re very public service oriented and must be able to pay our staff effectively. We’re all territorial about paying our employees and want to be able to ensure we fix any problem with paychecks immediately.”

Finally, the alliance needed strong customer support, as their experience with the previous provider was difficult at best, and non-existent at times. Spencer notes, “We would call, and they would say they didn’t have an account with us, so it was often time-consuming and not rewarding.”

The GPLAA selected MIP Accounting for their new accounting management program. Their team is delighted with functionality including the exporting feature on MIP which has provided superb donor/grant reporting for auditor visits. MIP exports to Excel seamlessly and there is no need to reformat.

“When my auditor is here and wants to look at what I spent on a grant, it takes seconds to pull the report and just hand it to him,” Spencer said. “MIP exports so beautifully. With this program we have software that does not try to force you to change for it, it changes for you.”

The mixed skill set of users has also benefited from the free customer tech support that MIP offers and have found filtering data in customizing reporting much easier to pull.

“It’s a great use of our library resources, cost-effective and financial reporting is very flexible,” Spencer said. “Libraries are funded by so many different sources and being able to pull reports when the funding agents ask is so important. We can just respond so quickly.”

About Momentive Software

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

The Coastal Plain Regional Library System is a free public agency committed to supporting and contributing to educational, informational, cultural, and recreational needs of its communities and schools. It is part of the Georgia Public Library Accounting Alliance, a consortium of library systems in Georgia.
Georgia Public Library Accounting Alliance and Coastal Plain Regional Library System improved reporting and financial clarity with accounting software.
How two libraries improved reporting with accounting

The exceptional service and commitment to our success have been invaluable.

ACC has partnered with Momentive to host our career center. Not only do they support the job board, but they also create a well-rounded experience for job seekers, providing various resources to help them in their journey. This support has significantly boosted our revenue and expanded engagement with our in-person career fair. The exceptional service and commitment to our success have been invaluable.
Associate Web Manager, American College of Cardiology

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:22:"box-bxs-bookmark-heart";s:13:"feature_title";s:85:"World-class member career development destination on a cutting-edge software platform";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bxs-badge-check";s:13:"feature_title";s:101:"Dedicated sales support making thousands of touches every month to drive employer signups and revenue";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bxs-blanket";s:13:"feature_title";s:171:"Expert marketing support to drive member engagement through strategic rebrand with integrated logo design, custom campaign development and multi-channel marketing strategy";}}

The American College of Cardiology (ACC) was looking to grow their member engagement, career resources, and non-dues revenue. The Momentive team demonstrated to ACC how their association could provide members with an invaluable career resource while generating additional revenue through turnkey solutions. 

Momentive provided a turnkey career fair service to engage members during ACC ‘24, with over 250 job seekers attending and 46 exhibitors, thereby generating non-dues revenue. A Resume Sweepstakes rounded that out to drive additional awareness and engagement to cardiology careers.

The first challenge was how to provide career services to members that they would find valuable and engage with. ACC and Momentive developed a marketing strategy to ensure the success of sweepstakes and career fairs.

Additionally, ACC was looking for dedicated media sales support and custom marketing touchpoints for employers. Momentive’s support led to ACC seeing an increase in sales on their job board.

With the strategic support of Momentive, ACC increased career center engagement by 47% in less than two years (June 2022 – August 2024) and resume uploads by 79% in three months (November 2023 – February 2024), all while increasing career center revenue by 17% in the first year (July 2022 – June 2023).

They continue to work with Momentive on new offerings for their members, including Career Benchmark Dashboards, which provide real industry peer data that members can use when making career decisions. These offerings further solidify ACC Cardiology Careers as a valuable member benefit and a compelling career destination.

The American College of Cardiology (ACC) is the global leader in transforming cardiovascular care and improving heart health for all. As the preeminent source of professional medical education for the entire cardiovascular care team since 1949, ACC credentials cardiovascular professionals in over 140 countries who meet stringent qualifications and leads in the formation of health policy, standards and guidelines. Through its world-renowned family of JACC Journals, NCDR registries, ACC Accreditation Services, global network of Member Sections, CardioSmart patient resources and more, the College is committed to ensuring a world where science, knowledge and innovation optimize patient care and outcomes. Learn more at www.ACC.org.

Boosting Revenue and Engagement with YM Careers

We wanted a strategic partner who was invested in our success.”

And best of all, our members have a career center that offers them best-in-breed career development tools that keep them returning to the site.

SHRM previously worked with several different job board vendors but was seeking a true business partner who could help them engage members in new ways, collaborate on strategy, and drive revenue to new heights. Our job board gave SHRM the technology, service, and resources they needed to accomplish all of their goals.

In addition to needing creative solutions to improve a flat revenue trend, SHRM’s job board required a total overhaul to provide members with a better user experience, including visibility to the right jobs. After SHRM’s previous job board provider didn’t deliver, SHRM sought a more strategic partner to help them improve revenue and member engagement.

A new career center was just the tip of the iceberg. In addition to a more than 7-figure revenue growth through expert sales and marketing strategy, SHRM created a true career destination for members. This keeps them coming back to the job board for career development resources, events, resume reviews, jobs, and much more.

SHRM, the Society for Human Resource Management, creates better workplaces where employers and employees thrive together. As the voice of all things work, workers and the workplace, SHRM is the foremost expert, convener and thought leader on issues impacting today’s evolving workplaces. With 300,000+ HR and business executive members in 165 countries, SHRM impacts the lives of more than 115 million workers and families globally.

SWE hosted a Virtual Career Fair for engineers to connect with employers looking to hire. The 2020 SWE Virtual Career Fair connected over 500 professional and experienced engineers representing all sectors and disciplines of engineering and technology, with top employers such as Apple, Delta, Northrup Grumman, and others.

Hiring niche, qualified talent within engineering and technology can be difficult for employers. Employers in engineering and technology don’t always have the time, budget, or resources to interview as many candidates as they would like.  

SWE hosted a Virtual Career Fair for engineers to connect with employers looking to hire. We provided sales support and marketing strategy to sell employer booths while promoting registration.
This was SWE’s most successful virtual event, with 66% attendance rate and a 120% increase of registered job seekers. Over 31 employers’ booths and 1,930+ chats between job seekers and employers.

The Society of Women Engineers is centered around a passion for members’ success and continues to evolve with the challenges and opportunites reflected in today’s exciting engineering and technology specialties.

The challenge was creating an effective marketing strategy to get the word out to their members about their 1st virtual event, while also attracting prospective members. We provided sales & marketing support to sell employer booths while also engaging with AWIS members to register for the event.

This was such a successful virtual event that they decided to host a second one, and they continue to host biannually. They boast over 400 registered job seekers with a 58% attendance rate, more than 13 employer booths, and 400+ chats between job seekers and employers.

Founded in 1971, the Association for Women in Science (AWIS) is dedicated to driving excellence in STEM by achieving equity and full participation of women in all disciplines and across all employment sectors.

Veterinary Emergency and Critical Care Society (VECCS)

The Veterinary Emergency and Critical Care Society (VECCS) moved from using their in-house online job board to partnering with Momentive for turnkey job board software and career services. Our team demonstrated to VECCS how their association could provide members with an invaluable career resource while generating additional revenue. After a smooth rollout of their new online career center, VECCS has increased member engagement, boosted revenue by 300%, and continued to provide added career resources for members – all with no added work for VECCS staff.

VECCS wanted to upgrade their job board to provide a better member online career center while generating more revenue. As the veterinary industry faces skyrocketing demand and a shortage of veterinarians, VECCS members needed a reliable career resource. 

With a small staff, VECCS also needed a partner to manage all aspects of sales and marketing. To achieve their goals, they implemented Momentive's job board software and career services. 

With Momentive’s customer marketing and sales teams, VECCS now provides members with email alerts, recruitment guides, and more to help members grow their careers. VECCS utilizes the Association Placement Service to provide members with personal recruiters who find them their dream jobs. And, through Career Benchmark Dashboards, VECCS offers members and employers unprecedented career and industry insights, enabling them to make informed career decisions and become employers of choice.

In addition to increasing member value and engagement, VECCS has seen a 300% increase in revenue. This revenue boost for the small staff association has provided VECCS with a crucial replenishment of resources, all while offering its members a true career destination, especially in times of need. 

 

VECCS continues to embrace new ideas for improving member value and driving revenue. Now, the small staff relies on our outstanding customer success team to handle all of the details. Over the years, our team has worked closely with VECCS to create new member offerings, such as Career Benchmark Dashboards and the Association Placement Service, to provide even more benefits for members and solidify their career center as the ultimate career destination for veterinary industry professionals.

Founded in 1974, the Veterinary Emergency and Critical Care Society (VECCS) promotes the advancement of knowledge and high standards of practice in veterinary emergency medicine and critical patient care. VECCS serves about 6,000 members.
