item.title: American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers: YM Careers Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers (ASHRAE) relies on Momentive to provide their members with services and resources that help further their careers, as well as suppliers seeking the right candidate to fill their positions.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: ASHRAE Case Study: Association Management Success
See how the American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers improved their job board and revenue with Momentive.
American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air Conditioning Engineers (ASHRAE)
It's a solution where you can go to the expert rather than try to become the expert.

Rather than investing the time and gaining the experience that Momentive already has, you can focus on your core mission and know that Momentive has your back. It's a solution where you can go to the expert rather than try to become the expert.

The American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers has partnered with Momentive to provide members with resources to advance their careers, as well as suppliers seeking candidates to fill their open positions.

ASHRAE struggled with a different job board provider who didn’t understand who ASHRAE was, what their members did, or the industry they served. Their job board wasn’t gaining the traction it needed to support members or suppliers. 

ASHRAE wanted to run their job board on their own, and they needed an innovative solution backed by industry experts.

Since their partnership with Momentive, there has been less strain on ASHRAE staff to maintain their job board, resulting in a higher quality and more targeted pool of positions being advertised, and an increase in revenue.

The American Society of Heating, Refrigerating, and Air-Conditioning Engineers, founded in 1894, is a global society advancing human well-being through sustainable technology for the built environment. The Society and its members focus on building systems, energy efficiency, indoor air quality and sustainability within the industry. Through research, standards writing, publishing and continuing education, ASHRAE shapes tomorrow’s built environment today.
How ASHRAE improved job board revenue with Momentive

item.title: United Way of Salt Lake: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The United Way of Salt Lake was seeking a digital solution to mobilize its Young Leaders.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: United Way Salt Lake Motivates Young Leaders with Text
Traditional forms of communication didn’t resonate with its Young Leaders group, leading United Way of Salt Lake to look for a digital solution.

United Way of Salt Lake needed to enrich its relationship with its Young Leaders, a group of young philanthropic professionals committed to reducing poverty and focusing on educating and empowering youth. Traditional forms of communication didn’t seem to resonate with the group, and participation was lagging. This led them to look for a digital solution to mobilize the Young Leaders.

With the help of GiveSmart, United Way implemented a text subscription campaign targeted at young professionals. They were able to upload a list of cellphone numbers, verify the numbers, and send out a subscription campaign opt-in message. The subscription campaign allowed members to learn about events, learn about volunteer opportunities, and sign-up to participate via short link in the text message. New members could opt-in at any time by sending a keyword to a 51555, and they too would be subscribed for the Young Leaders updates.

As expected, results followed. Text messaging allowed United Way to interact with the group members through their preferred method of communication and thus improved engagement and participation. The response speed to volunteer and event sign-ups drastically increased, and the unsubscribe rate remained incredibly low at 4%. To date, United Way of Salt Lake has 2,025 subscribers and maintains an active group of Young Leaders.

Established in 1904 as the Salt Lake Charity Association, its original mission was to help the poor, discourage panhandling, and coordinate multiple programs. The historic “community chest” with a broad charitable mission has transformed into an agent for social change. Today, United Way of Salt Lake is pursuing lasting social change on some of the toughest challenges we face, including poverty, poor health and lagging educational achievement.

item.title: Memorial Medical Center Foundation: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Memorial Medical Center Foundation sought an easier way to collect donations and expand its donor base.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: $450K Raised for Memorial Medical Center via Text-to-Donate
Memorial Medical Center Foundation utilizes GiveSmart for in-the-moment text-to-donate at annual gala.
Because of the support we received from [GiveSmart], and the amazing fundraising tools it offers, we were able to have tremendous success in exceeding our campaign fundraising goal!
Because of the support we received from [GiveSmart], and the amazing fundraising tools it offers, we were able to have tremendous success in exceeding our campaign fundraising goal!

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"133";s:16:"data_description";s:32:"total gifts received from donors";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:8:"$463,408";s:16:"data_description";s:31:"total raised through this event";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"$290";s:16:"data_description";s:31:"average gift size for the event";}}

Every year, Memorial Medical Center Foundation hosts its annual gala with a ‘Dancing for Our Stars’ competition. In previous years, they garnered support through peer-to-peer fundraising strategies, in which the dancers created their own pages and reached out to their networks to ask for donations. In 2018, they were looking for an easier way to collect donations and expand their donor base.

To help Memorial Medical Center Foundation achieve its goals, the [GiveSmart] experts recommended setting up a keyword and using text-to-donate before, during, and after the event to make it easier to reach their constituents, expand their donor base, and encourage in-the-moment giving. During the event, attendees texted their donations using a keyword. With the live thermometer displayed on the main screen, they could see their donations in real-time, creating more excitement than they’d seen at previous events.

• 133 total gifts received from donors 
  • $463,408 total raised through this event 
  • $290 average gift size for the event

Memorial Medical Center Foundation, a nonprofit health system, aims to improve the well-being of individuals, families, and communities by making high-quality care easier to access and more affordable.

item.title: Center for Applied Behavioral Instruction (CABI): GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: CABI was searching for text-to-donate and mobile messaging to optimize their campaign and needed a solution with exceptional customer service to walk them through the ins and outs of their first significant event.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: CABI Surpasses Fundraising Goals with Text-to-Donate
CABI used GiveSmart to set up & connect with supporters for their first annual Achieve Golf Tournament.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"$188";s:16:"data_description";s:32:"total gifts received from donors";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"$47,421";s:16:"data_description";s:31:"total raised through this event";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"$402";s:16:"data_description";s:31:"average gift size for the event";}}

The Center for Applied Behavioral Instruction (CABI) already had a fundraising software provider, but was looking for a new, more straightforward solution to set up and connect with supporters for their first annual Achieve Golf Tournament. They were searching for text-to-donate and mobile messaging to optimize their campaign in the most effective and efficient way possible. They wanted a solution with exceptional customer service to walk them through the ins and outs of their first significant event.

CABI Autism made the switch to GiveSmart in April before their event, and in short order, the team got them up to speed on the new platform. Their use of text-to-donate and mobile messaging enabled guests to easily support the event using their mobile devices.

CABI’s initial goal was $15,000. When they surpassed that, they increased the goal to $25,000. When they then beat that, they upped it again. With the help of [GiveSmart], all the excitement and passion translated into a successful event that well surpassed their ultimate goal of $35,000, raising $47,421 from 118 donors, with an average gift size of $402.

The Center for Applied Behavioral Instruction (CABI) is a private Massachusetts 766-approved special education day school for students with Autism Spectrum Disorder and challenging behaviors. CABI’s mission is to provide the best-practice behavioral and educational programming to children and young adults with autism. They believe every child, given the right tools, resources, and environment, has the potential to achieve the possible and live a more independent and fulfilling life.

item.title: Sav-A-Life: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Sav-A-Life’s goal was to take a multichannel approach to fundraising by incorporating text-to-give capabilities, social media, email, and other channels. GiveSmart was able to help.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Sav-A-Life Surpasses Their Year-End Fundraising Goal with GiveSmart
Sav-A-Life Bolsters End-of-Year Campaign with Branded Text-to-Donate
This campaign was so much simpler because [GiveSmart] basically did all the work for me!

This campaign was so much simpler because GiveSmart basically did all the work for me! All I had to do was schedule the posts, emails, etc. Our investment has already paid dividends! We raised just over $2,500 on #GivingTuesday. I thought that was outstanding, considering we have not attempted a #GivingTuesday campaign with any intentionality in the past. Thank you GiveSmart for helping us reach our goals!

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"138";s:16:"data_description";s:32:"total gifts received from donors";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:11:"$137,407.94";s:16:"data_description";s:34:"total raised through this campaign";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:0:"";}}

In the past, Sav-A-Life has focused much of its efforts on year-end fundraising and a direct mail piece that was the campaign's cornerstone. They had never put a concerted effort into #GivingTuesday and were now ready and excited to try it as the launch of their year-end campaign. Their goal was to take a multichannel approach to fundraising by incorporating text-to-give capabilities, social media, email, and other channels.

Sav-A-Life worked alongside GiveSmart to develop a plan for when they should be communicating the campaign and how. They utilized a branded text-to-donate keyword (LIVES to 71777), so supporters could easily donate to their campaign. In addition, they included links to branded online donation forms in all their campaign communications.

Integrating a strategic online approach that incorporated #GivingTuesday enabled Sav-A-Life to surpass its year-end fundraising goal of $120,000, raising $137,407.94 from 138 donors. Their efforts also significantly bolstered overall engagement with their supporters and allowed them to continue expanding their mission.

Sav-A-Life, Vestavia is the largest pregnancy care center in the Birmingham metropolitan area and is dedicated to putting an end to abortion, not by legal and public policy, but by supporting one woman at a time to spare her and her child from abortion. They work to accomplish this goal by offering free and confidential services, counseling, education, and support to empower women to carry their babies to term.

item.title: Alzheimer’s Association: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Needing technology to assist in silent auction fundraising, the Alzheimer’s Association looked towards GiveSmart.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Alzheimer's Association Drops Paper for Interactive Giving
By choosing to incorporate technology, ALZ enhanced their “A Reason to Hope” attendee experience and increased overall engagement.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:9:" $20,000+";s:16:"data_description";s:36:"average amount raised per fundraiser";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"78";s:16:"data_description";s:24:"average number of donors";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"$275";s:16:"data_description";s:21:"average donation size";}}

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:19:"box-bx-donate-heart";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"text-to-donate form ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bx-credit-card";s:13:"feature_title";s:74:"credit card swipers to process the silent auction items and raffle tickets";}}

Every spring, the Alzheimer’s Association hosts its ‘A Reason to Hope’ event. This event is a national endeavor that brings significant funding to the organization. They wanted to eliminate paper donations and offer a more modern and interactive method of donating during the event. Also needing technology to assist in silent auction fundraising, they looked towards GiveSmart.

With the help of GiveSmart, they offered a text-to-donate form to their donors that could be texted to anyone who desired to make a gift. They also improved event participation by having their staff circulate with credit card swipers to process silent auction items and raffle tickets. They also projected a GiveSmart thermometer that showed donations as they were made and identified the donors on the screen. GiveSmart implemented this strategy to energize the crowd and motivate donors to give in the moment.

ALZ was very satisfied with the GiveSmart solution, which provided an easy, effortless donation process. Throughout the many events spread across the U.S., the fundraisers averaged $20,000+, 78 donors, and a $275 average donation. By incorporating technology, they enhanced the event attendee experience and increased overall engagement.

A Reason to Hope community events raise funds for programs and services critical to the individuals and families living with Alzheimer’s disease and raises awareness of the work of the A Reason to Hope community events raise funds for programs and services critical to the individuals and families living with Alzheimer’s disease and raises awareness of the work of the Alzheimer’s Association.

item.title: The Elmbrook Education Foundation: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: At the onset of the pandemic, the EEF was prepared and set up for success with GiveSmart. They were able to quickly expand online fundraising and provide the necessary support and an online fundraising platform for their nine schools and three booster organizations, without scrambling to learn and adopt other technologies.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: GiveSmart provided the EEF with the tools they needed to fundraise before, during, and after the pandemic.
We're selling out every event ... I think a lot of it has to do with GiveSmart bridging those donor relationships.

GiveSmart offers us a great opportunity to build relationships, and if you look at where we are now compared to then [pre-2020], ...the money, especially for the EEF events that we bring in, we're selling out every event ... I think a lot of it has to do with GiveSmart bridging those donor relationships.
Executive Director at the Elmbrook Educational Foundation

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"$925K";s:16:"data_description";s:22:"Raised with GiveSmart ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"1";s:16:"data_description";s:55:"Award-Winning Fundraising and Donor Management Platform";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"100%";s:16:"data_description";s:16:" Event Sellouts ";}}

Back in 2014, the Elmbrook Education Foundation (EEF) needed a better value for itsevent fundraising management solution. The Foundation felt it had been overpaying for its previous platform, which also included superfluous features and services irrelevant to its needs.   

The foundation evaluated a few options, concluding that they could take full advantage of GiveSmart’s platform and that it offered the best value. The team was happy to save money and streamline its use of one platform that could do more.

The EEF quickly realized how easy it was for staff and volunteers to use and navigate GiveSmart’s platform. And because of this, its mall staff could dedicate the limited event time to stewarding donors, rather than being stuck troubleshooting behind a laptop. From the beginning, they have found the full functionality of the GiveSmart platform easy to use for the team, volunteers, and supporters.   

The team continues to be empowered to expand their use of the platform, leaning into their creativity to find new ways to engage their support network and solicit donations.

The EEF has been a happy GiveSmart customer for many years. They’ve worked hard to achieve fundraising growth, had consistent event sell-outs, and found high donor satisfaction.   

At the onset of the pandemic, the EEF was prepared and set up for success. They were able to quickly expand online fundraising and provide the necessary support and an online fundraising platform for itsnine schools and three booster organizations, without scrambling to learn and adopt other technologies. Since GiveSmart offers unlimited admins in the system, as well as the ability to host unlimited fundraisers under one contract and vendor, the EEF had nearly all of the tools they needed to best support itsmission.  

As GiveSmart has expanded its features, now offering more robust peer-to-peer fundraising and highly customizable forms, EEF is fully set up to best support its students and staff and to develop further.    

Over the years, EEF’s long-standing use of GiveSmart, combined with its ease of use for volunteers and donors, has led EEF’s staff and Board of Directors to feel confident they chose the right solution all those years ago.

The EEF is a nonprofit organization that has provided scholarships and grants to Wisconsin-based Elmbrook students and schools for over 60 years. Founded in 1958 by parents and community partners as a scholarship fund, the EEF has since awarded $1,500,000 in scholarships to Elmbrook students.   

In 1999, EEF’s mission expanded to include innovative teacher grants for projects that would benefit all students, and it raised money to support and strengthen outstanding learning ideas that improve the overall educational quality of its public schools. The Elmbrook Education Foundation has since given $500,000 in grants to Elmbrook educators and schools.   

The EEF accomplishes its mission by partnering with parents, teachers, administrators, community members, and businesses throughout the year.

item.title: Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City was tired of the number of manual transactions their events involved and the time those took. They knew fundraising technology was becoming the norm, and later discovered, for good reason.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Boys & Girls Clubs KC Find Success with GiveSmart
Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City chose GiveSmart to help with silent auctions, live auctions, dynamic reporting, and more!
Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City
One year we had raised $30,000 total in that, and then the next year we used GiveSmart, we had raised $70,000 before we even walked in the door that night.

The first time that we used the software, we had such a ridiculous return on the silent auction alone. ...one year we had raised $30,000 total in that, and then the next year we used GiveSmart, we had raised $70,000 before we even walked in the door that night ...this is working, this is what we need it to be.
Senior Director, Special Events at Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"40%";s:16:"data_description";s:25:"Revenue growth since 2019";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"1";s:16:"data_description";s:55:"award-winning fundraising and donor management platform";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"$2.5M";s:16:"data_description";s:14:"Raised in 2022";}}

Back in the mid-2010s, Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City was tired of the number of manual transactions their events involved and the time those took. They knew fundraising technology was becoming the norm, and later discovered, for good reason. The Special Events team, alongside staff and volunteer leadership, evaluated nearly 10 silent auction platforms and ultimately decided to use GiveSmart to power their Kids Night Out event.

Not only did their silent auction generate a significant return in the first year using GiveSmart, but the Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City quickly saw how the right fundraising technology enhanced their guests’ experiences and reduced wasted time and transactional moments on event night for their staff and volunteers. As they found success with more GiveSmart features over the years, such as a live auction, instant buys, and dynamic reporting, they were confident they had chosen the right partner.

Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City has been a GiveSmart customer for 7+ years. They’ve seen fundraising growth by using GiveSmart for all of their fundraising events and have found opportunities to try new things, with success ever since. 

It is a good thing they were consistently growing over the years because during the height of the pandemic, Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City suddenly found themselves needing to fill more critical, unmet needs of their kids, such as nonexistent internet access, no supportive space for e-learning, and a lack of stable meals. Because Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City had grown their fundraising powered by GiveSmart, they were in a prime position to open more sites, expand their offerings, and deliver for their kids in new, needed ways. While other local organizations were forced to close, Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City did even more.

For more than 100 years, Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Kansas City has been inspiring kids to live out their dreams through affordable, accessible opportunities in the urban core. 

With the help of generous supporters, nearly 8,000 local kids get to experience the life opportunities every child deserves. We give them outlets to learn and grow, access to mentors and peers who care, and the chance to let loose and be a kid—all in a safe space. 

Whether it allows them to attend a baseball game, see a private tutor, take art classes or go on a field trip, your support provides the enrichment and resources to help kids stay on the path to a brighter future.

item.title: Over The Rainbow Housing: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Over the Rainbow Housing was looking to stretch their creativity, truly inspire giving, and host a campaign that was truer to the OTR Housing community and mission. They needed technology that would best support their ideas without limiting their fundraising potential.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Over the Rainbow Housing Goes Beyond the Donate Button
Over the Rainbow Housing used GiveSmart to integrate merchandise sales, text-to-donate & expand their audience year after year.
Over The Rainbow Housing Kindness Tag Holiday Fund (OTR Housing)
The BEST thing about GiveSmart is that we can fundraise in all these creative ways.

(GiveSmart’s fundraising platform) offers us the ability to do things so much differently than we ever did before. We knew that it would keep track of our donations, names and email addresses, and that it would help us with reporting. But the BEST thing about GiveSmart, for sure, is that we can fundraise in all these creative ways.
Manager of Development at OTR Housing

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"200%";s:16:"data_description";s:51:"year-over-year increase in end-of-year fundraising ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"1";s:16:"data_description";s:26:"kind act is now a movement";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"24-fold";s:16:"data_description";s:46:"increase in one event's fundraising in 4 years";}}

Over The Rainbow (OTR) Housing is an Illinois nonprofit organization dedicated to providing housing, independent living solutions, high-quality of life, services, and community for individuals with physical disabilities. OTR Housing had been participating in Giving Tuesday and sending an annual appeal letter for years. It was moderately successful, but it lacked the spirit of OTR Housing.  

Their Vice President, Katie Miglore, and Manager of Development, Alex Costelle, were looking to stretch their creativity, truly inspire giving, and host a campaign that was truer to the OTR Housing community and mission. They needed technology that would best support their ideas and not limit their fundraising potential. And they didn’t have hours to dedicate to multiple platforms and to figuring out how they work. Could they successfully revamp their end-of-year campaign?

Kindness Tag was initially inspired by an OTR resident who asked for help in gifting another resident with life-changing hearing aids. It then became that recipient’s turn, with the help of their Resident Service Coordinator, to pay the kindness forward to another resident. The movement continued. There had been no real designated, organized funding to support this project, though.  

With the overwhelming support of their board and leadership team and inspired by these residents, Miglore and Costelle developed the Kindness Tag Holiday Fund.   

These creative women turned to GiveSmart to help them create an extended end-of-year campaign. They had run “old school” campaigns in the past. Their campaign needed to offer more than just a donate button. Miglore and Costelle needed to bring the Kindness Tag story to life, integrate merchandise sales, use text-to-donate, and expand both their audience and offerings year after year. They also needed organized, easy reporting on their successes and better support for their stewardship efforts.

The OTR Kindness Tag Holiday Fund raised double what their previous Giving Tuesday and end-of-year campaigns had raised.   

This year, the organization is partnering with restaurants for the first time, as it expands its healthy food programming. Because Miglore and Costelle have the flexibility to customize their donation form with GiveSmart, sharing more on the impact of this expanded program and offering once-in-a-lifetime chef table experiences for donation was easy. The real-time, organized reporting ensured everyone was in the know and that staff and board knew a lot about their donors’ motivations.  

With the options GiveSmart offers, OTR Housing has no limits on expanding its Kindness Tag Holiday Fund offerings. And, even with the expanded offerings, it’s still easy for their support network to give. All of this allows the nonprofit to best deliver on its mission and meet the growing, critical needs of residents and future residents.

OTR Housing is an Illinois nonprofit organization dedicated to providing independent living solutions for individuals with physical disabilities. Their value statement is to envision an America, and more specifically an Illinois, where all persons with disabilities who desire to live independently, combined with the skills to do so, can find an accessible, affordable apartment.

item.title: The Solomon Schechter Day School of Greater Hartford: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Solomon Schechter needed a better way for the school’s Millennial-aged parents to easily donate to fundraising events.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Solomon Schechter's Fundraiser with Live Thermometer
Solomon Schechter’s annual event gets a fundraising boost from Fundraising software tools, such as text-to-donate and a live fundraising thermometer.
The Solomon Schechter Day School of Greater Hartford
If you don’t utilize a live thermometer at your fundraising event, you’re leaving money on the table.

Within the first hour of our event, GiveSmart paid for itself and then some. We raised over $13,000 after the first 30 minutes of displaying the live event thermometer at our annual Ner Tamid gala. If you don’t utilize a live thermometer at your fundraising event, you’re leaving money on the table.
Development Officer, Solomon Schechter Day School of Greater Hartford

Before [GiveSmart], Solomon Schechter had no way to accept donations at fundraising events other than checks or cash, which created significant extra work for donors and staff. They needed a better way for Millennial-aged parents to donate to its fundraising events more easily.

Solomon Schecter’s Development Officer, Sherri Pliskin, was the first to hear of [GiveSmart]. After seeing another nonprofit organization display a [GiveSmart] live fundraising thermometer at their event, she was impressed with the platform. She decided Solomon Schecter needed their own for their upcoming Annual Ner Tamid Gala. Ner Tamid is Solomon’s annual fundraising gala for parents, students, staff, and school supporters, and hosts between 200 and 300 attendees. After extensive research and comparison, Sherri chose [GiveSmart] for the fundraising thermometer’s ease of use and donors' ability to give effortlessly from their seats in any amount, via text-to-donate keywords and a shortcode.

Within the first hour of Ner Tamid, [GiveSmart] paid for itself and then some, as the keynote speaker raised over $13,000 through the fundraising event thermometer. During the following year’s Gala, the live fundraising thermometer raised $18,000 the first hour it was displayed! The school credits the thermometer’s success to the ease of texting to donate to their parents and supporters. The excitement of seeing donors’ names in real-time on the thermometer’s screen also encouraged more in-the-moment giving. Sherri was impressed by her entire experience with [GiveSmart], including the training provided to set up and operate their live fundraising thermometer, text-to-donate, and even script talking points. 

With the partnership of [GiveSmart], the live fundraising thermometer, and text-to-donate at their Annual Ner Tamid campaign, Solomon Schechter surpassed their event goal of $18,000, raising a total of $32,365 from 124 donors.

Solomon Schechter Day School is a Jewish Day School for children from early childhood through 8th grade. Their mission is to help develop each child in an academically challenging and nurturing environment with wisdom and compassion to navigate an ever-changing world.

item.title: St. Anthony Catholic School: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Between constrained staff resources and inadequate event and donor management systems, St. Anthony’s Director of Development knew the organization needed centralized fundraising and donor management, as well as more fundraising opportunities.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: St. Anthony's Journey Back to Better Fundraising
GiveSmart's connected platform helped St. Anthony's save time & fundraise more strategically across events, donors & communications.
What really differentiated the experience was GiveSmart’s relationship building.

I’m not exactly an easy person to sell to. I spent years poking holes in everything ... I was really impressed with Kait. She was prepared, knew her business, and what stood out is that she came from the fundraising industry...What really differentiated the experience was Kait’s relationship building.
Director of Development at St. Anthony Catholic School
Why GiveSmart Was the Right Choice

St. Anthony Catholic School had fallen into a pattern of starting from scratch for every fundraiser. Each campaign required rebuilding their approach, creating a patchwork system that felt inconsistent and disjointed. After moving away from GiveSmart to Bloomerang, they found themselves with mismatched systems that couldn't provide a comprehensive overview of donor activity, forcing staff to rely on memory rather than data when interacting with supporters.  

 

St. Anthony’s serves a community filled with significant needs. The school’s development staff knew that to provide their students with a more promising future, they needed to better steward and grow their donor base and expand their fundraising programs. Without improved systems and approaches, their ability to fulfill this vital mission would remain limited.

Between constrained staff resources and inadequate event and donor management systems, Jennifer B., St. Anthony’s Director of Development, knew the organization needed centralized fundraising and donor management, as well as more fundraising opportunities.   

The school had eager, inspired volunteers ready to contribute, but needed user-friendly tools they could use effectively. This led Jennifer to explore GiveSmart again, looking for time savings and top-of-the-line customer service to reach their fundraising goals.   

After connecting with Kait from GiveSmart’s customer success team, Jennifer sought three key improvements: volunteer enablement for event management, improved donor visibility, and expanded fundraising options.   

Kait’s consultative approach, rich with fundraising insights, demonstrated to Jennifer that St. Anthony’s would be supported as it looked to grow its revenue and better steward its donors. This approach convinced Jennifer that GiveSmart would be a true partner in their mission, leading the school to implement GiveSmart's full suite for events, online fundraising, and donor management.

The difference in customer experience was immediate and significant. Jennifer experienced top-notch service and support with GiveSmart, which stood in stark contrast to their experience with Bloomerang, where they struggled to even connect with their customer representative.  

In using GiveSmart, she recognized that the intuitive design and helpful templates made customization and automation easy for both staff and volunteers.   

The single-source solution that connected its fundraising, event, communication, and donor management data helped St. Anthony’s realize time savings and more strategic fundraising. They’re already more strategic with their gala planning, making impactful changes to donor engagement, ticketing, and sponsorships. Using Donor CRM's stewardship strategy tools, St. Anthony earned a $5,000 donation within 24 hours of their campaign's start. 

Like many development staff members, Jennifer wears many hats at St. Anthony's. The partnership with GiveSmart not only addresses current needs but also opens the door to future growth. Jennifer looks forward to exploring other fundraising campaigns, such as auctions and peer-to-peer fundraising, without incurring additional costs. 

St. Anthony Catholic School just broke ground on its much-anticipated new school construction. St. Anthony's partnership with GiveSmart makes this investment possible. By fueling the school’s mission and supporting their resilient community, GiveSmart is helping ensure that St. Anthony’s students, families, and community will continue to benefit for generations to come.

St. Anthony Catholic School aims to educate each child academically, physically, socially, and spiritually in a Christian context. They seek to instill moral integrity within each child. Working together as a cooperative community, each child will be prepared for secondary and higher education via a school environment that fosters love and respect for God and others.

item.title: YMCA of Greenville: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The YMCA of Greenville was using multiple, disjointed event and fundraising platforms, and switched them all over to GiveSmart’s all-in-one platform.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Strengthening Community with the YMCA of Greenville
The YMCA of Greenville found success by moving all of its fundraising efforts to GiveSmarts unified platform.
We use GiveSmart for everything from events, auctions, online giving and QR codes to text-to-give.

We use GiveSmart for everything from events, auctions online, giving QR codes to text-to-give …. all the different ways to maximize donor giving, and meet the donor where they are. We found that it's incredibly successful, especially for our Day of Giving.
Executive Director of Foundation and Fundraising Innovation at the YMCA of Greenville

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"400%";s:16:"data_description";s:23:"growth in Day of Giving";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"1";s:16:"data_description";s:56:"award-winning fundraising and donor management platform ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:" 3,000+";s:16:"data_description";s:13:"annual donors";}}

The YMCA of Greenville had contracted with GiveSmart for several years before the current Executive Director, MaryEllis Petrosian, took office. However, they were also using other event and fundraising management tools. The catalog of tools that their fundraising staff had was full of redundancies and excess spending. The use of multiple tools created a disjointed experience for their donors and excessive manual work and reporting for their staff.    

Petrosian had over a decade of experience fundraising with GiveSmart and knew it could be utilized for far more. The fundraising team felt that transitioning to GiveSmart for more of their initiatives would be best for both donors and staff, saving money and increasing efficiency by committing to one platform that could do it all.

The expanded GiveSmart platform offered the tools they needed to save money and raise more. GiveSmart offers unlimited fundraising as part of its contracts, and the Y takes full advantage of the option.   

They host a dynamic Day of Giving with multiple creative avenues to give. Being able to engage board members, their community branches, and area businesses via peer-to-peer fundraising, text-to-donate, and their support network through communication channels is integral to their fundraising success. In its third year, the Y’s Day of Giving raised $200,000.

The YMCA of Greenville has been using more included GiveSmart features and integrations each year since Petrosian began her role. Their team has seen consistent fundraising growth, provided better giving experiences for their donors, and easier data management for their staff.   

The Y has moved nearly all its fundraising initiatives to GiveSmart. In so doing, all donor information, reporting, and payment processing are in one place, making the addition of new initiatives efficient and manageable. In the first few months of 2024, their five fundraising events will all be run via GiveSmart, some for the very first time.   

The fundraising foundation they have established with GiveSmart, along with the growth they’ve worked hard to achieve, will allow them to remain a cornerstone of the Greenville community for generations to come.

For more than a century, the YMCA of Greenville has strengthened the foundations of the community it serves.    

The Y is committed to nurturing the potential of children through Youth Development, improving the nation's health and well-being by promoting Healthy Living, and fostering a sense of Social Responsibility by providing opportunities to give back and support our neighbors.    

They're proud of the diverse population they serve, and are passionate about keeping the organization’s promise to be a force for good. The YMCA of Greenville serves thousands of people from all walks of life through membership and programs.

item.title: Homeless Health Care Los Angeles: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The GiveSmart team provided structure and guidance throughout their #GivingTuesday campaign, including email, social media, and text messaging, to create a multi-channel approach to drive fundraising outcomes.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: How HCLA Used a Messaging Calendar to Drive Impact
HHCLA helps over 200 people per week and can now increase its efforts and do even more good!
Having the support from [GiveSmart] for this year’s #GivingTuesday campaign was a total game changer.
Having the support from [GiveSmart] for this year’s #GivingTuesday campaign was a total game changer.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"$13,542";s:16:"data_description";s:29:"pledged through this campaign";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"$12,264";s:16:"data_description";s:35:"more dollars from the previous year";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"56";s:16:"data_description";s:10:"new donors";}}

#GivingTuesday was approaching, and Homeless Health Care wanted to have its most successful year yet. They had such success with GiveSmart at its last gala that they decided to give [the platform] another try.

The GiveSmart team quickly went to work and identified the most significant problem–their donors weren’t giving as much as they could. The team prepared a messaging calendar, complete with timing and content, to illustrate the impact HHCLA had been making and could make with additional funding. This allowed HHCLA to take a strategic approach to their #GivingTuesday campaign and to develop professionally designed assets that enhanced the overall presentation. The team provided structure and guidance throughout the campaign, including email, social media, and text messaging, to create a multi-channel approach to drive fundraising outcomes.

With GiveSmart’s support , they were able to more than doubled the average gift size despite receiving only 56 new donors! By engaging their supporters and encouraging them to give more, they successfully raised $13,593 ($12,315 more than the previous #GivingTuesday campaign). Armed with strategy and support, HHCLA saw a significant improvement in donor response and can now continue using these practices to enhance its fundraising efforts year-round. Most importantly, this organization, which helps over 200 people per week, could now increase its efforts and do even more good!

Homeless Health Care Los Angeles 501 (c) (3) provides comprehensive behavioral and holistic health care to people experiencing homelessness. We have been providing a pathway to health and recovery, free from bias or judgment, for more than 30 years. With a continuum of services that includes syringe exchange, overdose prevention, substance use treatment, counseling, and housing with supportive services, we seek to improve the quality of life and capacity for stability of the people we serve.

item.title: Littleton Public Schools Foundation: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The Littleton Public Schools Foundation sought to consolidate its technology and eliminate manual work to better serve its team, volunteers, board, students, and teachers.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Helping Students Thrive with the Littleton Public Schools Foundation
LPSF used GiveSmart's connected platform to run their gala & golf outing without outdated, disjointed tools.
What I like about GiveSmart is that things don't fall through the cracks, unlike when you have multiple spreadsheets and multiple places that people need to look for information.

What I like about GiveSmart is that things don't fall through the cracks, unlike when you have multiple spreadsheets and multiple places that people need to look for information. If it's automated, it just happens, and GiveSmart allows us to have most of it in one place. That has been really essential, knowing that it's all in one place.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:8:"$50,000+";s:16:"data_description";s:7:"auction";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"100%";s:16:"data_description";s:49:"★★★★★ reviews of GiveSmart’s checkout";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"1";s:16:"data_description";s:19:" sold out wine pull";}}

The Littleton Public Schools Foundation (LPSF) has been dedicated to filling district funding gaps that Littleton Public Schools (LPS) has faced for 30+ years. During their tenure, LPSF staff, volunteers, and board members have established and grown several cornerstone events.    

The trouble was, they were fueling fundraising at those events through several different vendors, leaving their data, guest management, and communication tools scattered and disjointed. Some event setups were insufficient and clunky. Others made reporting unnecessarily challenging with ill-formatted spreadsheets. The foundation desired to consolidate its technology and eliminate manual work to better serve its team, volunteers, board, students, and teachers.    

The foundation evaluated several options and concluded that it could fully leverage GiveSmart’s connected platform to support its gala, The Spirit Celebration and its golf outing. GiveSmart offered the best value and opportunities for growth.

LPSF quickly saw how GiveSmart automated tracking and reporting, significantly reducing errors and manual work. Additionally, the guest management tools, easy seat assignments, sponsorship activation, paddle raise management, and day-of donor experience at their Spirit Celebration confirmed that GiveSmart was the best choice to support their small staff and large mission.    

They’ve seen consistent growth in their Spirit Celebration since using GiveSmart, in part because they can text attendees about giving opportunities, sell out their revenue enhancers, and grow silent auction funds raised from donors in the room and online. They also coordinate with a Smart Partner at their Spirit Celebration.   

They have also consolidated workloads and purchasing opportunities for their golf outing, creating early sign-ups and freeing up time on the day of the outing for board members and staff.

From the beginning, LPSF has found the full functionality of the GiveSmart platform easy to use for their team, volunteers, board members, and supporters.    

At their most recent Spirit Celebration, the check-in experience received only five-star ratings from donors in their post-event survey, and the checkout experience received a majority of five-star ratings, along with a few four-star ratings. Because they have streamlined the golf-outing registration experience, golfers, board members, and staff can look forward to a more engaging, mission-centered experience.    

Since adopting GiveSmart, LPSF has been able to spend more time telling its story and less time on behind-the-scenes, manual work. The team continues to be empowered to expand their unlimited access to the platform, specifically with peer-to-peer fundraising and text-to-donate, leaning into their creativity to find new ways to best support LPS students and staff and grow even more.    

The foundation’s use of a connected, automated GiveSmart platform has enabled them to fund teacher grants, technology purchases, and scholarships, as well as establish an as-needed rainy-day fund.

Littleton Public Schools Foundation raises money to support each LPS student’s unique path to success. Working with the school district and passionate partners, they identify and fund opportunities that foster personal and academic development for all students.

item.title: United Way of Buffalo & Erie County: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: When their crestfallen constituents experienced several high-profile catastrophes in a short period, United Way quickly realized they needed an avenue for evergreen, individual giving.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Encouragement Pours In for United Way of Buffalo & Erie
GiveSmart helped new fundraising groups quickly mobilize for the United Way of Buffalo & Erie County.
United Way of Buffalo & Erie County
That's a thing that is really valuable to us - that ease of setting it up quickly, of updating it quickly, and of embedding it where you need it to be.

GiveSmart is straightforward to set up and embed on any website. If you need to make an update to the form, it is automatically applied. That's really valuable to us. That ease of setting it up quickly, of updating it quickly, and of embedding it where you need it to be.
Director of Marketing and Communications at United Way of Buffalo & Erie County

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:6:"$214k+";s:16:"data_description";s:21:"Raised with GiveSmart";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"1";s:16:"data_description";s:56:"Award-Winning Fundraising and Donor Management Platform ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:6:"1,000+";s:16:"data_description";s:50:"Mobilized donors from over 1,000 unique ZIP codes ";}}

The greater Buffalo area has experienced significant crisis and hardship within its community since 2020. With as many as four in 10 people living in hardship, the COVID-19 pandemic was disproportionately difficult for so many and created a deficit, so that when other challenges came, the community needed dedicated fundraising and support.     

  

United Way of Buffalo & Erie County had well-established workplace giving and other funding sources. Still, when their crestfallen constituents experienced several high-profile catastrophes in a short period, United Way quickly realized they needed an avenue for evergreen, individual giving.

When the racially-motivated May 14 shooting happened, several partner organizations collaborated with United Way of Buffalo & Erie County to put up a fundraising form and page, so that individual donors had an avenue to provide both short- and long-term community support. GiveSmart offered an easy-to-set-up webpage and form so that this new group of fundraising and grant-making organizations could mobilize quickly.    

Then, a devastating blizzard arrived in December of 2022, claiming over 40 lives and leaving countless other people without power and heat for days, with significant property damage, lost wages, lost groceries, and more. United Way of Buffalo & Erie County knew people would expect a way to give.    

GiveSmart enabled them to respond agilely and mobilize a fund within days of the crisis. GiveSmart’s customizable, embeddable forms allowed the United Way of Buffalo & Erie County team to better engage with their support network in creating The Community Resilience Fund.

United Way of Buffalo & Erie County, recognizing that the need for crisis response is likely to recur, determined that establishing an evergreen emergency response fund would be prudent. The Community Resilience Fund and its predecessor have engaged donors from over 1,000 ZIP codes; an easy online giving option has proven critical to the Fund’s success. Now, when a crisis occurs, they do not need to wait to set up a new outlet for giving. The existing Community Resilience Fund can be repromoted at any time within minutes of a need arising. 

One surprising piece of United Way of Buffalo & Erie County's response fundraising was how donors used the Note field, available on all donation forms. As funds poured in from around the country, donors began leaving touching notes of encouragement and support. This bit of inspiration was one more way that GiveSmart's easy-to-use platform made a challenging period a little easier.   

United Way of Buffalo & Erie County has been a GiveSmart customer for nearly 8 years, initially turning to the solution for a text-to-donate platform. Their team finds the platform intuitive, which has opened up additional opportunities to use the platform. They’ve more recently tried new-to-them initiatives, such as peer-to-peer giving as part of their largest volunteer day, QR codes in marketing, voting competitions, and third-party fundraisers.   

GiveSmart offers unlimited campaigns and use of these tools with every contract, so when United Way of Buffalo & Erie County was looking for more, they already had all the tools they needed to best support their goals.

For over 100 years, United Way of Buffalo & Erie County has worked to close the health, education, and financial divides that threaten the well-being of their community. By forging unlikely partnerships, finding new solutions to old problems, and mobilizing the best resources, they inspire individuals to join the fight against our community’s most daunting social crises. Breaking a cycle of hardship to build a strong, equitable Erie County. One that is resilient in times of emergency—and beyond.

item.title: Virginia Tech Foundation: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: With a goal of a 22% alumni participation rate in mind, the team made a plan to connect with their most extensive alumni base where they are, on their mobile phones.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Virginia Tech Uses Mobile Solutions to Engage Younger Alumni
Virginia Tech Foundation reaches more alumni across generations with mobile solutions, including a text-to-donate option.
Text messaging proved to be just as important as our other marketing channels like direct mail and email marketing.
Text messaging proved to be just as important as our other marketing channels like direct mail and email marketing.
Director of Annual Giving, Virginia Tech

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"800+";s:16:"data_description";s:5:"Gifts";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"200";s:16:"data_description";s:19:"Gifts made via text";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"60%";s:16:"data_description";s:43:"Percentage of gifts made on a mobile device";}}

Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University (Virginia Tech) has a passionate alumni base but, like many universities, has struggled to connect with younger graduates, specifically Millennials and Generation X.  

When the Virginia Tech team received a mandate from their president to accomplish a 22% alumni participation rate by 2022, they knew they’d need to try new marketing channels. With this goal in mind, the team made a plan to connect with their most extensive alumni base where they are, on their mobile phones.

Virginia Tech’s Director of Annual Giving, Heather Keopec, decided to incorporate GiveSmart’s text-to-donate and mobile messaging into their #GivingTuesday campaign. Initially, her team was hesitant to devote more resources to their #GivingTuesday campaign, but Virginia Tech needed a way to stand out on their alumni’s crowded social media feed. Heather worked with the GiveSmart team to create paid social campaigns specifically for Facebook and Instagram. These ads featured a “Hokie Challenge,” where the university prompted viewers to donate and sign up for text message alerts. After capturing sign-ups from the ads, Heather sent three text message donation requests within a 4-day window before #GivingTuesday. She also segmented the campaign by participants' time zone, so alumni outside the Eastern Time Zone did not receive the messages too early in the day.

The text-to-donate addition to Virginia Tech’s #GivingTuesday campaign was successful, with over 800 gifts received. Additionally, 60% of the #GivingTuesday donations were made on mobile devices. With mobile giving proving to be a viable marketing channel, the team decided to add text-to-donate to other marketing campaigns. Adding a text-to-donate component to their Annual Giving Day drove 295 clicks and 163 gifts, raising $15,665. These text messages, with an embedded video, were also sent to the Baby Boomer and Silent Generation alums. By reaching younger and older alumni through mobile messaging, the Virginia Tech team increased alumni participation from 10% to 13%, bringing it closer to the university’s goal of 22% by 2022.

Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University is a public land-grant institute with its main campus in Blacksburg, Virginia. The school offers 280 undergraduate and graduate programs and has a total student enrollment of over 34,000.

item.title: Boy Scouts of America – Long Beach Area Council: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: With a staff of only four people, BSA Long Beach needed to find flexible, easy-to-use mobile donation software.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Boy Scouts Long Beach Funds Dining Hall with Text-to-Give
With a staff of only four people, BSA Long Beach needed to find flexible, easy-to-use mobile donation software.
Boy Scouts of America – Long Beach Area Council (BSA Long Beach)
Through the GiveSmart platform solutions, I’m thoroughly convinced it can help us revolutionize the way BSA leverages the power of its immense volunteer base.
Through the GiveSmart platform solutions, I’m thoroughly convinced it can help us revolutionize the way BSA leverages the power of its immense volunteer base.
Development Director of BSA Long Beach

The Long Beach Area Boy Scout Council found itself in a predicament, wanting to grow but needing funds. They faced a world in which parents no longer carried cash or checks, often opting for credit or debit cards. With a staff of only four people, they needed to find a flexible, easy-to-use mobile donation software solution.

GiveSmart worked with the Long Beach Area Boy Scout Council to develop the best possible solution. Within two days of deciding to work with GiveSmart, a customer service representative helped create a text-to-give online campaign for their fundraising event, featuring a live thermometer for recognition. They also set up a subscription campaign to capture the phone numbers of donors who wanted to receive updates, allowing them to send text messages with compelling reasons to donate to those who care most about their cause.

The GiveSmart solutions resulted in 75% of event attendees donating. Also, $48,000 was raised through online giving, $23,000 of which was donated specifically to fund a new summer camp dining hall. GiveSmart also cultivated 36 recurring donations and engaged 390 local donors.

Boy Scouts of America, Long Beach Area Council (Long Beach BSA) is the Long Beach, California branch of the Boy Scouts of America, whose mission is to prepare young people to make ethical and moral choices over their lifetimes by instilling in them the values of the Scout Oath and Law.

item.title: United Way of Central Maryland: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: With a big fundraising goal for their Thanksgiving campaign, the Baltimore-region nonprofit decided to digitize its fundraising by partnering with an organization that offers digital fundraising services.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Donating Thanksgiving Meals with United Way Maryland
United Way of Central Maryland delivered 4,000+ Thanksgiving meals using GiveSmart to power their Harvest of Plenty campaign.
The United Way Of Central Maryland
Technology helps us connect with supporters faster and easier than traditional methods.

When we go into the workplace and across social media, we use online forms, and when we are hosting in-person events, we use text to give. Technology helps us connect with supporters faster and easier than traditional methods."

Every Thanksgiving, United Way of Central Maryland holds its Annual Harvest of Plenty event, which aims to deliver more than 4,000 Thanksgiving meals to families in need. A donation of $25 feeds an entire family. With a big campaign goal, the Baltimore-region nonprofit decided to digitize its fundraising by partnering with an organization that offers digital fundraising services.

Through GiveSmart, United Way of Central Maryland set up crowdfunding pages and online forms to track where their donations were coming from, and an offline donation entry page to capture offline donation information. A real-time thermometer was also set up, allowing for live donation tracking throughout the campaign. All a donor had to do to donate was text "GiveThanks16" to 71777, and they could easily give their gift to a family in need!

The solution proved very successful. United Way of Central Maryland delivered more than 4,000 Thanksgiving meals to underserved families thanks to the success of its Harvest of Plenty campaign. During 2016, they received pledged donations of over $150,000 from 403 donors across 37 crowdfunding campaigns.

With the goal of offering hope during the holidays to underserved communities, United Way of Central Maryland aims to deliver more than 4,000 Thanksgiving meals to low-income families. The intention is for the community to give back to those who are underserved and make their holiday one to remember.

item.title: Supporting Our Aging Religious: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The SOAR! team discovered they could create a compelling virtual event program that engaged supporters, honored beneficiaries, and still raised critical funds with lower overhead costs.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: SOAR! Hosts Virtual Galas as Donations Pour In
In 2020, SOAR! was forced to cancel events due to social distancing restrictions and decided to create virtual galas with a virtual production partner.
We experienced a higher net profit for both of our virtual galas compared to in-person galas of previous years.

Thanks to GiveSmart, our virtual galas were successful financially (which was a big relief) and in quality (which was just as important to us). We experienced a higher net profit for both of our virtual galas compared to in-person galas of previous years.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"25%";s:16:"data_description";s:34:"increase in net profit for DC Gala";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"40%";s:16:"data_description";s:34:"increase in net profit for NY Gala";}}

Supporting Our Aging Religious (SOAR!) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to raising funds and grants to help Catholic congregations across the U.S. care for their elderly and infirm members. SOAR! holds two important fundraising galas each year, one in New York City and the other in Washington, D.C. Both galas have been a significant source of funding for SOAR! 's beneficiaries for the past 34 years. However, in 2020, SOAR! was forced to cancel both events due to COVID-19 social distancing restrictions. The SOAR! team decided to create virtual events in place of both galas and began searching for a virtual production partner.

The SOAR! team vetted ten different online fundraising platforms before choosing GiveSmart. The platform stood out for its robust virtual fundraising capabilities and expert support. SOAR! broadcast the live events in Washington, D.C. and New York City on separate event pages with text-to-donate instructions and a donation progress circle. Attendees could see their donation amount and name displayed on the screen, creating a live-giving experience. 

During the planning process, the SOAR! team decided it would be a mistake to try to imitate their usual in-person event and instead focused on creating a new program that highlights SOAR!'s mission while giving honorees an opportunity to accept their SOAR! awards in a stylish setting. Their primary focus was to present the religious communities of Sisters, Brothers, and Priests as the stars of the galas. SOAR! promoted both galas through social media, email marketing, and direct mail channels. SOAR! kept both programs to 40 minutes to keep attendees' attention, while their president, Sister Kathleen Lunsmann, emceed both events.

SOARS!’s New York City and Washington, D.C., galas were successful both financially and in quality. Their net profit (revenue minus expenses) for the 2020 SOAR! New York Awards Gala (a virtual gala) was approximately 40% higher than the 2019 New York Awards Gala (an in-person dinner). The 2020 D.C. Gala raised nearly the same amount of revenue as the 2019 DC Gala, with a 15% increase in net profit. The SOAR! team discovered they could create a compelling virtual event program that engaged supporters, honored beneficiaries, and still raised critical funds with lower overhead costs.

SOAR!, or Supporting Our Aging Religious, works to raise funds for Catholic congregations across the United States to support their retired and infirm Sisters, Brothers, and Priests. They also work to educate individuals about the retirement needs confronting the population’s aging religious leaders and develop a network of concerned individuals to respond to this need.

item.title: Berkeley Repertory Theatre: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Berkeley Rep raised nearly $550,000 through peer-to-peer and online fundraising, while maintaining sponsor support and building their artistic community.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Berkeley Rep's OVATION Gala Goes Virtual
Berkeley Rep maintains the momentum of existing peer-to-peer fundraising while honoring their artist community.
Once we set up the structure of our GiveSmart Event Page, our board members and volunteer fundraisers got really excited and thought: ‘Yes! We can really do this!
Once we set up the structure of our GiveSmart Event Page, our board members and volunteer fundraisers got really excited and thought: ‘Yes! We can really do this!

With only three weeks before Berkeley Rep’s biggest fundraiser, the “Party of the Year,” needed to be converted into a virtual event celebrating the theatre's resilience and its loyal patrons. They wanted to maintain the momentum of existing peer-to-peer fundraising while honoring their special guest and their artist community.

The theatre’s annual gala, “OVATION: Celebrate the Transformative Power of Theatre,” went virtual to honor actor, writer, and teacher Anna Deavere Smith. The event featured special tributes and a conversation between Anna, Artistic Director Johanna Pfaelzer, and Managing Director Susie Medak about the power of theatre to restore, inspire, and bring communities together. Berkeley Rep displayed the live and pre-recorded videos on its Event Page, along with their event sponsors, donor rewards, recipes for at-home cocktails, and a donor wall featuring a fundraising progress circle.

Overall, Berkeley Rep raised over $950,000, $400,000 of which was raised before pivoting to a virtual event. In the remaining three weeks, they raised nearly $550,000 through peer-to-peer and online fundraising, while maintaining sponsor support and building their artistic community.

Berkeley Rep has been creating ambitious theatre that entertains and challenges its audiences, provokes civic engagement, and inspires people to experience the world in new and surprising ways since 1968.

item.title: Society for Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science (SACNAS): YM Careers Success Story

SACNAS nearly doubled membership conversions and boosted resume uploads by 103% using targeted strategies.

The results have been fantastic, and they get better every year.

Our work with Momentive has been highly effective at showing student and graduating members the value of continued membership with SACNAS. The results have been fantastic, and they get better every year.
Communications and Membership Manager Society for Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science (SACNAS)

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bxs-megaphone";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Marketing services ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:10:"box-bx-cog";s:13:"feature_title";s:28:"Resume sweepstakes toolkit ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:18:"box-bxs-guitar-amp";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Recruitment guides ";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-fullscreen";s:13:"feature_title";s:27:"Internship board software ";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bx-laptop";s:13:"feature_title";s:20:"Job board software ";}}

SACNAS is a membership-based organization that fosters the success of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans. Their team knew that to attract the next generation of members, they needed to understand their student members better and deliver more of what those members wanted. By utilizing Momentive’s software and services to collect and analyze member data, then create multiple campaigns and offerings to drive membership conversions, SACNAS was able to encourage new grad resume uploads to their job board, and connect more members with industry employers.

SACNAS wanted to increase conversions of student memberships to professional memberships, as well as get new graduate members and employers more engaged with the SACNAS job board. 

SACNAS worked with us to create campaigns that promote SACNAS benefits to new grad members as well as drive more resume uploads to the SACNAS job board. They also built a recruitment guide to connect members with top industry employers.

SACNAS nearly doubled the conversions of student to professional memberships from 2018 to 2019. The SACNAS team also boosted resume uploads to their job board by 103% and distributed a career recruitment guide to 4,000 members.

SACNAS is a national organization dedicated to fostering the success of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans, from college students to professionals, in attaining advanced degrees, careers, and positions of leadership in STEM.
How SACNAS boosted conversions and job board engagement
SACNAS Career Platform Success with YM Careers

As UCA’s membership grew, managing various operational aspects, such as membership data, certification, and accreditation tracking, became increasingly complex. UCA needed a comprehensive system that could centralize their data, streamline member interactions, and offer customization to adapt to their evolving needs.   

They wanted a solution that would save time and money while integrating seamlessly with their existing Microsoft ecosystem. UCA also required a platform that was responsive to feature requests and offered easy data tracking and reporting.

With Cobalt’s solution in place, UCA was able to streamline operations and save both time and resources. The Engagement Dynamics platform enabled UCA to manage all of its membership, certification, and accreditation data in one centralized system, making it easier to track interactions, member application progress, and the overall customer experience.   

The integration with Microsoft products allowed for better reporting, enabling UCA to gain deeper insights into member engagement and operations.   

Additionally, the implementation of the universal cart feature significantly improved the user experience for UCA’s members, simplifying the process of making multiple transactions.   

Thanks to Cobalt’s responsiveness and commitment to ongoing improvement, UCA continues to benefit from a system that evolves alongside their needs, providing them with a scalable, efficient solution for their growing organization.

The Urgent Care Association (UCA) is a membership association for Urgent Care health and management professionals and clinics representing over 4,000 Urgent Care centers through the United States and abroad. UCA provides educational programs in clinical care and practice management, and maintains an active online presence and member community for the daily exchange of best practices. UCA’s members provide crucial services in communities across the country, including immediate care for non-emergency conditions, offering both convenience and high-quality care.

Developing an association management software (AMS) with a nuanced integration to the national member database and credit card processing system was something nobody had been able to achieve ten years ago. That hypothetical AMS would also need to be flexible enough to handle the myriad business rules of over 1,100 state and local associations.  

Since each of those associations had their own processes for things like collecting dues and other information or delivering education, it was too complex and expensive a prospect to develop unique vendor systems for every association. It was a perfect storm of many, many competing and specific requirements, the need for a complicated integration with an older system that housed all their data, and unparalleled flexibility to accommodate new users.

NAR’s ongoing commitment to understanding their end users’ needs is the most important ingredient in the recipe for this success story. They relentlessly press the executives at the 1,100 associations they serve to understand what those teams and members need to do their work every day.  

What do the individual members they serve expect and need? Those kinds of questions drove the innovation of an AMS that has been adopted by more REALTOR® associations than anything else on the market. With more than seven years of refinement and ongoing development, Cobalt built the AMS to be powered by Dynamics 365, and, so far, has created custom implementations for more than 160 of NAR’s key associations.

About Momentive Software   

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.  

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

National Association of REALTORS® 

With more than 1.3 million members, the National Association of REALTORS® (NAR) serves close to 1,200 local associations and boards as well as 54 state and territory associations of REALTORS®. More than 50% of state realtor associations (with hundreds of thousands of individual members) depend on the custom REALTORS® Association Management Cooperative (RAMCO) Dynamics 365-driven AMS platform.

item.title: Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB): Momentive Software Certification Case Study
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Working with Cobalt for over a decade has been a truly collaborative experience. Their ability to continuously customize and improve our system to meet our specific needs has made a significant impact on our operations. From seamless data integration to innovative product bundling, Cobalt’s solutions have empowered us to better serve our credentialing candidates and stakeholders.

Working with Cobalt for over a decade has been a truly collaborative experience. Their ability to continuously customize and improve our system to meet our specific needs has made a significant impact on our operations. From seamless data integration to innovative product bundling, Cobalt’s solutions have empowered us to better serve our credentialing candidates and stakeholders.

Over the years, PTCB’s business challenges have evolved significantly. Initially, they faced issues with handling high volumes of traffic during limited renewal windows, causing strain on both their systems and staff. It became clear that they needed a robust solution to manage these traffic spikes while maintaining smooth operations.   

Additionally, the credentialing process for pharmacy technicians differs from most other credential markets. Besides individual candidates, PTCB also needed to cater to large employers who sponsor their employees’ credentials. This unique requirement called for functionality that could efficiently handle high-volume credentials awarded to individuals yet funded by employers.

Through this decade-long partnership, PTCB and Cobalt have successfully streamlined many aspects of the credential management process. Engagement Dynamics empowered PTCB with:   

  • Scalable Traffic Management: System enhancements that enabled PTCB to handle high-traffic spikes during exam and renewal windows, ensuring a smooth experience for staff and certification candidates.   
  • Bundled Product Functionality: A custom enhancement that allowed PTCB to offer more relevant and strategic product recommendations during the purchasing process, leading to improved customer satisfaction and increased sales opportunities.   
  • Employer-Focused Features: Functionality designed to facilitate easy interactions between PTCB, credential candidates, and their employers, ensuring that large-scale credentials can be managed seamlessly.   
  • Seamless Integration with Testing Vendors and Badging Solutions: Integration with platforms like Pearson VUE and Credly, eliminating the need for manual test result imports and costly external integrations.   
  • Continuous Improvements: A close, ongoing collaboration between PTCB’s tech-savvy team and Cobalt ensures that the platform continuously evolves with new features and integrations to support PTCB’s mission and strategic goals.   

Cobalt’s flexibility with customizations, paired with the robust capabilities of Dynamics 365, has made PTCB’s credentialing processes more efficient and adaptable. This long-standing partnership highlights Cobalt’s dedication to evolving the system to align with PTCB’s needs, allowing them to focus on their mission of advancing medication safety through credentialing pharmacy technicians.

About Momentive Software   

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.  

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

The Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) advances medication safety by credentialing pharmacy technicians who are qualified to support pharmacists and patient care teams in all practice settings. There are over 300,000 active Certified Pharmacy Technicians (CPhTs), and their certificates can often be found proudly displayed in their pharmacies. This visible mark of patient care and safety reflects the high value placed on PTCB credentials across the industry. PTCB has been a Cobalt customer for over 10 years, using the Cobalt Engagement Dynamics platform to manage its highly customized credentialing management process.
