STS initially sought a new AMS (Association Management System) to replace their fragmented system, which lacked integration between their public-facing website and member portal. They needed a solution that could streamline data processes, support the growing needs of their worldwide community, and create a unified experience for members. Additionally, STS was looking for a cost-effective, secure way to manage their data and improve the overall functionality of their systems to meet the demands of a fast-evolving healthcare industry.

Partnering with Cobalt transformed STS’s member experience by introducing streamlined features like single sign-on (SSO), integrated webforms, and automated renewals. With a modern, scalable system, STS is ready to grow and adapt to the ever-evolving demands of their profession while strengthening connections within their expanding community.  

Moving to the cloud has also delivered tangible benefits for STS, including lower ongoing security costs and a more flexible, scalable infrastructure.  

Cobalt’s personalized approach to support has been vital for STS, offering tailored solutions that address their unique operational challenges. This partnership has not only improved response times but also alleviated the workload for their internal teams.  

Today, STS views Cobalt as a trusted partner, helping them innovate and thrive in their mission to support the global cardiothoracic surgery.

About Momentive Software   

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.  

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

The Society of Thoracic Surgeons (STS) is a professional organization dedicated to enhancing the quality and outcomes of cardiothoracic surgery around the world. With over 7,000 members worldwide, STS provides resources, education, and advocacy for cardiothoracic surgery professionals globally. a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:40:"Refusing to Replicate Their Old System ";s:16:"data_description";s:37:"meant new solutions for old problems ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:20:"Improved Reporting ";s:16:"data_description";s:26:"empowered their whole team";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:44:"Migrating to Microsoft Dynamics 365 Online ";s:16:"data_description";s:35:"gave them access to instant updates";}}

One of the biggest challenges, when FSI came onboard with Cobalt in 2016, was their old system. Their former database was messy. The data structure was broken and it was time-consuming for them to try and solve problems in the backend database. Even worse, after all that work, their reporting capabilities were still very limited.  

FSI knew they needed a new CRM partner and a reliable association management system that could streamline their access to data and reporting. Thankfully, they were committed to finding a new solution instead of just replicating old workflows and headaches.

As an association, membership renewals were another important focus of the new system. With time and testing, the Cobalt team was able to develop a more efficient process that saved the FSI staff time and energy and significantly improved their end users’ experience. Once again, they were committed to digging in and focusing on the nature of the problem, which gave the team the leeway and insights to come up with a creative new solution that benefited them and their members. 

The new reporting functionality has made a big difference for FSI. With their old system, all the reports had to be generated by the staff in the accounting department because nothing was intuitive or easy to use. Today, most of the FSI staff have the access and confidence to pull the information they need to do their day-to-day work.  

We were also able to implement some helpful new integration with their content management system. Through Cobalt’s Web Elements software, the entire FSI team gained the ability to embed self-service member portals on their current website. Their members get a seamless experience with consistent branding and interface design, and the FSI team was empowered to implement custom work on their own site without needing to call a vendor or developer.

About Momentive Software   

Momentive Software amplifies the impact of over 30,000 purpose-driven organizations in over 30 countries. Mission–driven organizations and associations rely on the company’s cloud-based software and services to solve their most critical challenges: engage the people they serve, simplify operations, and grow revenue.  

Built with reliability at the core and strategically focused on events, careers, fundraising, financials, and operations, our solutions suite is bound by a common purpose to serve the organizations that make our communities a better place to live.

The Financial Services Institute (FSI) is an association dedicated to ensuring everyone has access to affordable financial advice, products, and services. FSI has a small staff, but they serve more than 100 financial services companies with over 35,000 individual employees and members.

item.title: Bloom Our Youth: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The fundraising and donor management foundation that Bloom has established with GiveSmart, along with the growth they’ve worked hard to achieve, has set them up to realize their vision.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Bloom and Grow: Nonprofit Growth Strategies
Returning to GiveSmart helped Bloom Our Youth substantially grow their fundraising and awareness efforts.
They can help you find a solution that works best for you and your goals.

Given the things that are affecting us as an organization, it makes a huge difference knowing that whoever we're talking to [at GiveSmart] has nonprofit experience ... especially when you talk about donor experience and retention ... People on the GiveSmart team understand. It's people who know the technology piece AND the industry piece. They can help you find a solution that works best for you and your goals.
Development Associate at Bloom Our Youth

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:11:"$1 Million+";s:16:"data_description";s:21:"Raised with GiveSmart";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"1";s:16:"data_description";s:55:"Award-Winning Fundraising and Donor Management Platform";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"10+";s:16:"data_description";s:39:"NEW Recurring Giving Donors Every Month";}}

Back in the late 2010s, Bloom Our Youth had floundered with their event fundraising management tools, always returning to GiveSmart because it was easy and engaging for donors and volunteers to use, and it supported their exponential growth for their two distinguished events. As they’ve grown, they've looked into consolidating the tools they use for fundraising.   

The Bloom staff were using multiple credit card processors (with high fees) to process website donations. They also had a separate patchwork of tools for their partner-to-peer fundraising and program-specific fundraisers. The team knew they could save money and increase efficiency by using a single platform that could do more.

The expanded GiveSmart platform offered the tools they needed to save money and raise more. With customizable, embeddable forms and best-in-class peer-to-peer fundraising, the Bloom team was able to better engage with their support network.   

GiveSmart offered them far more than award-winning technology and one vendor, though. Bloom has a small staff and has found that the GiveSmart Community Roundtables and the connections made have offered them a network of like-minded, hard-working fundraisers who come together to brainstorm and share ideas. Bloom is also deeply appreciative of the GiveSmart staff they’ve worked with, who have first-hand nonprofit experience. It’s meaningful to the Bloom team that their chosen technology partner is not only familiar with the industry but also cares about their success.

Bloom Our Youth has been a GiveSmart customer for over three years. They use GiveSmart for all their fundraising campaigns and have found success ever since.  

It is a good thing they had familiarity with GiveSmart before the height of the pandemic, when their Bloom Closet program found itself overflowing with in-kind donations and saw increased awareness of their mission. They found themselves with willing volunteers and fundraisers, and the expanded GiveSmart platform allowed them to set up branded giving opportunities for these partners to use.   

Bloom already had all of the tools they needed to best support their growth. All donor information aggregation, reporting, and payment processing were handled by a single vendor, making it easy for their team to track, manage, and scale these initiatives.   

Bloom has also committed to fully serving all foster youth in Georgia within the next five to 10 years, a twofold increase from what they can currently handle. The fundraising and donor management foundation they have established with GiveSmart, along with the growth they’ve worked hard to achieve, has set them up to realize this vision.

Bloom strengthens foster children and empowers the families who care for them. Bloom connects the community to evidence-based, outcome-driven programs that support and strengthen the lives of vulnerable children and their families.   

Through their foster care agency and support network, Bloom has worked tirelessly for over 30 years to provide housing, foster parent training, a resource center, and more critical programming to support tens of thousands of vulnerable children.

item.title: St. Augustine High School: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Needing a way to reach alumni online, St. Augustine began searching for a fundraising platform that supported online and in-person giving.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: St. Augustine Supports Giving with GiveSmart
St. Augustine High School raised record donations for Give Purple Day while boosting engagement with younger alumni.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:8:"$250,634";s:16:"data_description";s:12:"total raised";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"656";s:16:"data_description";s:32:"total gifts received from donors";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"$381";s:16:"data_description";s:31:"average gift size for the event";}}

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:20:"box-bxs-donate-heart";s:13:"feature_title";s:10:"Donations ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:22:"box-bxs-user-rectangle";s:13:"feature_title";s:93:"all-in-one fundraising platform that supports both digital and traditional marketing channels";}}

St. Augustine High School is a college preparatory school for young men in grades 8-12 in New Orleans. When St. Augustine launched their first-ever day of giving, Give Purple Day, they were looking for a powerful way to engage and motivate younger alumni to participate. They created a Young Alumni Challenge in which corporate sponsors doubled each gift, but they still needed a way to reach alumni online. St. Augustine’s Director of Marketing and Public Relations, Melissa Duplantier, began searching for a fundraising platform that supported online and in-person giving.

Melissa ultimately chose [GiveSmart] because it is an all-in-one fundraising platform supporting digital and traditional marketing channels. She included links to online giving pages in each email for faculty, staff, parents, alumni, and supporters. On Instagram, she included text-to-donate instructions, as they didn’t have enough followers to qualify for the “swipe up” feature. They launched a live event thermometer on campus, that displayed donors’ names and their contribution amounts, encouraging friendly competition.

With the help of [GiveSmart], St. Augustine High School raised a record number of donations for Give Purple Day, all while increasing engagement with younger alumni. In addition to raising over $200,000 for their Give Purple Day, St. Augustine could also collect more donor data and insights than ever before. When Hurricane Katrina hit in 2005, St. Augustine lost many of its files, making it difficult to contact former supporters again. Now, their marketing department can reach alumni and past supporters through digital and traditional channels and gain better insight.

St. Augustine High School in New Orleans is a private, all-boys Catholic college-prep school founded by Josephite priests in 1951, known for developing leaders with strong academics, Christian values (Gratia Est Vita - Grace is Life), and famous programs like the "Marching 100," focusing on academic excellence, music, athletics, and service for young men of all backgrounds.

item.title: St. Saviour High School: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The Saint Saviour staff was already using a fundraising software program, but wanted one that was more affordable, easier to use, and with more advanced fundraising and engagement capabilities.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: St. Saviour Surpasses Athletic & Science Fundraising Goals
Individual online giving pages from GiveSmart allowed Saint Saviour to raise awareness for new initiatives to expand their science and athletic programs.
What drew me to [GiveSmart] was the ease of use. We don’t have an IT department, so I needed a platform I could set up all on my own.

What drew me to [GiveSmart] was the ease of use. We don’t have an IT department, so I needed a platform I could set up all on my own. After watching a few [training] videos, I was able to create mobile giving pages and set up text-to-donate. Other fundraising software platforms require too much technical expertise.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"188";s:16:"data_description";s:31:"total gifts received from donor";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"$17,458";s:16:"data_description";s:31:"total raised through this event";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:6:"$92.86";s:16:"data_description";s:18:"average gift size ";}}

Saint Saviour High School, an all-girls Catholic High School in Brooklyn, New York, wanted to raise funds for its annual appeal and for special funding for its science and athletic programs. The Saint Saviour staff was already using a fundraising software program but wanted one that was more affordable, easier to use, and with more advanced fundraising and engagement capabilities.

With no IT department, the Saint Saviour staff needed a fundraising platform that required minimal technical expertise. After watching [GiveSmart] videos and working with the platform, they created sleek online giving pages for both of Saint Saviour's science and athletic programs. Peer-to-peer fundraising pages give staff, alumni, and students their own way to reach their respective communities and share the excitement of their fundraising thermometer.

With [GiveSmart's] expert guidance, Saint Saviour surpassed expectations for the athletic and science programs and better connected with new and existing donors. Individual online giving pages allowed Saint Saviour to raise awareness for new initiatives to expand both programs. Peer-to-peer fundraising features helped them tap into a new donor base, specifically through social media. Saint Saviour also leveraged mobile messaging to communicate with parents, sending text message updates and reminders for holidays, casual dress days, field trips, snow days, and more.

Since 1917, Saint Saviour has nurtured driven young women from across New York City and beyond, preparing them to make a meaningful impact on the world. Today, that mission is more vital than ever, as we continue to empower our students to lead with purpose and compassion.

item.title: The American Association of Orthopaedic Surgeons’ Political Action Committee: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Beginning with a goal of $90,000 and ending with a total collected of $116,974, the integration of text-to-donate at The Orthopaedic PAC’s event was a huge success. This case study is a perfect example of how the power of technology can elevate engagement and participation to whole new levels.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Expanding and Exceeding Expectations with GiveSmart
By expanding their approach within their boundaries, The Orthopaedic PAC was able to deliver results that exceeded those of traditional methods.
The American Association of Orthopaedic Surgeons’ Political Action Committee (The Orthopaedic PAC)
We love the results we can see in real-time… everyone is into instant gratification.
We love the results we can see in real-time… everyone is into instant gratification.
Political Affairs Senior Manager Political Action Committee of the American Association of Orthopaedic Surgeons

The American Association of Orthopaedic Surgeons’ Political Action Committee, or The Orthopaedic PAC, needed to raise their total donations at their annual meeting, but was bound by strict practices. Without the ability to reach out to potential donors beyond their members and due to special bank security requirements, they needed a solution that could foster more donations while still playing by their rules.

Through [GiveSmart], The Orthopaedic PAC set up a gated text-to-donate page that their members could easily access. They promoted their keywords PAC to 71777 or AAOS to 41444 at member-only events and generated excitement around the option to give in this fashion.  

At their annual meeting, they showcased a real-time thermometer that displayed each donor as they made donations to The Orthopaedic PAC. They also soon realized that [GiveSmart] offered peer-to-peer fundraising campaigns, something they had not initially planned to use. After successfully setting up text-to-donate and peer-to-peer campaigns, the only problem was choosing whom to contact. The [GiveSmart] app made it easy to target specific people in the AAOS database, so only those who could be contacted were contacted.

Beginning with a goal of $90,000 and ending with a total collected of $116,974, the integration of text-to-donate at the event was a huge success. By expanding their approach within their boundaries, they were able to deliver results that exceeded those of traditional methods. They continue to use [GiveSmart] in innovative ways, such as having audience members text to submit questions to a guest speaker. This is a perfect example of how the power of technology can elevate engagement and participation on whole new levels.

The American Association of Orthopaedic Surgeons Political Action Committee, or “The Orthopaedic PAC,” acts as the single national political action committee in Washington, DC, representing orthopaedic surgeons in front of Congress. They represent the best interests of both surgeons and patients in the political spectrum.

item.title: Black Tie & Boxing: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: After incorporating GiveSmart’s mobile, online, and live-event fundraising tools into the Black Tie & Boxing gala, year-over-year revenue increased substantially.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Black Tie & Boxing for JDRF: Fundraising Event Success
By partnering with GiveSmart, Black Tie & Boxing attracted new supporters and event attendees and raised significantly more donations.
The platform proved to be dynamic, user-friendly and will take your event to the next level.

Although our team was hesitant to sign up with GiveSmart, because it was something new and different, we’re glad we did. The platform proved to be dynamic, user-friendly and will take your event to the next level.

a:5:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:17:"box-bx-mobile-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:31:"mobile and online giving tools ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:11:"box-bx-text";s:13:"feature_title";s:15:"text-to-donate ";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:21:"box-bx-calendar-event";s:13:"feature_title";s:22:"live event thermometer";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bx-transfer";s:13:"feature_title";s:24:"peer-to-peer fundraising";}s:6:"item-4";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:23:"box-bxl-facebook-circle";s:13:"feature_title";s:13:"Facebook Live";}}

Black Tie & Boxing is an annual fundraising event consisting of a cocktail hour and formal dinner alongside a series of live, sanctioned kickboxing/MMA-style matches. As a West Tennessee Healthcare Foundation fund, proceeds from the Black Tie & Boxing event go towards the Juvenile Diabetes Research Foundation (JDRF). Year over year, the event proved successful, but the Black Tie & Boxing team knew it had the potential to reach more members of their community and raise more for Type 1 Diabetes.

Black Tie & Boxing partnered with GiveSmart to incorporate mobile and online giving tools, including text-to-donate, a live event thermometer, peer-to-peer fundraising, and Facebook Live. The Black Tie team launched a Fight Club-themed peer-to-peer fundraising campaign, inviting fighters in the community to sign up and train for the event. Fighters shared their peer-to-peer fundraising page with family and friends and even attended a pre-event weigh-in at a local brewpub. 

In addition, text-to-donate instruction cards were placed on all the tables and general admission seats at the event, while Black Tie displayed a live event thermometer on-screen next to speakers. This made it easier for attendees to submit donations in any amount without cash or check. The sold-out event was also broadcast via Facebook Live to include supporters who could not attend.

After incorporating GiveSmart’s mobile, online, and live-event fundraising tools into the Black Tie & Boxing gala, year-over-year revenue increased substantially, and they raised over $190,000. With peer-to-peer fundraising, fighters shared the event to help sell out the event. The addition of text-to-donate and a live event thermometer doubled donations compared to previous years. The extra revenue generated using the platform and tools allows the Black Tie team to advertise more widely in the coming years. By partnering with GiveSmart, Black Tie & Boxing attracted new supporters and event attendees (both on and offline) and raised significantly more year-over-year.

Black Tie & Boxing is a fund of the West Tennessee Healthcare Foundation. WHF created the Black Tie & Boxing fund to benefit Juvenile Diabetes Research Foundation in its mission to fight against Type 1 Diabetes. The Juvenile Diabetes Research Foundation advocates for policies that accelerate access to new therapies and provides a support network for millions of people around the world impacted by T1D.

item.title: Southern Wesleyan University: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The Southern Wesleyan team was challenged to create a Week of Giving campaign that could help students facing financial and spiritual hardship while also being sensitive to constituents who may not be able to give.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Southern Wesleyan's Week of Giving Goes Online
Southern Wesleyan University utilizes GiveSmart technology to support its annual Week of Giving campaign.
During this uncertain time, we wanted to create a unique week of giving campaign that brought students, alumni and staff together, but also indicated our need for support.
During this uncertain time, we wanted to create a unique week of giving campaign that brought students, alumni and staff together, but also indicated our need for support.
Associate Vice President, Southern Wesleyan University

Each April, Southern Wesleyan University, a private Christian university in South Carolina, holds its Week of Giving. By the spring of 2020, the Southern Wesleyan staff knew they’d have to package their annual giving campaign to provide relief to students impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic. As a Christ-centered organization, the school sought a way to help students facing financial and spiritual hardship while also being sensitive to constituents who may not be able to give. The Southern Wesleyan team was challenged to create a Week of Giving campaign that could do both.

Tony Payne, Southern Wesleyan’s Associate Vice President for Development, wanted a centralized online place to host details on the 2020 Week of Giving. He worked with the GiveSmart team to set up an Event Page for the campaign. They worked together to create a campaign web page that respected the constituent’s possible inability to give while also indicating the continued financial need for Southern Wesleyan University community members. The Event Page links to mental health, work and career, and spiritual and community resources. In addition to their email marketing and mobile messaging channels, the Southern Wesleyan team shared their Event Page on social media with #SWUCares.

Despite social distancing restrictions, Southern Wesleyan raised $22,024 for its Week of Giving campaign, almost the same amount they raised in 2019 ($22,306). In addition to the emergency funds raised for students displaced by the COVID-19 pandemic, Southern Wesleyan provided spiritual, health, career, and media-enrichment resources. With help from GiveSmart, the team quickly pivoted their annual week of giving into a meaningful week of caring.
Southern Wesleyan University was founded in 1906 by what is now the Wesleyan Church. The university offers 35 major areas of study for undergraduates, along with graduate and doctoral degrees.

item.title: The Steve & Marjorie Harvey Foundation: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Motivated by donations, Steve Harvey bikes 4 hours per day, 5 days per week, to raise money for his mentorship foundation.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Virtual Bike Ride for the Steve & Marjorie Harvey Foundation
Text-to-give donations skyrocket for The Harvey Foundation
As I sit there pedaling on the bike and see names scroll on the screen, I can see everybody who gives. I keep it in front of me because it motivates me.
As I sit there pedaling on the bike and see names scroll on the screen, I can see everybody who gives. I keep it in front of me because it motivates me.

Steve Harvey announced the Steve Harvey Ride for Charity fundraising campaign on his nationally syndicated radio show, The Steve Harvey Morning Show. As a promotion for this campaign and throughout the month-long ride, Mr. Harvey promised to exercise on a stationary bicycle, treadmill, or elliptical in his Chicago studio for the duration of his daily radio show — four hours a day, five days a week, to raise awareness and money for his mentoring program benefiting young men and women. While he exercised, he planned to encourage supporters to donate to his text-to-give campaign. He promised that 100% of all donations raised would go directly to the cause.

The Steve & Marjorie Harvey Foundation used GiveSmart to set up a text-to-give campaign. Mr. Harvey instructed listeners to donate by using the text-to-give keyword STEVE to 91999 or by going to harveyfoundation.com. They also customized the campaign’s mobile-friendly online donation form to show $1 as the lowest gift amount to encourage gifts of any size. As donations came in, they appeared on a live fundraising thermometer displayed in the studio, allowing Mr. Harvey to announce gift amounts and thank donors in real time. Every donor who texted the keyword was also subscribed to receive text message updates from their foundation.

The Steve Harvey Ride for Charity was a huge success. Exactly 63,679 donors donated $1,013,102 online and via the text-to-give keyword through Facebook Live, Periscope, and radio. GiveSmart’s follow-up text solution resulted in the fulfillment of 86% of text donations. This money went towards the mentoring program for at-risk youths that Steve Harvey captains.

The Steve & Marjorie Harvey Foundation provides nationwide programming & mentoring camps, global service experiences, and scholarships to ensure the needs of the whole child are met, fostering excellence in the lives of underserved youth and helping them pursue higher education.

item.title: Advocates for Children: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Text-to-donate enabled Advocates for Children to fundraise during the COVID-19 pandemic, getting the crucial financial support they needed.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Pivoting to a Virtual Event During COVID-19
Advocates for Children couldn’t hold their annual fundraiser during the COVID-19 pandemic, so they pivoted to a virtual event instead.
Using GiveSmart allowed us to easily contact ticket holders and donors before and after our scheduled event.
Using GiveSmart allowed us to easily contact ticket holders and donors before and after our scheduled event.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"$13,569";s:16:"data_description";s:58:"raised through peer-to-peer fundraising ahead of the event";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"470";s:16:"data_description";s:12:"tickets sold";}}

Advocates for Children, a nonprofit dedicated to treating and preventing child abuse in all forms, holds one of its annual fundraising events each April during Child Abuse Awareness Month. For April 2020, the Advocates for Children team planned a Gourmet Graze & Raise gala instead of their usual live auction with dinner.

The Advocates for Children team worked for seven months to develop a new event concept featuring five local chefs competing for the title of “Best Chef.” However, two weeks before the competition, the event had to be postponed due to COVID-19. Advocates for Children decided to move their in-person event to the fall and hold a virtual version of the Gourmet Graze and Raise on the original date. The team then needed a way to inform their sponsors and supporters who had already contributed and purchased over $10,000 in tickets of the event changes. 

Renee Shields, Advocates for Children’s Development & Special Projects Associate, reached out to GiveSmart for advice on pivoting portions of the Gourmet Graze & Raise gala into a virtual event. Renee used the information that was already part of their fundraising campaign to quickly create an Event Page. 

Advocates for Children president and CEO Rachel L. Castillo then went on Facebook Live from her home on the originally scheduled date of the Gourmet Graze & Raise event. She displayed the Event Page on the television behind her and announced the winning chef and team, determined by the amount raised through peer-to-peer fundraising. Thanking the event committee and sponsors, Rachel also explained how Advocates for Children responded to COVID-19 and why they still urgently needed support. She asked viewers to use text-to-donate, sharing that all who did would be entered to win a giveaway from the participating local restaurants.

With the help of their GiveSmart, Advocates for Children successfully transitioned part of their in-person fundraising event into a virtual one. Text-to-donate software made it easy for viewers to submit donations from wherever they were watching. Those who did not attend the live stream could still view a recording, as the video was directly uploaded to their Event Page. 

With shelter in place and children not at school, there was an increased risk for child abuse and the financial strain many families experience. Because of the virtual event, Advocates for Children could communicate with their donor base that they needed their support now.

This also gave the team a chance to thank the event committee, sponsors, and local chefs who participated.

Advocates for Children provides support and services to children who have been the victims of abuse or neglect. With an array of programs and services, Advocates for Children provides evidence-driven, national and statewide programs that focus on working directly with young people and their families.

item.title: Blossom IDD: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how Blossom IDD boosted donor engagement and streamlined fundraising using GiveSmart’s platform in this detailed success story.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: From 2024's event to 2025, my stress went from a 10 to a 2. The ability to enter… just a quick paddle raise… was like night and day. We really wanted to invest in doing a smooth event. GiveSmart set us up for so much success.

From 2024's event to 2025, my stress went from a 10 to a 2. The ability to enter… just a quick paddle raise… was like night and day. We really wanted to invest in doing a smooth event. GiveSmart set us up for so much success.
Finance and Development Director, Blossom IDD

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"53%";s:16:"data_description";s:57:"of auction items went at or above Fair Market Value (FMV)";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"16";s:16:"data_description";s:34:"sponsorships granted via GiveSmart";}}

Blossom IDD was looking for a more reliable and easy-to-use fundraising solution to host Growing Through Music, its largest fundraiser of the year. As a small staff, growing nonprofit, Blossom IDD found that its previous technology relied heavily on manual processes, making events unnecessarily stressful. They were hoping for smoother staff and donor experiences to ultimately grow the event.

Their staff sought recommendations for fundraising event technology, and, alongside their volunteers, other local nonprofits overwhelmingly recommended GiveSmart.

Blossom IDD connected with GiveSmart to understand how the technology could lower event-day stress, increase visibility into real-time fundraising, and improve the donor experience. After deciding on GiveSmart, Blossom IDD worked with its Implementation team, “with an amazing trainer who was almost psychic... She was really able to answer and anticipate our questions.”

In Summer 2025, they hosted Growing Through Music through GiveSmart for the first time.

Using GiveSmart for Growing Through Music streamlined event execution, reduced staff stress, improved donor and bidder experiences, and offered clear, real-time fundraising visibility (especially during the critical paddle raise), eliminating wasted hours previously spent reconciling revenue and reporting.

Robust training and responsive support gave the team confidence, while flexible fundraising tools positioned Blossom IDD to raise more funds, reach more of the community, and scale their programs.

Blossom IDD began as one family’s effort to support a young woman to build an independent, connected adult life and grew into the nonprofit it is today, serving families across Ozaukee County, WI., and beyond. Blossom supports adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities by providing guidance, community, and life-building opportunities that foster connection, purpose, and choice.
Blossom IDD Boosts Fundraising with GiveSmart
Discover how Blossom IDD boosted donor engagement and streamlined fundraising using GiveSmart’s platform in this detailed success story.

item.title: Birch Family Services: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: By creating a virtual fundraising event, the Birch Team celebrated its 45th anniversary and raised over $366,000, with $83,000 of that from peer-to-peer fundraising alone.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: How Birch Family Services Surpassed Fundraising Goals
Pivoting to a virtual campaign during COVID-19, Birch Family Services surpassed their fundraising goal for their 45th Anniversary.

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:21:"box-bx-calendar-event";s:13:"feature_title";s:11:"Event Page ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:11:"box-bx-text";s:13:"feature_title";s:14:"text-to-donate";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:15:"box-bx-transfer";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"peer-to-peer fundraising page";}s:6:"item-3";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:16:"box-bx-share-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:35:"email and social templates to share";}}

In 2020, Birch Family Services, a nonprofit organization dedicated to helping individuals with autism and developmental disabilities, celebrated its 45th anniversary. However, when the COVID-19 pandemic limited in-person fundraising events, Birch Family Services needed an alternative to their typical spring fundraising event to fundraise and celebrate their anniversary. The Birch Team knew they could not forgo this vital opportunity to fundraise and celebrate their milestone.

The Birch Team created Birch@45, a 45-day virtual campaign comprising a 5K Challenge at the Celebrating Today, Building Tomorrow gala. They hosted Birch@45 on a GiveSmart Event Page with all essential event details, including event honorees, links to registration, donation, and sponsorship forms, text-to-donate instructions, and a donation progress circle showing their fundraising results in real-time. Also included were links to a peer-to-peer fundraising page, allowing supporters to sign up for the 5K and raise funds through their social networks. 

Additionally, the organization provided peer-to-peer fundraisers with email and social templates to share Birch@45 with their friends and family. Solomon Schecter’s Development Officer, Sherri Pliskin, was the first to hear of MobileCause. She was impressed by the platform after seeing another nonprofit organization display a MobileCause live fundraising thermometer at their event, and decided that Solomon Schecter needed one for their upcoming Annual Ner Tamid Gala. Hosting between 200-300 attendees, Ner Tamid is Solomon’s annual fundraising gala for parents, students, staff and supporters of the school. After much research and comparison, Sherri chose the MobileCause platform for the fundraising thermometer’s ease of use and donors' ability to give effortlessly from their seats, in any amount, via a text-to-donate keyword and shortcode.

By creating a virtual fundraising event, the Birch Team celebrated its 45th anniversary and raised over $366,000. The peer-to-peer fundraising portion of their campaign was incredibly successful. Initially, the Birch Team’s goal for peer-to-peer fundraising was just $25,000, but after they quickly surpassed that, they aimed for $35,000, then $45,000, and ultimately raised over $83,000. Engagement amongst peer-to-peer fundraisers was higher than in past campaigns, as it was easy for participants to share their peer-to-peer pages through email and social media. Birch Family Services crushed its goals with its first-ever virtual fundraising event, helping to reach new donors and increase engagement among existing supporters.

Birch Family Services provides children, adolescents, and adults with autism and developmental disabilities across New York City with opportunities to reach their fullest potential. They offer individualized education and community services programs for individuals ages 3-21 and comprehensive, practical, supportive services for both adults and children.

item.title: Genesis Community Health: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Genesis Community Health needed a way to know who registered for their event, provide them a meal, and showcase their entertainment online.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Genesis Community Health Shifts to Virtual Just 30 Days Before Event
Genesis Community Health converted their in-person HGTV Boise Boys event into a fully virtual experience in just 30 days.
Thanks to GiveSmart’s Event Page and its easy-to-use modules, we were able to put our event together quickly and in a way that our staff who aren’t tech savvy could really understand.

When the COVID-19 outbreak began in early 2020, many nonprofits, such as Genesis Community Health, were forced to halt their in-person fundraising events. Genesis Community Health converted their in-person dinner and meet-and-greet with HGTV’s Boise Boys into an entirely virtual event just 30 days before the event date. They needed a way to know who registered, provide a meal, and showcase their entertainment online.

Genesis turned their in-person event into a virtual one and still provided a meal and a live Q&A with HGTV’s Boise Boys, Luke Caldwell and Clint Robertson. The organization reached out to Chick-fil-A to request gift cards for attendees. Genesis asked guests to “purchase” a free ticket so they could track who would be attending and reach out to major donors who had not registered. The ticket form included the option for supporters to submit a question to the Boise Boys and request a Chick-fil-A gift card to use on the event night to have a meal while they watched the virtual event. They secured their special guests by sending a request to HGTV corporate, and the two stars agreed due to a shared vision of transforming lives (and houses). 

Along with the dedicated GiveSmart team, Genesis Community Health created their Event Page with updated details on their virtual fundraiser. Guests who registered online by purchasing a free ticket could opt to receive a gift card in the mail to be used for a meal during the event. This also allowed the organization to contact major donors with a personal invite if they hadn’t already registered. Guests were also allowed to submit questions in advance to the Boise Boys to help create a live feel for the festivities. 

On the day of the fundraiser, Genesis Community Health went live from a local church studio on their Event Page using YouTube. They showcased their progress circle and donor wall prominently both online and during the event to encourage more giving. Videos during the event highlighted their mission, services, interviews with beneficiaries, and tours of their community facilities. Genesis CEO Steven D. Davis spoke on behalf of the organization, making a passionate appeal and highlighting their text-to-donate feature, which allows supporters to give from anywhere. He also thanked and recognized donors and sponsors, which helped inspire giving. The real highlight was when the Boise Boys came to the studio, answered guests’ questions, and shared stories of their connection to and admiration of Genesis Community Health.

Through the convenience and ease of GiveSmart’s online fundraising tools, Genesis Community Health raised over $35,000 from 173 donors with a fun and unique virtual fundraising event that over 500 guests viewed both within and outside of their Idaho community. They saw a 73% increase in attendees and grossed more funds than their in-person benefit the previous year. Thanks to the virtual event, Genesis Community Health can continue providing much-needed medical services to those at risk during the pandemic.

Genesis Community Health’s mission is to provide transitional primary health care, social and spiritual support, to low-income, uninsured individuals in Idaho’s Treasure Valley, as an expression of the love of Jesus Christ.

item.title: Independent Insurance Agents of North Carolina: Path LMS Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how Independent Insurance Agents of NC improved training efficiency with Momentive Software’s LMS. Learn key outcomes & software benefits.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Independent Insurance Agents of North Carolina
Path LMS has been the least painful implementation we’ve ever done. The support team is responsive, collaborative, and open to feedback—it truly feels like a partnership.

We knew we were buying Path LMS not just for today, but for where we wanted to be in the next three to five years. Momentive gives us the functionality to keep evolving our programs as our members’ needs change.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"40 ";s:16:"data_description";s:7:"staff ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:5:"100+ ";s:16:"data_description";s:20:"member organizations";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"1000 ";s:16:"data_description";s:61:"Independent insurance agencies and partner branch locations ";}}

The Independent Insurance Agents of North Carolina (IIANC) is a professional association representing independent insurance agencies across North Carolina. Looking to advance advocacy, professional development, networking and community, IIANC launched Path LMS in December 2024, as part of its broader “InsurAcademy” initiative, designed to train and license new insurance professionals while providing continuous development for agency staff.

As IIANC prepared to launch new workforce development and training programs, leadership quickly realized their legacy LMS could not support the level of innovation and scale they envisioned. IIANC needed a more robust platform to power a growing hybrid learning initiative to blend live webinars, on-demand coursework, and self-directed learning. At the same time, IIANC sought a solution capable of scaling beyond North Carolina to support future partnerships with other state associations and external learners. Deeper integration with their AMS was also critical to streamline learner data, reporting, and operational efficiency. Additionally, they required the ability to create multiple branded learning portals so member agencies could deliver training directly to their own staff under their own organizational brand. 

After evaluating several LMS vendors, IIANC selected Path LMS for its flexibility, scalability, and member-centric experience. The platform enabled each agency, and even partner state associations, to launch custom-branded learning environments, empowering members to share training seamlessly with their teams. Self-service tools allow agencies to upload content, assign courses, and run reports independently, reducing administrative burden. IIANC is also excited about implementing role-based learning paths, now structured into nine distinct tracks, including a new “Benefits from Partners” pathway, ensuring relevant, career-aligned development. With seamless AMS integration and responsive, partnership-driven support from Momentive Software, IIANC gained a future-ready learning ecosystem designed to evolve alongside its strategic growth plans.

Organizational Impact 

Following the implementation of Path LMS, IIANC experienced rapid expansion, extending its LMS offering to an additional state within just three months of launch, and another seven additional states in the first quarter of 2026. The platform’s scalable architecture enabled IIANC to introduce white-labeled portals for sister associations and strategic partners, creating a new driver for both non-dues revenue and member engagement. 

Operationally, the organization streamlined course management, reporting, and member onboarding processes, reducing administrative complexity, and improving internal efficiency. Leadership described the overall transition and implementation as “the least painful” of any major technology initiative, highlighting both the platform’s usability and the strength of its support partnership. 

Learner Impact 

The learner experience has been equally strong with Path LMS. Mentor sessions achieved an NPS of 85, while agency training and courses overall earned NPS scores of 75, demonstrating consistently high satisfaction across program types. Learners frequently describe the platform as “very easy to navigate,” citing smooth course delivery and a clear, intuitive structure. 

By combining live webinars, on-demand coursework, and self-directed learning paths within a single ecosystem, IIANC now delivers a flexible learning experience that accommodates varying schedules and professional development needs across its member agencies.

The Independent Insurance Agents of North Carolina (IIANC) is a professional trade association representing independent insurance agencies across North Carolina. It provides education, advocacy, and business resources to help member agents serve clients effectively and maintain high ethical and professional standards.
Independent Insurance Agents of NC | LMS Success & Results
Discover how Independent Insurance Agents of NC improved training efficiency with Momentive Software’s LMS. Learn key outcomes & software benefits.

item.title: Talk School: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how Talk School increased fundraising & donor engagement using GiveSmart’s all-in-one event & fundraising platform.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: It didn't require a lot of training to figure out what to do. And anytime we had an issue, we were able to get an answer pretty quickly, which is pretty rare.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:15:"2x ticket price";s:16:"data_description";s:30:"average total giving per guest";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"1";s:16:"data_description";s:22:"successful Giving Week";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:1:"0";s:16:"data_description";s:40:"checkout issues reported during the gala";}}

Talk School serves students with significant speech and language disorders. Many students have autism and other developmental delays, and they all benefit from Talk School’s therapeutic, academic environment. As the only school of its kind in Pennsylvania, fundraising is critical to closing the gap between district funding and the cost of providing its students with what they truly need.  

As a small team without a dedicated development staff member, Talk School needed a fundraising platform that could keep everyone aligned without requiring extensive training or a designated expert to manage it.

To address this challenge, Talk School connected with GiveSmart to bring its events and fundraising into a single, centralized platform. Without a donor CRM or dedicated advancement staff, they needed a platform that was intuitive enough for anyone on the team to navigate and that also included a dedicated, responsive support team for when questions arose. After a check-in call that surfaced features they hadn't yet explored, Talk School expanded its use of GiveSmart from event management into active fundraising campaigns.   

Thanks to this expanded approach, the team was able to monitor ticket sales, donations, and auction items in real time with no learning curve.

Using GiveSmart for both their gala and Giving Week campaign gave Talk School's lean team visibility, consistency, and confidence. Checkout at the gala was seamless. With just 30 minutes to pack up and clear the venue, a smooth checkout process made all the difference. While their Giving Week campaign finished just short of its goal, the ability to look back across events, track auction data, and automate donor acknowledgments replaced hours of manual reconciliation.  

Looking ahead, with unlimited campaigns, matching gift tools, and offline donation capture all in one place, Talk School is positioned to consolidate its donor data, reduce its reliance on spreadsheets, and grow its fundraising strategically, including a spring giving event already on the horizon.

Talk School’s mission is to serve a unique and worthy community — students with severe speech and language differences who possess the skills to embark on the journey of communication and literacy development. Their model provides intensive, integrated therapeutic interventions within a comprehensive, supportive academic environment.
Talk School Fundraising Success Story with GiveSmart
Discover how Talk School increased fundraising & donor engagement using GiveSmart’s all-in-one event & fundraising platform.

item.title: National Shooting Sports Foundation/ConvExx SHOT Show: Momentive Event Management Software Success Story
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how NSSF & ConvExx used Core-Apps event technology to unlock new revenue streams at a sold-out trade show with no room to physically expand.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: National Shooting Sports Foundation/ConvExx SHOT Show

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:98:"App sales from sponsorship opportunities generated a 169% increase in revenue over the prior year.";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:188:"Momentive Event Management Software technology made it easy for exhibitors to see the value of enhancements, advertising, and sponsorship opportunities as they purchased their booth space.";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:138:"SHOT Show Management worked with Momentive to make an event website, mobile app, and wayfinders — a one-stop shop for their exhibitors. ";}}

The Shooting, Hunting, and Outdoor Trade Show, owned by the National Shooting Sports Foundation and managed by ConvExx, is a well-known, critically acclaimed expo and conference that has been sold out for the past several years.

Consistently selling out is a great problem to have, but unfortunately, running at capacity leaves only a few options for increased revenue generation potential. The SHOT Show is held at the Sands Convention Center in Las Vegas, where there is no room to expand. 

One option for generating revenue is digital sponsorships through an Exhibitor Dashboard. SHOT Show Management wanted to make the three digital mediums – event website, mobile app, and wayfinders — a one-stop shop for their exhibitors. The exhibitors appreciated having one dashboard to upload and manage everything. 

To help promote the purchase, SHOT Show management presented the opportunities graphically through screenshots of the website and app. They wanted to give the exhibitors a visual of what to expect when they made the purchase.

Innovative technology and achievement do not have to be anything fancy or earth-shaking to be successful and innovative. In this case, designing a system/process to help better display available upgrades and putting everything in one place was simple, but it achieved great success for the SHOT Show. 

The use of Momentive’s conference app  technology made it easy for exhibitors to see the value of enhancements, advertising, and sponsorship opportunities as they purchased their booth space. The new sales flow generated a 169% increase in revenue over the prior year.

National Shooting Sports Foundation/ConvExx SHOT Show

The Shooting, Hunting, and Outdoor Trade Show (SHOT Show) is the largest and most comprehensive trade show for all professionals involved with the shooting sports, hunting and law enforcement industries. It is the world’s premier exposition of combined firearms, ammunition, law enforcement, cutlery, outdoor apparel, optics and related products and services. The SHOT Show attracts buyers from all 50 states and more than 100 countries.
How NSSF & ConvExx Boosted Trade Show Revenue with Core-Apps
Learn how NSSF & ConvExx used Core-Apps event technology to unlock new revenue streams at a sold-out trade show with no room to physically expand.
