item.title: Women’s Cancer Resource Center: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The [GiveSmart] peer-to-peer fundraising pages enabled the Women’s Cancer Resource Center to leverage itssupporters’ social networks to expand itsreach to new donors and communities.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Women's Cancer Resource Center Fundraises Through Pandemic
The Women’s Cancer Resource Center needed to pivot its Swim a Mile for Women with Cancer event to adhere to COVID-19 social distancing requirements.
The pandemic has created an opportunity for nonprofits to think differently about their fundraising programs and how to use technology to strategically drive engagement and increase revenue.

The pandemic has created an opportunity for nonprofits to think differently about their fundraising programs and how to use technology to strategically drive engagement and increase revenue. Technology has enabled us to broaden our reach for participation and fundraising. Just as we now serve clients who are not physically able to get to our center, we can also engage our supporters who have been previously unable to participate due to distance.
Executive Director, Women’s Cancer Resource Center

Each year, the Women’s Cancer Resource Center holds its Swim a Mile for Women with Cancer fundraising event. In 2020, the event’s 25th year, the Women’s Cancer Resource Center team was forced to cancel the in-person elements due to COVID-19 social distancing restrictions. However, with the Swim a Mile event accounting for one-third of the Women’s Cancer Resource Center’s annual budget, the team couldn’t forgo the fundraising event entirely.

In 2020, the Swim a Mile event went virtual and was renamed Swim a Mile / Move a Mile for Women with Cancer, giving participants the option to swim, bike, or jog one mile or to choose their own adventure. To promote Swim a Mile/Move a Mile for Women with Cancer, the Women’s Cancer Resource Team used social media, email marketing, direct mail invitations, and made phone calls to supporters. The WCRC Team added new virtual components to the event, including:   

  • [GiveSmart’s] peer-to-peer fundraising pages   
  • A drive-by celebration to hand out swag   
  • A t-shirt design competition   
  • An online dance party  
  • A donor honor wall on a [GiveSmart] Event Page   
  • A competition for the best fundraiser to win a private swim lesson from an Olympic gold medalist.

The Swim a Mile/Move a Mile for Women with Cancer event proved a huge success, raising over $300,000 for the Women’s Cancer Resource Centers’ free programs and resources. The [GiveSmart] peer-to-peer fundraising pages enabled the Women’s Cancer Resource Center to leverage its supporters’ social networks to expand its reach to new donors and communities. The online dance party created community engagement and a closer connection to other supporters than a traditional Zoom event. The donor wall on [GiveSmart’s] Event Page created an exciting giving environment where supporters could view their donations in real time. Despite challenges created by the pandemic, the Women’s Cancer Resource Center retained about the same number of participants, while the average amount raised per participant increased.

The Women’s Cancer Resource Center provides extensive services to women with cancer and their loved ones in the San Francisco Bay Area. Its primary focus is helping women with cancer, but the center also offers wellness programs for everyone, regardless of gender, and including those who do not have cancer.

item.title: United Way of Tulare County (UWTC): GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: UWTC wanted to inspire more teens to apply for college and keep them updated on application deadlines, so they utilized GiveSmart’s text subscription service.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: UWTC Text Reminders to Inspire Teens to Apply for College
To reach high schoolers, UWTC needed to meet teens where they already were: on their phones.

Considering education as a vital part of building a successful future, United Way of Tulare County (UWTC) wanted to find a way to engage young donors, local high school students, and their families to get them thinking about college. UWTC needed to reach this younger audience, but getting a response from this group proved challenging.

Given that high school students are always on their phones, [GiveSmart’s] text communication option was seen as a perfect way to encourage teenagers to engage. This communication solution meant that anyone who texted the keyword G2C2017 (go2college2017) to 51555 could subscribe to updates and receive immediate reminders about FAFSA and college application deadlines. 

  • [GiveSmart’s] peer-to-peer fundraising pages   
  • A drive-by celebration to hand out swag   
  • A t-shirt design competition   
  • An online dance party  
  • A donor honor wall on a [GiveSmart] Event Page   
  • A competition for the best fundraiser to win a private swim lesson from an Olympic gold medalist.

This campaign to engage young donors significantly increased communications between UWTC and the kids they sought to help. The campaign captured over 1,250 subscribers, 747 in just one day! Also, United Way sent many messages to these kids and their parents to increase interest in college among those who hadn’t considered it.

United Way of Tulare County works to advance the education, income, and health of neighborhoods and communities to ensure every child succeeds, every step of the way, from cradle to career. This work is done by partnering with companies and volunteers in their metropolitan area.

item.title: YMCA Florida First Coast: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: First Coast Y decides to offer free admission to its annual Y-BASH, and in return, its supporters meet its bold fundraising goal of sending 20 kids to camp.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Donations Pour in for the Florida First YMCA’s Y-Bash
Florida First YMCA Utilizes Text-to-RSVP and Text-to-Donate and Raises Enough to Send 22 Kids to Camp
[GiveSmart] makes giving very exciting. We were so thrilled when donations started flooding in, and we reached our goal in three minutes.
[GiveSmart] makes giving very exciting. We were so thrilled when donations started flooding in, and we reached our goal in three minutes.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"$11,000";s:16:"data_description";s:13:"amount raised";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"22";s:16:"data_description";s:17:"kids sent to camp";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:9:"3 minutes";s:16:"data_description";s:38:"fundraising goal achieved in 3 minutes";}}

First Coast Y is a volunteer-driven organization. Each year, they host their annual Y-BASH, an event to reflect on the past year and acknowledge the special people who have continuously given back to the Y and their community. They decided to offer free admission to the previously ticketed event and, in return, hoped their supporters would help them meet their bold fundraising goal of sending 20 kids to camp.

Without needing to sell tickets, First Coast Y used text-to-RSVP and received an outstanding response of 800 attendees. During the evening's most compelling moment, when a young camper spoke about his experience, the First Coast Y CEO asked attendees to pledge to help send more kids to camp by texting CAMP to 91999 to help meet the event's fundraising goal.

Donations rolled in, the CEO called out donor names, and the live fundraising thermometer shot up. Within 3 minutes, they exceeded their goal, with 125 attendees making text-to-give donations totaling $11,000. This money went towards sending 22 kids to camp.

Records show the YMCA of Florida’s First Coast was started in 1908. Today, they have 13 membership facilities, one resident camp, one charter elementary school, 40 school-based child care sites, two facilities for people with developmental disabilities and three youth development campuses.

item.title: Cheryl Lyn Welter Charitable Foundation: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Together with GiveSmart, the Cheryl Lyn Welter Charitable Foundation created a multi-channel fundraising strategy and communications plan, complete with week-to-week activity outlines.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Cheryl Lyn Welter Foundation Surpasses Fundraising Goals
Using tools from GiveSmart, this one-person charitable foundation met and surpassed its fundraising goals.
GiveSmart has gone far beyond what I would have expected.

I can’t thank you enough for your help…and, especially for your patience! I know I’m paying you, but you’ve gone far beyond what I would have expected. Rest assured that, when we begin distributing grants to help those kids, YOU will be largely responsible for doing God’s work.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"$115";s:16:"data_description";s:13:"average gift ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:7:"$38,363";s:16:"data_description";s:13:"raised so far";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"154";s:16:"data_description";s:31:"new donors supporting the cause";}}

The founder of the Cheryl Lyn Welter Charitable Foundation, Harold, had a mission of providing $50-250 grants to teachers and others who work with students K-12 who are underprivileged after his daughter, Cheryl, died in an auto accident the night before homecoming. Cheryl loved being involved in her high school activities. Harold wanted every student struggling with finances to have access to books, sporting equipment, and field trips, ensuring a fulfilling school experience. Harold, a one-person show, runs the foundation while also maintaining a full-time job as a financial adviser. As a new foundation, it needed to reach $37,000 by year-end to start providing grants in 2018!

Not knowing how to optimize his supporters or his current communication channels, he enlisted [GiveSmart] 's support. Together, they created a multi-channel fundraising strategy and communications plan, complete with week-to-week activity outlines. The [GiveSmart] team helped him set up his peer-to-peer fundraising page, online donation forms, and a text keyword (Text Cheryl to 71777). Harold was then encouraged and guided to share these digital fundraising tools with messages and methods that would generate the most awareness and support for his cause.  

As the foundation's founder and the only part-time volunteer, there was a lot to do and very little time available. But Harold was ambitious and followed all the best practices that [GiveSmart] provided. He reached out to his network and community through social media, local media, school events, and peer-to-peer fundraising. He also posted regular videos on social media, sharing status updates about the foundation’s work.

The support began to roll in, and 194 donors collectively contributed $37,062.65 by year-end, successfully meeting his $37,000 goal to endow his foundation grants in 2018. The outcome was a direct result of focused planning combined with digital fundraising tools that empowered him to connect with donors and build support. In February 2018, Harold and the Cheryl Welter Foundation began distributing grants to teachers in Knox, Indiana. After reaching their goal and realizing even more potential, the foundation now has a “Drive for 55” campaign to raise $55,000 to enable more grants as school starts again in August.

In memory of Cheryl’s love of helping others and her love of participating in school activities, the Cheryl Lyn Welter Family Charitable Foundation has been established to help needy students in grades K-12 have similar experiences and opportunities. Proceeds from the endowment will be awarded as grants to those responsible for helping underprivileged children.

item.title: Great Rivers United Way: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Before leveraging digital channels to expand its donor reach, Great Rivers United Way relied on traditional workplace giving methods, such as payroll deductions and paper pledge forms.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Great Rivers United Way Raises $20,000+ in Workplace Donations
Great Rivers United Way raised $20,000+ in workplace donations through the platform and had 150+ digital forms completed from employees.
Very responsive and helpful with the things I didn’t know how to set up and work properly to truly run an effective campaign.

[GiveSmart], thank you! As we piloted our first workplace giving campaign using online pledge forms, you were very responsive and helpful with the things I didn’t know how to set up and work properly to truly run an effective campaign.
Before leveraging digital channels to expand its donor reach, Great Rivers United Way relied on traditional workplace giving methods, such as payroll deductions and paper pledge forms.

By leveraging [GiveSmart] solutions, and at the request of their corporate partners, Great Rivers United Way launched two pilot programs to test online pledge forms in place of the standard paper pledge forms they had been using.  

[GiveSmart’s] online workplace giving campaigns allowed them to:   

  • Alleviate all paperwork associated with using paper pledge forms  
  • Send daily campaign reports and updates to all participants  
  • Leverage meaningful content through videos hosted on the form to show the impact that donors create through their donations  
  • Collect donor information more easily  
  • Deployed an ‘impact calculator’ which showed in real-time how donations were being distributed

Using [GiveSmart’s] online workplace giving forms, Great Rivers United Way raised $20,000+ in workplace donations through the platform and had 150+ digital forms completed from employees through their corporate partnerships after launching the two pilot programs.

Great Rivers United Way is working to advance the common good and create opportunities for a better life for everyone in its six-county region: Buffalo, La Crosse, Monroe, Trempealeau, and Vernon counties in Wisconsin, and Houston County in Minnesota. Great Rivers United Way focuses on the building blocks for a good life:  

  • Education: helping children and youth achieve their potential  
  • Income: promoting financial stability and independence  
  • Health: improving people’s health  
  • Community basics: providing access to basic needs like food, shelter, and safety  

United Way’s goal is to create long-lasting changes in the Coulee Region by addressing the underlying causes of these problems. Living united means being a part of the change.

item.title: Jackson State University: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: GiveSmart provided JSU with a crowdfunding campaign solution that allowed donors to target the university department they wished to donate to and to group together in hopes of “beating” other groups by raising more funds.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Jackson State Raises $110K with Crowdfunding Competition
JSU utilized a crowdfunding competition to raise money for its students.
The support has been great! If I ever have a question, I know I can rely on [GiveSmart] to help me or answer any questions I have.
The support has been great! If I ever have a question, I know I can rely on [GiveSmart] to help me or answer any questions I have.

Jackson State University (JSU) sought to increase donations to support its student services department's fundraising efforts. They quickly realized their greatest resource was the people they served: their students. As a result, JSU decided to launch a crowdfunding campaign to leverage its students’ social giving circles and expand its reach to a larger audience. In addition, they wanted the online giving forms to include an option to donate to specific departments at the university, so donors could give to the department they cared about most.

[GiveSmart] stepped in and offered a crowdfunding campaign solution to meet their needs and help them achieve their goal. They created crowdfunding pages that allowed donors to target the university department they wished to support (e.g., the School of Arts or the School of Business). There was also a section that allowed students and alumni to group together in hopes of “beating” the other groups by raising more funds! Every donor received a message of thanks that fostered improved communication throughout the student and alumni base and encouraged future support.

Exactly 1,288 donors supported Jackson State University by donating over $110,000! This total donation amount was driven by competition among crowdfunding groups, mainly fraternities and sororities, resulting in an easy, new, and fun way to fundraise for their university.

Since 1877, Jackson State University has provided young men and women with opportunities to empower them to succeed in an increasingly complex world. As the Urban University of Mississippi, Jackson State emphasizes public service programs designed to enhance quality of life and seek solutions to urban problems in the physical, social, intellectual, and economic environments.

item.title: Good Neighbor Homeless Shelter: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Good Neighbor Homeless Shelter (GNHS) needed to raise more money to support growing organizational initiatives, so they decided to host a Dancing with the Stars fundraiser with GiveSmart.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Dancing with the Stars Fundraiser for Good Neighbor Homeless Shelter
Good Neighbor Homeless Shelter hosted a Dancing with the Stars fundraiser with GiveSmart to support growing initiatives.
[GiveSmart’s] easy-to-use fundraising solutions have increased our overall revenues and donor database by 10x!
[GiveSmart’s] easy-to-use fundraising solutions have increased our overall revenues and donor database by 10x!

Good Neighbor Homeless Shelter (GNHS) needed to raise more money to support growing organizational initiatives, so they decided to host a Dancing with the Stars fundraiser. To engage community members in voting and making real-time donations to the competing dance teams participating in this event, they contacted [GiveSmart].

With [GiveSmart], dance teams could share their unique keyword (for example, text ‘Shelley’ to 51555) and use short links via social media, text, email, television, radio, and print. Donor names and donation totals appeared live on each team’s thermometer, creating an exciting, competitive giving environment during the Good Neighbor Homeless Shelter fundraiser.

More than $124,000 was raised for the shelter by donations made to vote for dance contestants. Leading up to this event, they sent over 6,000 text messages to update the donor base on the event plans. The Good Neighbor Homeless Shelter fundraiser was a success, and they thanked every donor for their contribution. They also offered [GiveSmart’s] mobile messaging subscription to stay connected to GNHS through text updates, with the option to donate more at any time.

When homeless members of the community face the loss of shelter, a basic human need, the Good Neighbor Shelter welcomes them as guests. They understand that many circumstances lead to homelessness, and they provide a program that helps guests identify and address a broad range of factors to facilitate their return to self-sufficiency.

item.title: American Leadership Forum (ALF): GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Each year, the ALF Houston chapter hosts the Joseph Jaworski Leadership Award Celebration Dinner to honor Senior Fellows for their contributions that year. However, when COVID-19 restrictions became an obstacle for the 2020 Award Dinner, the ALF Houston team knew they’d have to find an alternative fundraising campaign.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Success For Alf Houston’s First-Ever Entirely Virtual Award Celebration
ALF empowered supporters to fundraise virtually through a peer-to-peer page for the Joseph Jaworski Leadership Award.
I was concerned our team wouldn’t have enough time to create a prerecorded, ‘live’ virtual fundraising event. But with help from [GiveSmart], we were able to hit the ground running.
I was concerned our team wouldn’t have enough time to create a prerecorded ‘live’ virtual fundraising event. But with help from [GiveSmart], we were able to hit the ground running.
Vice President, American Leadership Forum, Houston/Gulf Coast

The American Leadership Forum (ALF) is a national nonprofit organization that brings together and strengthens diverse leaders to serve the common good through classes and workshops. The Houston/Gulf Coast chapter was the first ALF, founded in 1981 by Joseph Jaworski, to address what he saw as a crisis of leadership across the U.S.  

Each year, the ALF Houston chapter hosts the Joseph Jaworski Leadership Award Celebration Dinner to honor Senior Fellows within the chapter for their contributions that year. However, when COVID-19 restrictions became an obstacle for the 2020 Award Dinner, the ALF Houston team knew they’d have to find an alternative fundraising campaign.

ALF Houston’s Vice President of Development, Kym King, reached out to a nonprofit colleague who recommended [GiveSmart] to host a virtual event to replace the in-person Award Dinner. Limited on time, Kym signed up with [GiveSmart]. By working with [the platform], Kym quickly set up an Event Page for the virtual Joseph Jaworski Leadership Award Celebration.  

The Event Page included links to donation and registration forms, allowing ALF to capture important information on supporters, such as first name, last name, email address, etc. Also included were text-to-donate instructions and links to the Award Celebration’s peer-to-peer fundraising page and sign-up form.  

The peer-to-peer fundraising page was a new element of the Award Celebration, empowering supporters to fundraise virtually. Kym worked with her staff and editor to pre-record portions of the “Live” event, including guest speaker segments and behind-the-scenes footage of award winners. After the video editor finalized the Award Celebration, Kym “live-streamed” the Award Celebration onto the Event Page at its scheduled start time.

ALF Houston’s first-ever entirely virtual Award Celebration was a success. The Event Page for the Award Celebration acted as a microsite to host the entire event. The virtual Event Page allowed ALF Houston to attract more attendees than previous in-person Award Celebrations. Typically, ALF Houston’s venue for the Award Celebration has a limit of 400 attendees. But since the event was virtual in 2020, 1,400+ virtual attendees from around the U.S. could attend without paying for a plane ticket or lodging. 

Additionally, ALF Houston raised $132,198 from just the peer-to-peer fundraising element of the Award Celebration by utilizing members’ social networks to reach new donors. The ALF Houston Team surpassed their original $300,000 goal, raising $304,711 for the virtual event. Kym plans to incorporate a virtual element into future Joseph Jaworski Leadership Award Celebrations, when members can meet in person again.

American Leadership Forum unites diverse leaders across the country to serve the common good. Its Senior Fellows from various industries, including arts, philanthropy, healthcare, politics, education, business, and more, participate in year-long programs to foster learning and trust among all sectors of society. By training their Senior Fellows in collaboration and consensus-building, ALF works to build more effective communities from its chapters across the U.S.

item.title: Ronald McDonald House of Central Ohio: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded:
Download this case study to learn more about:
How streamlining their revenue sources in one spot best supports their donor stewardship
Why hosting everything -- auction, raffles, games, and more -- online helped RMHC Central Ohio grow its revenue
What staff can accomplish when your event setup is done right

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Big Picture Thinking: Strategic Planning for Nonprofits
How GiveSmart best supports Ronald McDonald House of Central Ohio to better their donor and volunteer stewardship

It’s been huge for us in terms of revenue generation – paper bidding only captures the people at the event. Our events cap out at a certain number … everyone has a cap to what they are going to spend...How do we grow our events if we can't grow the numbers?

We've started learning who these donors are, and thanking them. It’s opened our eyes to a whole new set of donors that we didn’t really know existed prior. With GiveSmart, it is so much easier. It’s made us so much more aware of our donors and trends.
Director of Sponsorship & Events, RMHC of Central Ohio
a:1:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"117%";s:16:"data_description";s:54:"A recent golf outing achieved 117% of its revenue goal";}}

tewarding donors is a long-term strategy that, if done well, can help your nonprofit’s bottom line grow steadily and sustainably. 

The overall donor retention rate is only about 45 percent. The amount of time and effort it takes to replace those lost donors is far more than the time and effort it takes to grow and nurture the relationships you already have. 

Donor stewardship helps your staff save time and raise more! 

Ronald McDonald House Charities of Central Ohio (RHMC Central Ohio) first started using GiveSmart in the Spring of 2018, solely to assist with their auction at one event. However, as their staff started using GiveSmart more and more, they found it so easy to use that they expanded its use into other fundraisers. And the insights gained from the reporting led to happier volunteers and ultimately helped RHMC Central Ohio raise more. 

The power of tailoring your communication 

Technology has made communication easier today, so it’s essential to keep your donors’ attention. Using this donor segmentation guide will give you tips and strategies to engage donors and tailor messaging.

Let’s streamline  

From the beginning, GiveSmart streamlined RHMC Central Ohio’s auction - eliminating paper, pen, and the heavy staff or volunteer needs that come with it, especially during closeout. 

Now that they’ve used the technology for raffles, event games, and online donations, nearly all of their revenue funnels through a single channel. That keeps the data and reporting in a single system of record, making tracking much easier. And since everyone on their staff is well-versed with GiveSmart, they can all dive into the data-rich information to gain influential donor and bidder insights. 

Expanded their reach 

In fundraising, your event's net revenue must grow each year. But, depending on venue constraints, health and safety guidelines, and other limits, your organization can’t necessarily add more guests to the room to help you raise more. 

GiveSmart helped RMHC Central Ohio grow even when its guest list couldn’t. And because their staff is so comfortable with the technology, they’re empowered to try new things, while thinking outside the box, they continue to gain a more in-depth understanding of donor and bidder behavior. 

Virtual Auction  

Having a virtually hosted auction was the answer to that question for RMHC Central Ohio. At first, they weren't totally seeing the big picture. Of course, a virtually hosted auction was easier for staff, and it offered the added benefit of a real-time view of the auction’s performance from the admin dashboard. 

Growth wasn’t limited to offsite guests, as RMHC Central Ohio also saw more bids from in-room guests after adding a virtual auction component. Guests could set automatic bids and didn’t have to go back and forth to the auction tables. It meant guests were more engaged with the program and mission. 

When RMHC Central Ohio later sent its virtual auctions – from gala to golf outings - to itsentire distribution list, thousands of people gained access, whether they were inside or #OutsidetheRoom. Golfers could enjoy their game and bid throughout the day, without having to rush to the short dinner. 

The organization was able to expand its reach beyond auctions as well. 

Raffles  

One revenue-enhancing measure they added that helped them achieve this success was a high-end alcohol bottle raffle at every course. 

The odds were higher, which drove interest. And, past golfers and high-end alcohol fans from the donor database were able to get a chance, even if they missed the outing. 

Because the raffles were all hosted online, staff knew the status of revenue for each element – raffles, on-course games, hole-in-one contests – from anywhere at any time. This meant staff could text volunteers to encourage them to promote specific opportunities that generate more revenue for the organization. RMHC Central Ohio staff could congratulate each other when their course hit its revenue target. The chair of the event knew that his and his committee’s hard work had literally paid off.

Expanded Reach 

As RMHC Central Ohio expanded its auction and raffles to its entire supporter network, a trend emerged where people who were not at the event were giving and giving big! Names they weren’t familiar with kept popping up, and these generous donors were consistently winning items. 

Paper bidding and physical raffle tickets lead to incomplete, manual tracking. With GiveSmart, each event staff member had firsthand, real-time breakdowns of revenue streams and donors. This opened the door to other conversations on how these donors can get more involved, which, in the long term, benefits the RMHC Central Ohio most of all. 

Online Donations 

Stronger donor relationships resulted in increased online giving. These particular donations were not tied to an item. Instead, RMHC became more creative and used GiveSmart more. 

For one, they added mission ties to dollar amounts. For example, how much each load of laundry costs or each evening’s dinner costs for the House. This additional information inspired donations, and the mission ties were based on past supporter trends and involvement. If people gave $500 to offset laundry instead of a $500 general gift, that MEANS something. 

For another, RMHC Central Ohio staff added a competition element to some of itsevents to increase online donations. With their recent successful golf outing, they pitted course against course to see who could raise the most in general event donations. This peer-to-peer element helped bring more donors into the event, too. 

GiveSmart made the setup of these strategies, and others, easy, and staff were well-informed and able to access reporting at every turn. The setup and backend enable revenue growth. 

Created with nonprofit staff in mind 

Nonprofits are grossly understaffed. Anything that makes workflows more efficient and easier for staff and donors alike to use adds significant benefit to a nonprofit, from the bottom line to the revenue raised. 

RMHC of Central Ohio now has all its event revenue in one place thanks to GiveSmart. This means all staff have access to reporting in one place, and House administrators can export and import data as needed. 

Beyond that, the RMHC Central Ohio staff have real-time access to information. A volunteer wants to know how their golf hole is performing compared to others. (S)he/They can get an immediate answer. A committee chair wants to know how their revenue enhancer idea is performing. (S)he/They can get an immediate answer. A certain raffle isn’t getting the donations as expected. Staff can talk to those volunteers to pump them up. 

Event lead staff are empowered to set up and run their events from setup to wrap-up. There is a vast library of information, training videos, how-to guides, webinars on demand, and more that any GiveSmart administrator, staff or volunteer can access 

And volunteers can support on the day of, on the backend of the GiveSmart platform. Additional training is available to ensure everyone feels confident. With 24/7 support just a chat or call away, RHMC Central Ohio rests easy when it comes to event time. 

When they started using GiveSmart, RMHC of Central Ohio didn’t yet realize all of the benefits they would later see. From event set up to robust reporting to staff enablement, GiveSmart is the right partner to help revenue grow and better steward donors.

With 137 guest rooms, the Columbus Ronald McDonald House is one of the largest in the world. 

Every night, hundreds of moms, dads, brothers, and sisters stay in the House, only steps away from Nationwide Children’s Hospital. What they can provide at the Ronald McDonald House is a bit of stability amid challenging times. Families can make the House their home-away-from-home for as long as their child is being treated in the hospital. They serve around 4,500 families a year and frequently have waitlists due to demand. 

The mission of RMHC of Central Ohio is to create, find, and support programs that directly improve the health and well-being of children and their families.

item.title: Pace Center for Girls: A GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: How Pace Center for Girls found success in a few short months with virtual fundraising

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Creating A Bright, New Future: Fundraising Success Story
How Pace Center for Girls found success in a few short months with virtual fundraising

We needed a solution that worked for us, and we were exploring and vetting several different vendors. We landed on GiveSmart ultimately because it looked, for our team, like something we could really latch onto. And, we could use it to essentially help our locations build out their own campaigns and events. It was really user-friendly.

We’re a fully virtual story, but we’re really, really, really excited about continuing to use the platform for hybrid events and in-person events. We know the system can do it, and we’ve got the confidence that the system will be able to adapt. We’ll be able to replace all of the stuff we’ve done in the past with a system that we really have a lot of confidence in. What’s next is really exciting for us.
Pace Center for Girls’ Director of Advancement Operations

Virtual fundraising is now vital for any charity’s success. Today, accepting digital donations is the standard for nonprofits.  

But why settle for the minimum?

In June 2020, Pace Center for Girls adopted GiveSmart as their fundraising management tool in response to the need for a digital fundraising solution that could work for their multi-chapter structure, scale as they grow, and deliver more than expected. 

With 22 communities – and counting – Pace’s central office needed a solution that could work for local, central, and volunteer staff. The fundraising and events of each community vary, and the staff bandwidth is, in some cases, minimal. They were looking for a digital fundraising solution to serve their donors better and support their communities.

Then came March of 2020, and with it an expedited need to find a technology that could fully support virtual fundraising.

Many communities had depended almost exclusively on fundraising events and hed been successful with them. And while Pace had success soliciting donations to bridge the gap caused by the cancellation and postponement of fundraising events, it became clear they needed a new solution and fast. Some donors truly prefer an event-style engagement opportunity. 

Many of the options they were considering would have required multiple technologies to fundraise and stream, or to fundraise and communicate. With GiveSmart, they found that it “not only did the thing you wanted it to do”, but it was an easy-to-use solution. Fundraising, ticketing, auction, streaming, texting, emailing, and reporting can all be easily managed for each fundraising site from a single intuitive Backstage dashboard. And, with a highly customizable homepage, communication and promotion were simple and effective. 

With a fully-featured fundraising solution, what else is possible? 

 

   Pace Center for Girls  Compared to Avg. User 
Campaigns Ran*  29  +1443% 
Dollars Raised*  $200,058  +130% 
Transaction Fees Covered*  $4,376  +478% 
Donors Registered*  1,879  +74% 
Donations Received*  442  +591% 

*June '20 - June '21   

Time is funding. 

Pace Center for Girls was looking for a solution that would work best for virtual fundraisers and support their fundraising when returning to in-person events. 

They found GiveSmart to be the best, and it paid off in ways they were not initially expecting.

One place their staff found to be a huge time-saver was in the checkout process. With GiveSmart, you can run all transactions – auction payments, paddle raise pledges, raffle ticket purchases, and more – in one fell swoop. 

The ability to settle all balances with the simple click of a button eliminated the headache(s) of having to track donors down and instead allows the organization to focus on cultivating donor relations. Text message communication before, during, and after an event is another feature that helps maintain supporter engagement. And with GiveSmart, text messages can be scheduled in advance, freeing up staff time for other tasks. Pace had multiple events on the same evening, and this feature allowed for fewerstaff to be hands-on with logistics. 

Pace even hosted a few text-to-donate donation campaigns, in which they texted past donors with an ask to give again. It was so successful that they did it over and over! 

Not only were the time savings an added benefit, but the opportunities those freed-up hours gave them to cultivate donors and strategize for additional, stronger fundraisers are priceless. In an industry with traditionally short staffing and staff wearing many hats, a tool that truly saves time is invaluable. 

Benefits of virtual events 

While the temporary loss of in-person events was a challenge, and virtual events cannot authentically replace every element, there are some benefits. The cost of events can be prohibitive and ultimately not worth the investment if the return is uncertain. Virtual event costs are much, much lower when venue, sit-down dinner,  flowers, and other costs are not a factor. 

The opportunity to expand your reach is another significant benefit of events with a virtual component. Guests are not limited by geography, travel plans, babysitter schedules, or calendar conflicts. Pace saw an increase in out-of-state donors and an influx of first-time donors. With the right strategy and future offerings, those donors can continue to give to and advocate for their mission. 

At the ready 

Nonprofits have very little room to take risks. New investments are scrutinized to protect donor dollars. Having robust technology already in place enabled Pace to try new campaigns and events. 

 

GiveSmart subscribers get access to unlimited campaigns. So, if there is something your junior board wants to try, or you want to start up a mid-year text-to-donate campaign, you can. 

Fundraising is constantly evolving. Not so long ago, peer-to-peer fundraising was reserved for select events. And now? Charities are finding lots of success with voting competitions and adding peer-to-peer elements to their galas. 

With hybrid fundraising becoming the new standard and in-person events a tried-and-true revenue generator, there are steps all nonprofits should consider to stay relevant. 

It’s all about contactless ... 

Contactless interactions are here to stay. Consumers prefer them. Your guests have limited time to spend at your event. Don’t make them spend it in line! 

With GiveSmart, you can easily run a contactless check-in. From assigning bidder numbers, tables, and groups to seamlessly collecting payment information during pre-registration, your technology should evolve with the times, too. 

And with batch charging, electronic invoices, contactless checkout, and auction item pickup, checkout is easy. Dynamic reporting allows your staff and volunteers to pack up items for the winner, making item pickup or drop-off easy. 

A system that offered continuity was essential to Pace. How could they not only keep things going but also reach further? 

The additional engagement methods that GiveSmart offered met that need. The system is highly configurable, easy to use, and easy to teach. Plus, the number of resources available and the responsive support center make campaign setup and execution easy and engaging. And, the reporting from every campaign is archived, so when it comes to planning, organizations have all of the historical data they need to strategize.

Pace knew they would be returning to live events. After their experience with GiveSmart, they quickly affirmed that this technology will be something they continue to use long-term because it makes fundraising much easier for staff, volunteers, and supporters. 

The fundraising landscape will constantly be changing. The ability to manage multiple campaigns effectively and efficiently from anywhere, on any device, at any time is the wise choice to best prepare for whatever may come next.

Pace Center for Girls provides girls and young women a safe and caring environment to learn, grow and create a bright new future—our nationally recognized evidence-based model balances academics and social services for middle and high school-aged girls. 

Pace creates an environment to meet each girl where they are, to help her find her voice, and achieve her full potential. At locations across Florida and Georgia, Pace provides academic and social services during regular school hours in a safe, supportive environment tailored to girls’ needs, with an emphasis on relationships, relevant life skills, and the cultivation of girls’ strengths. 

A low team member-to-girl ratio allows for attention and an awareness of each girl’s individual history. Recognizing that each girl's experience has impacted their family and peer relationships, Pace team members strive to engage each girl's larger community in their care.

item.title: Honduras Outreach, Inc.: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Read this case study to learn why having GiveSmart best supported HOI in diversifying and modernizing their fundraising.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Strengthening your mission through modern fundraising
How having robust, varied solutions supported HOI’s success now and growth in the future

The integrations and reporting give me more time to not be in the data and to build the relationships with the people I need to be building relationships with. Without the relationships, we aren’t going to have any donations to enter.

The integrations and reporting give me more time to not be in the data and to build the relationships with the people I need to be building relationships with... Without the relationships, we aren’t going to have any donations to enter.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:3:"72%";s:16:"data_description";s:80:"Hope 51’s enrollment got to 72% of the goal just two months into the program! ";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:15:"< 30 minutes";s:16:"data_description";s:123:"The time from the volunteer’s request to the fundraising page's setup and the first dollar raised was under 30 minutes! ";}}

Honduras Outreach, Inc. (HOI) was founded in 1987 to improve the quality of life of the people in Honduras. They work with volunteers from all over the world. As they serve those Honduran communities, they have found a real need to stabilize and modernize their fundraising to better position themselves for revenue growth.

Then came 2020, and in blew two devastating hurricanes to Honduras, under the backdrop of a global pandemic.

These challenges on the ground and at home made the need to modernize even more urgent. They had many people willing to give, and HOI needed the tools to better support and steward them.

Why GiveSmart?

With new leadership and other technology updates, HOI sought to expand its digital fundraising beyond events. They had big future goals and soon-to-be-launched campaigns that they needed to stand up.

HOI had already been using GiveSmart Events and was vetting other online fundraising solutions to best support general online donations, advance yet-to-be-launched campaigns, and reach new donors. They had thought about only keeping one technology, but what would be possible if they chose both?

When looking at GiveSmart Fundraise or GiveSmart Events, each platform provided unique solutions to the challenges they were facing. Choosing one over the other would have hampered some of their plans, but combining both offered an opportunity to diversify and modernize their fundraising.

As GiveSmart Events and GiveSmart Fundraise are connected solutions, there was an opportunity and incentive to choose both. In so doing, HOI can elevate its fundraising to best support its mission.

Auction

HOI has been using the GiveSmart Events platform for its signature gala, ticketing, and auction for several years. They felt its functionality was unmatched, and the ease of use saved staff time and headaches.

Then came the need to pivot to a virtual event, which few people had any meaningful experience doing.

GiveSmart was a turnkey solution for them, and their virtual event was a great success. The ease of setting up and executing a virtual, standout auction also helped them reach new donors.

As they moved to a hybrid event in the fall of 2021, GiveSmart was the only choice they wanted to support that event. GiveSmart supported uncharted virtual territory, and it will be the tool they need to make their hybrid event great.

Event management and ticketing

Aside from the ease of use with the auction capabilities, HOI knows that GiveSmart’s ticketing features will best support their upcoming, hybrid fall event. Using a QR code on printed, exclusive invites, offering a variety of ticketing packages, and capturing all data are integral to HOI’s success.

Being able to still stream portions of the event in the virtual world, such as text-to-give, allows HOI to expand its reach and engage supporters who are in and out of the room.

And, with contactless check-in and checkout, GiveSmart Events will support a safe, efficient guest experience.

Integrations and reporting

Just before choosing GiveSmart Fundraise, HOI had migrated over to a new customer relationship management system (CRM). It was imperative that whichever fundraising technology they chose integrated with their CRM.

With built-in compatibility with several of the largest CRMs and an open API that makes data sharing easy, GiveSmart Fundraise became the obvious choice for their online fundraising and recurring giving campaigns. The integration allows HOI’s staff to get a whole picture of every supporter and volunteer, in a way they couldn’t before.

And robust, real-time reporting as part of both GiveSmart Events and Fundraise means their staff can gain powerful insights to inform future strategy and planning more effectively.

Online donations and recurring giving

HOI needed a solution with customizable, embeddable online donation forms, and GiveSmart Fundraise was the best choice for them.

All donations flow through the same place–whether they land on a dedicated donation campaign homepage or HOI’s website. This improves the donor and staff experience.

HOI also had BIG plans to launch a monthly giving program, Hope 51, in 2021. They have many devoted volunteers who travel to Honduras once a year, but HOI offers limited opportunities to engage with the story the other 51 weeks.

They needed a solution that fully supported this initiative. The embeddable, fully customizable form, and the ability for donors to cover the credit card processing fees, have helped get Hope 51’s enrollment to 72% of the goal just two months into the program!

Peer-to-peer

When Honduras suffered two devastating hurricanes back-to-back in late 2020, long-time volunteers who had traveled to Honduras previously wanted to help.

These volunteers requested a fundraising page, and the HOI team quickly set one up with GiveSmart. From the volunteer’s request to the first dollar raised was under 30 minutes!

With GiveSmart's subscription, nonprofits have unlimited access to fundraising campaigns, and the tools were there when HOI needed them.

And, HOI has been doing peer-to-peer fundraising offline for a long time. It is a cornerstone of their volunteer involvement and trips. Giving people the opportunity to fundraise online instead and have the funds directly deposited into HOI is a huge time-saver and benefit for everyone.

As HOI’s future planning takes shape, GiveSmart Fundraise’s peer-to-peer fundraising capabilities allow their campaigns to grow even further!

Having both GiveSmart Events and Fundraise supports HOI’s fundraising today, gives them confidence in plans, and opens the door to greater possibilities. Their mission depends on each step.

For nearly three decades, HOI has been following God’s call to strengthen communities in Honduras and Nicaragua through long-term partnerships. Every day, they continue to see God’s hand at work in the transformation of lives and families through the ongoing commitment between North and Central Americans.

HOI is a Christ-centered missions organization working alongside people of developing countries who desire to implement sustainable development partnerships. They organize mission trips to Honduras and Nicaragua.

Their vision is to create life-changing relationships between people in developing countries and North Americans, while promoting community-directed, integrated spiritual, physical, educational, and economic development for men, women, and children in the developing world through the promotion of dignity, cooperation, and self-sufficiency.

Thirty years later, HOI has grown in size and has extended its reach. Their programs have grown to include healthcare, agriculture, nutrition, education, faith-building, commerce, and construction as part of an integrative approach to community development.

Over the years, they have established several strategic partnerships with like-minded people and organizations, which have helped them promote their mission and achieve their goals. With their partners' valued assistance and the continued support of those who have traveled with them, HOI can foster an environment for sustainable change in the communities that they serve.

item.title: The Field Museum : GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: The Field Museum needed an online giving solution that supported multi-platform donations and multiple online forms. Their team found that, and more, with GiveSmart.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The Field Museum Increased Recurring Donors with GiveSmart
The Field Museum grew recurring donors and boosted average gift size with GiveSmart fundraising software.

The Field Museum needed to raise funds to support its ongoing initiatives, funds, and programs. They needed an online giving solution that was flexible enough to support multi-platform donations and to use multiple online forms. Additionally, they wanted to determine a way to thank their donors instantaneously for their donations.

Through [GiveSmart], The Field Museum embedded a secure, mobile-friendly donation form on its website with custom fields, including “in honor of,” membership details, and automatic email tax receipts with dynamic personalization fields. They also created multiple online forms for use across programs and membership opportunities, each customized with the corresponding program or membership branding, including colors, images, description text, custom suggested gift amounts, and recurring gift options to help boost giving. After a donor had made a donation, they landed on a thank-you page where The Field Museum embedded a video explaining its mission and showing visitors at the museum.

This new approach reminded donors of their favorite museums and encouraged new donors to support The Field Museum. Within the first year of implementation, they raised $1,073,000 in donations from 5,452 donors. They also had procured 57 new recurring donors and had increased the average gift size to $330. This new support, both financially and otherwise, helped improve the museum, bringing even more culture to the Chicago community.

The Field Museum, located in Chicago, is one of the world’s greatest natural history museums. Founded in 1893, The Field Museum fuels a journey of discovery across time to enable solutions for a brighter future rich in nature and culture, as highlighted by their mission of “Inspiring Curiosity.”

item.title: American Roentgen Ray Society: Momentive Career Center Software
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Explore how the American Roentgen Ray Society (ARRS) boosted engagement, job visibility, & member value through a strategic career center solution.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: American Roentgen Ray Society (ARRS) | Career Center Success
Explore how the American Roentgen Ray Society (ARRS) boosted engagement, job visibility, & member value through a strategic career center solution.
Eight weeks in, we've got $25,000 in receivables—and you're handling them, which is huge for us.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:20:"box-bxs-user-account";s:13:"feature_title";s:58:"$25,000+ generated in accounts receivable in first 8 weeks";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:20:"box-bxs-calendar-alt";s:13:"feature_title";s:55:" 145 job alert registrations signed up in first 8 weeks";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:12:"feature_icon";s:13:"box-bx-import";s:13:"feature_title";s:29:"45-day implementation period ";}}

The American Roentgen Ray Society (ARRS) is one of the oldest and most respected international professional associations in the medical imaging field. Founded over a century ago, ARRS serves radiologists and related medical professionals worldwide, advancing the discipline through education, research, and community. With a team of 39 staff members, the organization punches well above its weight, delivering world-class resources to a global membership.

For a professional association of ARRS's stature, the organization was seeking career center software that was more than a job board—it needed to reflect ARRS’s brand and a critical source of non-dues revenue. ARRS's legacy career center software generated $250,000 annually, but over time, that success eroded, and the platform became passive and ineffective. With revenue declining, payments being delayed, and an outdated career center software design, the ARRS team decided it needed a new career center solution. 

ARRS selected Momentive + YM Careers after a refreshingly different sales experience. Rather than pushing a standard pitch, the Momentive team took a consultative, relationship-driven approach—listening to ARRS's specific needs and offering transparent answers without pressure. Momentive + YM Careers worked as a partner with ARRS to address its core pain points with a fully branded career center design, flexible one-year contract terms, hands-on receivables management, integrated job alert functionality, and dedicated staff support—everything needed to restore revenue, reduce administrative burden, and re-engage the radiology community.

The mission of the American Roentgen Ray Society (ARRS) is to improve health through a community committed to advancing knowledge and skills in radiology. North America’s first radiological society, ARRS, remains dedicated to the advancement of medicine through the profession of medical imaging and its allied sciences.

item.title: Provident Behavioral Health: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: See how Provident Behavioral Health boosted donors, fundraising, & event success using GiveSmart fundraising software.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Provident Behavioral Health GiveSmart Success Story
See how Provident Behavioral Health boosted donors, fundraising, & event success using GiveSmart fundraising software.
GiveSmart is really intuitive. I taught a brand-new employee how to add auction items in about 10 minutes. It's seamless, and the reporting side is really easy.
GiveSmart is really intuitive. I taught a brand-new employee how to add auction items in about 10 minutes. It's seamless, and the reporting side is really easy.

Provident STL has been delivering mental and behavioral health services to its community since 1860. When they launched their annual gala over a decade ago, managing the event meant paper bid sheets, handwritten numbers, and hours of manual reconciliation at the end of the night. Looking for something better, they connected with GiveSmart and made the switch after that first year, and stayed with GiveSmart ever since.

As Provident's gala grew, eventually incorporating donors and attendees from two partner organizations, so did the complexity of running it. The team needed a platform that could scale alongside the event without straining a small advancement staff. They also were looking for something intuitive enough for new team members to learn quickly, without extensive training or a dedicated technical expert. 

GiveSmart delivered on both counts. A trusted GiveSmart on-site partner has returned year after year to staff the event on-site, giving the Provident team a reliable partner they can count on. The platform's ease of use meant that when a brand-new fundraising employee joined the team, she could confidently add auction items after a short walkthrough.

Since making the switch to GiveSmart, Provident’s gala has grown from roughly 340 attendees and $265,000 raised in their first year to about 500 guests and over $691,000 raised at their most recent event. With GiveSmart, they held silent and live auctions, a paddle raise, wine pull, and raffle, giving the team real-time visibility and a checkout process that practically runs itself. 

Donor comfort with mobile bidding has grown steadily over the years, to the point where the team no longer needs to think about it the night of the event. Provident's staff can now focus on what matters most: stewarding donors and building the relationships that keep the gala growing year after year.

Provident Behavioral Health has been a cornerstone of mental and behavioral health care in the St. Louis region since 1860, growing from its roots into a full-service organization serving individuals and families across four locations. Provident is committed to building brighter futures through exceptional behavioral health services, offering counseling, psychiatric care, crisis intervention, and caregiver support to foster mentally healthy communities for all.

item.title: True North to Freedom: GiveSmart Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: See how True North to Freedom partnered with GiveSmart to modernize fundraising operations, triple revenue, and build a sustainable foundation for greater impact.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: See how True North to Freedom partnered with GiveSmart to modernize fundraising operations, triple revenue, and build a sustainable foundation for greater impact.
Somebody obviously designed the platform, who had actually done it before. It's just put together end to end with a little bit more care and appreciation for the user and data.
Somebody obviously designed the platform, who had actually done it before. It's just put together end to end with a little bit more care and appreciation for the user and data.

a:3:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:2:"3x";s:16:"data_description";s:37:"operating revenue growth in two years";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:6:"2 days";s:16:"data_description";s:52:"to payout, versus weeks with their previous platform";}s:6:"item-2";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:4:"100s";s:16:"data_description";s:48:"of hours saved on manual tax reporting and admin";}}

True North to Freedom is a Christian-based nonprofit serving survivors of human trafficking, primarily women and mothers, by providing the skills, services, and support needed to rebuild stable, independent lives.  With fewer than 1,000 beds available nationwide for survivors of human trafficking, the organization fills a desperate gap in care. Their work addresses the total spectrum of recovery, including trauma, addiction, and legal and financial barriers. 

With no dedicated development staff and a board focused on delivering services, the organization needed a fundraising technology partner to expand its capacity. This gap set the stage for their search for the right solution.

Previously, True North to Freedom had relied on limited tools and bandwidth to fundraise. Their first year using fundraising technology revealed shortcomings in the platform: cumbersome reporting, manual tax receipt processing, and month-long payout delays that undermined cash flow stability. These constraints highlighted the need for a better fundraising solution.  

At that point, volunteer fundraiser Scott Goemmel stepped in to evaluate new platforms, combining technological savvy with a clear vision for both present and future needs. After assessing several options, GiveSmart emerged as the frontrunner with its user-friendly interface, enterprise-grade CRM, and structured onboarding. This choice directly addressed the organization’s prior challenges.  

GiveSmart's training gave the team the confidence to hit the ground running. Streamlined event management, automated receipts, and dramatically faster payouts replaced hundreds of hours of manual reconciliation work. And when questions came up, even late at night, responsive support was there.

In two years, True North to Freedom grew its operating revenue from $40–50K to roughly $150K, a transformation driven by a broader fundraising strategy, better tools, and a growing donor base.  

Their first GiveSmart-powered gala ran smoothly, with table management, ticketing, and real-time visibility giving the small volunteer team a level of control and clarity they hadn't had before. Payouts arrived within days rather than weeks. Tax reporting, previously an exhausting, manual process, became manageable through automated receipts and improved back-end reporting.  

After the success of their initial efforts, True North to Freedom launched its first peer-to-peer fundraising campaign through GiveSmart, generating revenue without expanding event capacity or stretching an already lean team.  

GiveSmart provides a foundation for what comes next for True North to Freedom. With unlimited events, easy event cloning, and a CRM built to scale with the organization, True North to Freedom is hoping to grow and add on with GiveSmart’s infrastructure to better support survivors.

True North to Freedom's mission is to create awareness of human trafficking and walk survivors toward true freedom, rescuing the innocent, restoring hope, and renewing life. Founded in 2015 out of a prayer group in Metro Detroit, the organization believes every survivor is beautiful, valuable, and worthy of a life fully lived. From case management and emotional support to job placement, housing assistance, and spiritual guidance, True North to Freedom meets survivors where they are and walks alongside them as they rebuild.

item.title: Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE): Momentive Event Management Success Story
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Attendees and exhibitors liked the app’s clean look and easy navigation.
Attendees and exhibitors liked the app’s clean look and easy navigation.

a:2:{s:6:"item-0";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:153:"Planning for the types of content going into the app helped structure an easy-to-use interface and enabled top-performing content to rise to the surface.";}s:6:"item-1";a:2:{s:10:"data_title";s:0:"";s:16:"data_description";s:154:"Having worked on a wide range of events, Momentive Event Management was able to help direct and share ideas on how best to utilize different app features.";}}

The Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) had a global audience in town for the annual WCX™18: SAE World Congress Experience. This event attracts talent from across the mobility industry and features a comprehensive educational component, complemented by a high-impact and impressive expo. Attendees had the opportunity to experience a show floor filled with cars of the future and the technology that powers the next wave of mobility.

With such a tech-savvy audience, it was important to ensure the event’s technology was intuitive and enhanced the attendees’ experience. With an audience that includes mobility engineers, you'd better get your event app right!

It was essential to plan for the types of content that would be a good fit for the app. This helped structure an easy-to-use interface and enabled top-performing content to rise to the surface, which is particularly helpful when categorizing and condensing dashboard icons or content collections.

Breaking out their many content areas in the app was a significant advantage. There is content in the classrooms and several on-the-floor content areas, so attendees needed to have the opportunity to easily find and identify programs, times, and speakers in those areas. Utilizing the app with Momentive’s platform further enhanced performance. Having worked on a wide range of events, Momentive Event Management was able to help direct and share ideas on how best to utilize different app features.

The Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) is the leader in connecting and educating mobility professionals to enable safe, clean, and accessible mobility solutions. SAE International is a global association of more than 128,000 engineers and related technical experts in the aerospace, automotive and commercial-vehicle industries. Our core competencies are lifelong learning and voluntary consensus standards development.
